7 May

Claude responded: Looking at your code and the API response, here's what needs to happen:

Looking at your code and the API response, here's what needs to happen:

  1. Accept bid → get clientSecret + paymentIntentId
  2. Open Stripe payment sheet in-app
  3. On success → refetch task (status updates automatically via backend)

Let me walk you through the full implementation.

Step 1: Install Stripe

bash
npx expo install @stripe/stripe-react-native

Add to app.json plugins:

json
{
  "plugins": [
    ["@stripe/stripe-react-native", {
      "merchantIdentifier": "merchant.com.yourapp",
      "enableGooglePay": true
    }]
  ]
}

Step 2: Wrap your app with StripeProvider

In your root _layout.tsx:

tsx
import { StripeProvider } from '@stripe/stripe-react-native';

export default function RootLayout() {
  return (
    <StripeProvider publishableKey="pk_test_YOUR_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY">
      {/* your existing layout */}
    </StripeProvider>
  );
}

Step 3: Update handleAcceptBid in TaskDetailsPage

Replace your current handleAcceptBid with this:

tsx
import { useStripe } from '@stripe/stripe-react-native';

// Inside TaskDetailsPage component:
const { initPaymentSheet, presentPaymentSheet } = useStripe();
const [isPaymentLoading, setIsPaymentLoading] = useState(false);

const handleAcceptBid = async (bidId: string) => {
  setIsPaymentLoading(true);
  try {
    // 1. Accept bid and get payment credentials
    const response = await acceptBid({ bidId }).unwrap();

    if (!response.success) return;

    const { clientSecret, stripeCustomerId, paymentIntentId } = response.data;

    // 2. Initialize the Payment Sheet
    // The backend developer confirmed saved payment methods come automatically
    // via stripeCustomerId — Stripe handles this natively
    const { error: initError } = await initPaymentSheet({
      merchantDisplayName: 'Your App Name',
      paymentIntentClientSecret: clientSecret,
      customerId: stripeCustomerId,       // ← this enables saved cards
      // customerEphemeralKeySecret: ..., // ask backend to also return ephemeralKey
      defaultBillingDetails: {
        // pre-fill if you have user data
      },
      returnURL: 'yourapp://payment-return', // needed for 3D Secure / bank redirects
      allowsDelayedPaymentMethods: false,
      googlePay: {
        merchantCountryCode: 'US',
        testEnv: true, // set false in production
      },
      applePay: {
        merchantCountryCode: 'US',
      },
    });

    if (initError) {
      Toast.show({
        type: 'error',
        text1: 'Payment Setup Failed',
        text2: initError.message,
      });
      return;
    }

    // 3. Present the Payment Sheet to user
    const { error: paymentError } = await presentPaymentSheet();

    if (paymentError) {
      if (paymentError.code === 'Canceled') {
        // User dismissed — don't show error
        Toast.show({ type: 'info', text1: 'Payment cancelled' });
      } else {
        Toast.show({
          type: 'error',
          text1: 'Payment Failed',
          text2: paymentError.message,
        });
      }
      return;
    }

    // 4. Payment successful — backend updates task status automatically
    Toast.show({
      type: 'success',
      text1: '🎉 Payment Successful!',
      text2: 'Provider has been assigned to your task.',
      visibilityTime: 4000,
    });

    await refetchTask();
    await refetchBids();

  } catch (error: any) {
    Toast.show({
      type: 'error',
      text1: 'Something went wrong',
      text2: error.data?.message || 'Please try again',
    });
  } finally {
    setIsPaymentLoading(false);
  }
};

Step 4: Pass loading state to BidConfirmationModal

Update where you pass isAccepting:

tsx
isAccepting={isAccepting || isPaymentLoading}

One thing to ask your backend developer

For saved payment methods (cards pre-filled) to work fully, the /accept-bid response should also return a customerEphemeralKey. Ask them to add it:

json
{
  "data": {
    "clientSecret": "...",
    "stripeCustomerId": "...",
    "customerEphemeralKey": "ek_test_...",  // ← request this
    "paymentIntentId": "...",
    "taskId": "..."
  }
}

Then add to initPaymentSheet:

tsx
customerEphemeralKeySecret: ephemeralKey,

Without the ephemeral key, the payment sheet still works — it just won't show previously saved cards. The flow itself is fully functional with what you have now.

Flow Summary

Accept Bid button pressed
    → BidConfirmationModal ("Yes, Accept")
        → handleAcceptBid()
            → POST /bid/accept-bid/:id  →  gets clientSecret
                → initPaymentSheet()
                    → presentPaymentSheet()  ← Stripe native UI opens
                        → User pays
                            → Toast success
                                → refetchTask() + refetchBids()

The returnURL scheme (yourapp://) needs to be registered in your app.json under scheme. Check your existing scheme or add "scheme": "yourapp".

{
  "expo": {
    "name": "snowOut",
    "slug": "snowOut_v2",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "scheme": [
      "snowout-v2"
    ],
    "platforms": [
      "ios",
      "android"
    ],
    "icon": "./assets/icon.png",
    "userInterfaceStyle": "light",
    "orientation": "portrait",
    "splash": {
      "image": "./assets/splash/splash.png",
      "resizeMode": "contain",
      "backgroundColor": "#ffffff"
    },
    "assetBundlePatterns": [
      "**/*"
    ],
    "ios": {
      "supportsTablet": true,
      "bundleIdentifier": "com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2"
    },
    "android": {
      "package": "com.sheikhrussell123.snowout_v2",
      "adaptiveIcon": {
        "foregroundImage": "./assets/adaptive-icon.png",
        "backgroundColor": "#ffffff"
      },
      "permissions": [
        "android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE",
        "android.permission.CAMERA",
        "android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION",
        "android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION"
      ]
    },
    "plugins": [
      "expo-router",
      "expo-font",
      "@react-native-community/datetimepicker",
      [
        "expo-image-picker",
        {
          "photosPermission": "Allow SnowOut to access your photos to upload",
          "cameraPermission": "Allow SnowOut to access your camera to take photos."
        }
      ],
      "expo-secure-store",
      "@stripe/stripe-react-native"
    ],
    "experiments": {
      "typedRoutes": true,
      "tsconfigPaths": true
    },
    "web": {
      "bundler": "metro",
      "output": "static",
      "favicon": "./assets/favicon.png"
    },
    "extra": {
      "router": {},
      "eas": {
        "projectId": "00a3c383-ef40-42c3-b215-a2244d8ffe96"
      }
    }
  }
}

what should be the updated version of it accordingly
7 May

Claude responded: Your app.

Your app.json already has @stripe/stripe-react-native in plugins and a scheme defined. Just add the merchantIdentifier for Apple Pay and a returnURL scheme. Here's the updated version:

json
{
  "expo": {
    "name": "snowOut",
    "slug": "snowOut_v2",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "scheme": [
      "snowout-v2"
    ],
    "platforms": [
      "ios",
      "android"
    ],
    "icon": "./assets/icon.png",
    "userInterfaceStyle": "light",
    "orientation": "portrait",
    "splash": {
      "image": "./assets/splash/splash.png",
      "resizeMode": "contain",
      "backgroundColor": "#ffffff"
    },
    "assetBundlePatterns": [
      "**/*"
    ],
    "ios": {
      "supportsTablet": true,
      "bundleIdentifier": "com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2"
    },
    "android": {
      "package": "com.sheikhrussell123.snowout_v2",
      "adaptiveIcon": {
        "foregroundImage": "./assets/adaptive-icon.png",
        "backgroundColor": "#ffffff"
      },
      "permissions": [
        "android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE",
        "android.permission.CAMERA",
        "android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION",
        "android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION"
      ]
    },
    "plugins": [
      "expo-router",
      "expo-font",
      "@react-native-community/datetimepicker",
      [
        "expo-image-picker",
        {
          "photosPermission": "Allow SnowOut to access your photos to upload",
          "cameraPermission": "Allow SnowOut to access your camera to take photos."
        }
      ],
      "expo-secure-store",
      [
        "@stripe/stripe-react-native",
        {
          "merchantIdentifier": "merchant.com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2",
          "enableGooglePay": true
        }
      ]
    ],
    "experiments": {
      "typedRoutes": true,
      "tsconfigPaths": true
    },
    "web": {
      "bundler": "metro",
      "output": "static",
      "favicon": "./assets/favicon.png"
    },
    "extra": {
      "router": {},
      "eas": {
        "projectId": "00a3c383-ef40-42c3-b215-a2244d8ffe96"
      }
    }
  }
}

The only change is the Stripe plugin entry — from a plain string to an array with config:

json
// Before
"@stripe/stripe-react-native"

// After
[
  "@stripe/stripe-react-native",
  {
    "merchantIdentifier": "merchant.com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2",
    "enableGooglePay": true
  }
]

And your returnURL in the code from my previous response should use your existing scheme:

tsx
returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',

Note on merchantIdentifier: This is only used for Apple Pay on iOS. The value merchant.com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2 mirrors your bundle ID — but it needs to be registered in your Apple Developer account under Identifiers → Merchant IDs for Apple Pay to actually work. If you're not setting up Apple Pay right now, the value doesn't matter in development, just keep the format consistent.

import { StripeProvider } from '@stripe/stripe-react-native'; export default function RootLayout() { return ( <StripeProvider publishableKey="pk_test_YOUR_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY"> {/* your existing layout */} </StripeProvider> ); }

provide the root layout accordingly

7 May

Claude responded: Here's your updated _layout.

Here's your updated _layout.tsx with StripeProvider wrapped in the right place:

tsx
import '../global.css';
import { SafeAreaProvider } from 'react-native-safe-area-context';
import { GestureHandlerRootView } from 'react-native-gesture-handler';
import { Stack } from 'expo-router';
import Toast from 'react-native-toast-message';
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
import { ActivityIndicator, KeyboardAvoidingView, Platform, View } from 'react-native';
import { useFonts } from 'expo-font';
import * as SplashScreen from 'expo-splash-screen';
import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { Provider } from 'react-redux';
import { StatusBar } from 'expo-status-bar';
import { StripeProvider } from '@stripe/stripe-react-native';
import { store } from '@/store';

SplashScreen.preventAutoHideAsync();

const STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY = 'pk_test_YOUR_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY'; // 🔑 Replace this

function AppNavigator() {
  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);

  useEffect(() => {
    const bootstrap = async () => {
      try {
        const token = await AsyncStorage.getItem('auth_token');
        console.log('🔐 Token exists:', !!token);
      } catch (error) {
        console.log('Error checking auth:', error);
      } finally {
        setIsLoading(false);
        await SplashScreen.hideAsync();
      }
    };

    bootstrap();
  }, []);

  if (isLoading) {
    return (
      <View className="flex-1 items-center justify-center bg-white">
        <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2B7FFF" />
      </View>
    );
  }

  return (
    <>
      <StatusBar style="dark" backgroundColor="#FFFFFF" translucent={false} />
      <Stack screenOptions={{ headerShown: false }} />
    </>
  );
}

export default function Layout() {
  const [fontsLoaded] = useFonts({
    'Nunito-Regular': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-Regular.ttf'),
    'Nunito-Italic': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-Italic.ttf'),
    'Nunito-Medium': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-Medium.ttf'),
    'Nunito-MediumItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-MediumItalic.ttf'),
    'Nunito-SemiBold': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-SemiBold.ttf'),
    'Nunito-SemiBoldItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-SemiBoldItalic.ttf'),
    'Nunito-Bold': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-Bold.ttf'),
    'Nunito-BoldItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-BoldItalic.ttf'),
    'Nunito-ExtraBold': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-ExtraBold.ttf'),
    'Nunito-ExtraBoldItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Nunito-ExtraBoldItalic.ttf'),
    'Syne-Regular': require('@/assets/fonts/Syne-Regular.ttf'),
    'Syne-Medium': require('@/assets/fonts/Syne-Medium.ttf'),
    'Syne-SemiBold': require('@/assets/fonts/Syne-SemiBold.ttf'),
    'Syne-Bold': require('@/assets/fonts/Syne-Bold.ttf'),
    'Syne-ExtraBold': require('@/assets/fonts/Syne-ExtraBold.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-Regular': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-Regular.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-Italic': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-Italic.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-Medium': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-Medium.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-MediumItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-MediumItalic.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-SemiBold': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-SemiBold.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-SemiBoldItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-SemiBoldItalic.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-Bold': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-Bold.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-BoldItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-BoldItalic.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-ExtraBold': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-ExtraBold.ttf'),
    'Montserrat-ExtraBoldItalic': require('@/assets/fonts/Montserrat-ExtraBoldItalic.ttf'),
  });

  if (!fontsLoaded) {
    return (
      <View className="flex-1 items-center justify-center">
        <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2C80EC" />
      </View>
    );
  }

  return (
    <GestureHandlerRootView style={{ flex: 1 }}>
      <Provider store={store}>
        <StripeProvider
          publishableKey={STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY}
          merchantIdentifier="merchant.com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2"
          urlScheme="snowout-v2">
          <SafeAreaProvider>
            <KeyboardAvoidingView
              style={{ flex: 1 }}
              behavior={Platform.OS === 'ios' ? 'padding' : undefined}>
              <AppNavigator />
              <Toast />
            </KeyboardAvoidingView>
          </SafeAreaProvider>
        </StripeProvider>
      </Provider>
    </GestureHandlerRootView>
  );
}

Three things to note:

urlScheme — set to "snowout-v2" matching your app.json scheme. This is required for 3D Secure redirects to return to your app correctly.

merchantIdentifier — matches what you set in app.json. Only active on iOS for Apple Pay.

Placement — StripeProvider sits inside Provider (Redux) but outside SafeAreaProvider. This is intentional: Redux should be outermost so Stripe can theoretically access store context if needed, and Stripe needs to wrap everything that uses useStripe() hooks.

Then in your handleAcceptBid, use this returnURL:

tsx
returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',
EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://10.10.20.9:9000/api/v1
EXPO_PUBLIC_WS_URL=ws://localhost:3000
EXPO_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY=pk_test_xxx
EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY=sdfsdfsdfsdf
EXPO_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY=SLDKFWOIJWERWFsdf

need to use the stripe publishabe key right below root layout>
7 May

Claude responded: You're currently hardcoding the string 'EXPOPUBLICSTRIPEPUBLISHABLEKEY' instead of actually reading the env variable.

You're currently hardcoding the string 'EXPO_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY' instead of actually reading the env variable. Fix it like this:

tsx
const STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY = process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!;

That's the only change needed. Your .env already has EXPO_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY defined, and Expo automatically exposes any variable prefixed with EXPO_PUBLIC_ via process.env. The ! is a TypeScript non-null assertion since you know it'll always be set.

You said: showing this issue after chagnes .

showing this issue after chagnes . do I need to rebuild the app for making this work?

7 May

Claude responded: Yes, you must rebuild the app.

Yes, you must rebuild the app. This is the core issue.

@stripe/stripe-react-native is a native module — it contains native iOS/Android code that cannot be loaded via Expo Go or a regular JS reload. It must be compiled into the binary.

What each error means

Error 1: 'StripeSdk' could not be found The native Stripe binary code isn't in your current build. This happens when you're running on Expo Go or an old development build that was built before Stripe was installed.

Error 2: could not find react-redux context value This is a cascading error caused by Error 1 crashing the app before <Provider> could mount. Fix Error 1 and this goes away automatically.

Fix: Create a new development build

bash
# If using EAS (recommended)
npx eas build --profile development --platform android
# or for iOS
npx eas build --profile development --platform ios

Or locally without EAS:

bash
# Android
npx expo run:android

# iOS
npx expo run:ios

Important notes

  • Expo Go will never work with Stripe — Expo Go is a pre-built app that doesn't include Stripe's native code
  • After the build installs on your device/emulator, start the dev server normally with npx expo start --dev-client
  • You only need to rebuild when adding/removing native modules — JS changes still hot-reload as usual

If you're using EAS and don't have an eas.json yet:

bash
npx eas build:configure

Then build with the development profile.

You said: And your returnURL in the code from my previous response should use your existing scheme:

And your returnURL in the code from my previous response should use your existing scheme: tsx

returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',

Note on merchantIdentifier: This is only used for Apple Pay on iOS. The value merchant.com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2 mirrors your bundle ID — but it needs to be registered in your Apple Developer account under Identifiers → Merchant IDs for Apple Pay to actually work. If you're not setting up Apple Pay right now, the value doesn't matter in development, just keep the format consistent.

didn't get it . make it more clear

7 May

Claude responded: Sure.

Sure. Let me break it down simply.

returnURL

When a payment requires extra verification (like 3D Secure — your bank sends you an OTP or opens a webpage to confirm), Stripe opens a browser outside your app. After the user confirms, Stripe needs to send them back to your app. The returnURL is how it knows where to send them back.

tsx
returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',
//          ^^^^^^^^^^^  ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
//          your app     just a path, can be anything
//          scheme

snowout-v2 is already defined in your app.json as the scheme:

json
"scheme": ["snowout-v2"]

So it's already set up. You don't need to do anything extra. Just paste that string in initPaymentSheet.

merchantIdentifier

This is only for Apple Pay (the "Pay" button with Face ID on iPhone). It has nothing to do with regular card payments.

Right now you have two options:

Option A — You don't care about Apple Pay right now: Just leave the value as is. It won't break anything. Card payments work fine without it.

Option B — You want Apple Pay to work: You need to go to developer.apple.com, create a Merchant ID there, and register it. This is a separate Apple setup process — not something you do in code.

Bottom line for you right now

Just focus on this — paste these two lines in your initPaymentSheet call and you're done:

tsx
returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',
merchantIdentifier: 'merchant.com.sheikhrussell123.snowoutv2', // ignore for now, only matters for Apple Pay

Card payments will work perfectly without touching anything Apple Pay related.

const { error: initError } = await initPaymentSheet({
        merchantDisplayName: 'SnowOut',
        paymentIntentClientSecret: clientSecret,
        customerId: stripeCustomerId, // ← this enables saved cards
        // customerEphemeralKeySecret: ..., // ask backend to also return ephemeralKey
        defaultBillingDetails: {
          // pre-fill if you have user data
        },
        returnURL: 'yourapp://payment-return', // needed for 3D Secure / bank redirects
        allowsDelayedPaymentMethods: false,
        googlePay: {
          merchantCountryCode: 'US',
          testEnv: true, // set false in production
        },
        applePay: {
          merchantCountryCode: 'US',
        },
      });

 what would be return url here?
7 May

Claude responded: That's it.

tsx
returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',

That's it. Just replace yourapp with snowout-v2 which is your app's scheme from app.json.

You said: why it was used?

why it was used?

7 May

Claude responded: Already explained this above — when a payment needs extra verification (like 3D Secure / OTP), Stripe opens a browser outside your app.

Already explained this above — when a payment needs extra verification (like 3D Secure / OTP), Stripe opens a browser outside your app. After the user confirms, the browser needs to know how to come back to your app. That's the only purpose of returnURL.

User pays
   → Bank requires OTP verification
      → Stripe opens browser/bank app
         → User confirms OTP
            → Browser uses 'snowout-v2://payment-return' to reopen your app
               → Payment completes

Without it, after OTP confirmation the user would be stuck in the browser with no way back to your app.

You said: will it open in the browser or will open in the app?

will it open in the browser or will open in the app?

7 May

Claude responded: It will open inside your app — in an in-app browser (WebView).

It will open inside your app — in an in-app browser (WebView). The user never fully leaves your app.

Your App
   └── Stripe Payment Sheet opens (native UI, inside app)
         └── Bank requires OTP/3D Secure
               └── In-app browser opens (still inside your app)
                     └── User confirms OTP
                           └── In-app browser closes
                                 └── Back to your app ✓

The returnURL is just the signal that tells the in-app browser "close yourself and hand control back to the app". Without it, the in-app browser wouldn't know when to close.

You said: well.

well. can you please tell me how the save previous payment information will be done by asking saving information with the user's permission ?

7 May

Claude responded: For saved cards to work properly, your backend needs to return an ephemeral key along with the existing response.

For saved cards to work properly, your backend needs to return an ephemeral key along with the existing response. Here's the full picture:

What you need from backend

Ask your backend developer to update the /bid/accept-bid response to include ephemeralKey:

json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "clientSecret": "pi_xxx_secret_xxx",
    "stripeCustomerId": "cus_xxx",
    "ephemeralKey": "ek_test_xxx",      // ← request this to be added
    "paymentIntentId": "pi_xxx",
    "taskId": "xxx",
    "bidId": "xxx"
  }
}

How it works under the hood

First Payment
   → User enters card details
      → Stripe Payment Sheet shows "Save this card" toggle
         → User enables it and pays
            → Card saved against stripeCustomerId on Stripe servers
               → Never stored in your app or your backend

Second Payment
   → Stripe Payment Sheet opens
      → Previously saved card appears automatically
         → User just taps Pay ✓

Code update

Once backend returns ephemeralKey, just add one line to initPaymentSheet:

tsx
const { clientSecret, stripeCustomerId, ephemeralKey } = response.data;

const { error: initError } = await initPaymentSheet({
  merchantDisplayName: 'SnowOut',
  paymentIntentClientSecret: clientSecret,
  customerId: stripeCustomerId,
  customerEphemeralKeySecret: ephemeralKey,  // ← add this line
  returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',
  allowsDelayedPaymentMethods: false,
  googlePay: {
    merchantCountryCode: 'US',
    testEnv: true,
  },
  applePay: {
    merchantCountryCode: 'US',
  },
});

What the user sees

First time paying:

┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Add a payment method       │
│                             │
│  Card number  [____________]│
│  Expiry       [____] CVC [__]│
│                             │
│  ☑ Save card for future use │  ← Stripe shows this automatically
│                             │
│  [        Pay $50         ] │
└─────────────────────────────┘

Second time paying:

┌─────────────────────────────┐
│  Pay with saved card        │
│                             │
│  ✓ Visa •••• 4242           │  ← appears automatically
│                             │
│  + Add new payment method   │
│                             │
│  [        Pay $50         ] │
└─────────────────────────────┘

Stripe handles the "Save this card" toggle and user consent natively — you don't need to build any UI for it. The ephemeralKey is what gives the Payment Sheet permission to read and save cards against that stripeCustomerId.

You said: here as you have said the backend dev will provide

here as you have said the backend dev will provide customerEphemeralKeySecret: ephemeralKey, in the response

but the backend dev provide

{ "success": true, "message": "Bid accepted successfully", "data": { "clientSecret": "pi_3TUHkgEODmIkbEGg0zhxJ00W_secret_wF7B0KcQbZoyLnR99IrqZUzPN", "stripeCustomerId": "cus_UTEDqjOuV94L1F", "taskId": "69f97fe361e01a5399acd8aa", "bidId": "69fbf950e73621e7bd97f4ed", "paymentIntentId": "pi_3TUHkgEODmIkbEGg0zhxJ00W" } } these in the accept offer response and {{local_url}}/bid/accept-bid/69fbf950e73621e7bd97f4ed this was the endpoint. and he manually provided me another key in my inbox:

which has started with this: pk_test_51NEO3gEODm

7 May

Claude responded: So the key he sent you starting with pktest51NEO3gEODm — that is the Stripe Publishable Key, not the ephemeral key.

So the key he sent you starting with pk_test_51NEO3gEODm — that is the Stripe Publishable Key, not the ephemeral key. You already have that in your .env as EXPO_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY.

Current situation

The backend is not returning ephemeralKey in the response. So right now you have two options:

Option A — Ask backend to add ephemeralKey (recommended)

Tell your backend dev:

"Please add ephemeralKey to the /bid/accept-bid response. It should be a Stripe Ephemeral Key created for the stripeCustomerId on the server side."

Expected response:

json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "clientSecret": "pi_xxx_secret_xxx",
    "stripeCustomerId": "cus_xxx",
    "ephemeralKey": "ek_test_xxx",   // ← this is what's missing
    "paymentIntentId": "pi_xxx",
    "taskId": "xxx",
    "bidId": "xxx"
  }
}

Then in your code uncomment this line:

tsx
customerEphemeralKeySecret: ephemeralKey,

Option B — Proceed without it for now

Remove customerId from initPaymentSheet since without ephemeralKey it won't work properly anyway and may cause errors:

tsx
const { error: initError } = await initPaymentSheet({
  merchantDisplayName: 'SnowOut',
  paymentIntentClientSecret: clientSecret,
  // customerId: stripeCustomerId,          // ← remove for now
  // customerEphemeralKeySecret: ephemeralKey, // ← remove for now
  returnURL: 'snowout-v2://payment-return',
  allowsDelayedPaymentMethods: false,
  googlePay: {
    merchantCountryCode: 'US',
    testEnv: true,
  },
  applePay: {
    merchantCountryCode: 'US',
  },
});

This way card payments work perfectly — the only thing missing is the "save card for future use" feature. You can add that later once backend provides ephemeralKey.

My suggestion

Go with Option B now to unblock yourself, and ask backend to add ephemeralKey later as a separate task. Payment will work fully, just without saved cards.

You said: with the help of these code now on accept offer click and then the stripe text modal sliding up.

with the help of these code now on accept offer click and then the stripe text modal sliding up. but it requires card number MM/YY CVC Billing address: country

then save your info for secure 1-click checkout with link check mark

so what should I give in those inputs?

7 May

Claude responded: Those are Stripe's test card details.

Those are Stripe's test card details. Use these:

Card Number: 4242 4242 4242 4242

MM/YY: 12/26 (any future date)

CVC: 123 (any 3 digits)

Billing Address / Country: United States

"Save your info"toggle: leave it or skip — doesn't matter for testing

Hit Pay and it should go through successfully. That 4242 card is Stripe's official test card that always succeeds.

Other useful test cards

ScenarioCard Number
Payment succeeds4242 4242 4242 4242
Payment declined4000 0000 0000 0002
Requires 3D Secure (OTP)4000 0025 0000 3155

These only work because your Stripe key is pk_test_.... In production with a live key, real card details are required.

// /components/ui/provider/tasks/details/ActionButtons.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View } from 'react-native';
import PrimaryButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import OutlineButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/OutlineButton';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { LocationIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import { ProviderTaskDetailsType } from '@/hooks/providerSide/useProviderTaskDetails';

interface ActionButtonsProps {
  taskDetails: ProviderTaskDetailsType;
  isOpenTask: boolean;
  isPrivateTask: boolean;
  isAssigned: boolean;
  isInProgress: boolean;
  isCompleted: boolean;
  canAccept: boolean;
  canStart: boolean;
  canComplete: boolean;
  canCancel: boolean;
  canCounter: boolean;
  onAccept?: () => void; // Made optional since provider might have already bid
  onCounter: () => void;
  onStart: () => void;
  onComplete: () => void;
  onCancel: () => void;
  onTrackMap: () => void;
  onViewInvoice: () => void;
  isCancelled: boolean;
  isAccepting?: boolean;
  isCountering?: boolean;
}

const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isOpenTask,
  isPrivateTask,
  isAssigned,
  isInProgress,
  isCompleted,
  canAccept,
  canStart,
  canComplete,
  canCancel,
  canCounter,
  onAccept,
  onCounter,
  onStart,
  onComplete,
  onCancel,
  onTrackMap,
  onViewInvoice,
  isCancelled,
  isAccepting = false,
  isCountering = false,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  return (
    <View className="gap-2.5">
      {/* Accept Task Button - Only show if canAccept is true AND onAccept exists */}
      {canAccept && onAccept && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title={isAccepting ? 'Accepting...' : 'Accept Task'}
          onPress={onAccept}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          disabled={isAccepting}
        />
      )}

      {/* Counter Offer Button - Only show if canCounter is true */}
      {canCounter && (
        <OutlineButton
          title={isCountering ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Counter Offer'}
          onPress={onCounter}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          bgColor="#FFFFFF"
          disabled={isCountering}
        />
      )}

      {/* Start Button */}
      {canStart && (
        <PrimaryButton title="Start Task" onPress={onStart} style={{ borderRadius: 12 }} />
      )}

      {/* Complete Button */}
      {canComplete && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Mark as Complete"
          onPress={onComplete}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12, backgroundColor: '#10B981' }}
          rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View on Map Button */}
      {(isAssigned || isInProgress || isOpenTask || isPrivateTask) && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View Invoice Button */}
      {isCompleted && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<Ionicons name="document-text-outline" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* Cancel Button */}
      {canCancel && !isCancelled && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Cancel Task"
          onPress={onCancel}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
        />
      )}
    </View>
  );
};

export default ActionButtons;





import {
  BushIcon,
  ShovelIcon,
  WaterDropIcon,
  GrassCuttingMachineIcon,
  LeafIcon,
  CalanderWithClockIcon,
} from '@/components/icons/index';
import { ServiceType } from '@/types/services';

export interface ServiceTheme {
  title: string;
  Icon: React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; color?: string }>;
  color: string;
  bg: string;
  border: string;
}

export const SERVICE_THEMES: Record<ServiceType, ServiceTheme> = {
  'snow-plowing': {
    title: 'Snow Plowing',
    Icon: BushIcon,
    color: '#13c2fe',
    bg: '#FFF7ED', // orange-50
    border: '#F97316', // orange-500
  },
  'snow-shoveling': {
    title: 'Snow Shoveling',
    Icon: ShovelIcon,
    color: '#23afe0',
    bg: '#F7FEE7', // lime-50
    border: '#84cc16', // lime-500
  },
  'salting-deicing': {
    title: 'Salting / De-icing',
    Icon: WaterDropIcon,
    color: '#32ecea',
    bg: '#ECFDF5', // emerald-50
    border: '#10B981', // emerald-500
  },
  'lawn-mowing': {
    title: 'Lawn Mowing',
    Icon: GrassCuttingMachineIcon,
    color: '#61a617',
    bg: '#ECFEFF', // cyan-50
    border: '#06B6D4', // cyan-500
  },
  landscaping: {
    title: 'Landscaping',
    Icon: LeafIcon,
    color: '#75d50c',
    bg: '#EFF6FF', // blue-50
    border: '#3B82F6', // blue-500
  },
  'seasonal-contracts': {
    title: 'Seasonal Contracts',
    Icon: CalanderWithClockIcon,
    color: '#16d5bc',
    bg: '#FAF5FF', // purple-50
    border: '#a855f7', // purple-500
  },
};


// /components/ui/inputs/ImageUploadInputField.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, Image, TouchableOpacity, ScrollView, StyleSheet, Text } from 'react-native';
import { Feather } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import InputLabel from '@/components/ui/shared/InputLabel';
import * as ImagePicker from 'expo-image-picker';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import { ImageUploadIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import Subtitle from '@/components/ui/shared/Subtitle';

type Props = {
  value: string[];
  name: string;
  handler: (name: string, value: string[]) => void;
  label?: string;
  placeholder?: string;
  required?: boolean;
  showLabel?: boolean;
  max?: number;
  error?: boolean;
  errorMessage?: string;
};

const ImageUploadInputField: React.FC<Props> = ({
  value = [],
  handler,
  name,
  label = 'Upload Photos',
  placeholder = 'Add up to 8 images',
  required = false,
  showLabel = true,
  max = 8,
  error = false,
  errorMessage = '',
}) => {
  const pickImage = async () => {
    if (value.length >= max) return;

    const result = await ImagePicker.launchImageLibraryAsync({
      mediaTypes: ImagePicker.MediaTypeOptions.Images,
      allowsMultipleSelection: true,
      quality: 0.7,
      selectionLimit: max - value.length,
    });

    if (!result.canceled) {
      const selectedImages = result.assets.map((asset) => asset.uri);
      const newImages = [...value, ...selectedImages].slice(0, max);
      handler(name, newImages);
    }
  };

  const removeImage = (uri: string) => {
    const filtered = value.filter((i) => i !== uri);
    handler(name, filtered);
  };

  return (
    <View style={{ width: '100%' }}>
      {showLabel && (
        <InputLabel
          text={label}
          style={{
            marginBottom: 6,
            color: required ? (error ? '#EF4444' : '#4B5563') : '#4B5563',
          }}
        />
      )}

      <TouchableOpacity onPress={pickImage} activeOpacity={0.6} disabled={value.length >= max}>
        <BorderCard
          style={{
            paddingVertical: 16,
            borderColor: error ? '#EF4444' : '#E5E7EB',
            borderStyle: 'dashed',
            borderWidth: 1,
            backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF',
          }}>
          <View style={{ alignItems: 'center', gap: 10 }}>
            <ImageUploadIcon size={24} color="#9CA3AF" />
            <Subtitle text={placeholder} style={{ color: error ? '#EF4444' : '#9CA3AF' }} />
          </View>
        </BorderCard>
      </TouchableOpacity>

      {/* Image Preview */}
      {value.length > 0 && (
        <ScrollView horizontal showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false} style={{ marginTop: 12 }}>
          {value.map((uri, index) => (
            <View key={`${uri}-${index}`} style={styles.imageWrapper}>
              <Image source={{ uri }} style={styles.image} />
              <TouchableOpacity style={styles.removeButton} onPress={() => removeImage(uri)}>
                <Feather name="x" size={14} color="#fff" />
              </TouchableOpacity>
            </View>
          ))}
        </ScrollView>
      )}

      {/* Error message */}
      {error && errorMessage && (
        <Text style={{ color: '#EF4444', fontSize: 12, marginTop: 4 }}>{errorMessage}</Text>
      )}
    </View>
  );
};

const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  imageWrapper: {
    position: 'relative',
    marginRight: 12,
    borderRadius: 10,
    overflow: 'hidden',
    width: 70,
    height: 70,
    backgroundColor: '#F3F4F6',
    borderWidth: 1,
    borderColor: '#E5E7EB',
  },
  image: {
    width: '100%',
    height: '100%',
  },
  removeButton: {
    position: 'absolute',
    top: 4,
    right: 4,
    backgroundColor: 'rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.9)',
    borderRadius: 10,
    width: 20,
    height: 20,
    alignItems: 'center',
    justifyContent: 'center',
  },
});

export default ImageUploadInputField;



{{local_url}}/task/start-task/69edcf734ee0517e76f2cfda

{
    "success": false,
    "message": "Before images are required to start the task",
    "errorDetails": {},
    "stack": "Error: Before images are required to start the task\n    at D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\src\\app\\modules\\task\\task.service.ts:896:15\n    at Generator.next (<anonymous>)\n    at D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\src\\app\\modules\\task\\task.service.ts:8:71\n    at new Promise (<anonymous>)\n    at __awaiter (D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\src\\app\\modules\\task\\task.service.ts:4:12)\n    at Object.startTaskByProvider (D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\src\\app\\modules\\task\\task.service.ts:894:5)\n    at D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\src\\app\\modules\\task\\task.controller.ts:157:39\n    at Generator.next (<anonymous>)\n    at D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\src\\app\\modules\\task\\task.controller.ts:8:71\n    at new Promise (<anonymous>)"
}

now I need to integrate the start task properly
8 May

Claude responded: Looking at the error, the backend requires before images to be sent to the API.

Looking at the error, the backend requires before images to be sent to the API. Currently your StartTaskPage is only calling startTask() without passing the photos. Let me fix this properly.

Step 1: Update useProviderTaskDetails hook

Add a startTaskWithPhotos function that accepts photos:

ts
// In useProviderTaskDetails.ts

// Add this import at top if not already there
import { useStartTaskMutation } from '@/store/api/tasksApi'; // we'll add this

// Add the mutation
const [startTaskMutation, { isLoading: isStarting }] = useStartTaskMutation();

// Replace the existing startTask callback with this:
const startTask = useCallback(
  async (photos: string[]) => {
    if (!taskId) return;

    if (!isAssigned) {
      Toast.show({
        type: 'error',
        text1: 'Error',
        text2: 'Cannot start task that is not assigned',
      });
      return;
    }

    try {
      const formData = new FormData();

      // Append each photo as a file
      photos.forEach((uri, index) => {
        const filename = uri.split('/').pop() || `photo_${index}.jpg`;
        const match = /\.(\w+)$/.exec(filename);
        const type = match ? `image/${match[1]}` : 'image/jpeg';

        formData.append('before_images', {
          uri,
          name: filename,
          type,
        } as any);
      });

      const response = await startTaskMutation({
        taskId,
        formData,
      }).unwrap();

      if (response.success) {
        Toast.show({
          type: 'success',
          text1: 'Task Started!',
          text2: 'Task has been marked as started successfully',
          visibilityTime: 3000,
        });
        await refetchTask();
      }
    } catch (error: any) {
      Toast.show({
        type: 'error',
        text1: 'Failed to Start Task',
        text2: error.data?.message || 'Please try again',
        visibilityTime: 3000,
      });
      throw error; // re-throw so StartTaskPage can handle it
    }
  },
  [taskId, isAssigned, startTaskMutation, refetchTask]
);

// Add to return object:
// startTask,
// isStarting,

Step 2: Add startTask mutation to tasksApi

ts
// In store/api/tasksApi.ts — add this endpoint:

export interface StartTaskRequest {
  taskId: string;
  formData: FormData;
}

export interface StartTaskResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: any;
}

// Inside injectEndpoints:
startTask: builder.mutation<StartTaskResponse, StartTaskRequest>({
  query: ({ taskId, formData }) => ({
    url: `/task/start-task/${taskId}`,
    method: 'PATCH',
    body: formData,
    // Don't set Content-Type — fetch sets it automatically with boundary for FormData
    formData: true,
  }),
  invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
}),

// Export:
export const { ..., useStartTaskMutation } = tasksApi;

Step 3: Update StartTaskPage

tsx
const StartTaskPage = () => {
  const router = useRouter();
  const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();
  const [photos, setPhotos] = useState<string[]>([]);
  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
  const [error, setError] = useState('');

  const { taskDetails, isLoading, startTask, isStarting } = useProviderTaskDetails(id as string);

  const handlePhotoUpload = (name: string, value: string[]) => {
    setPhotos(value);
    if (value.length > 0) setError('');
  };

  const handleStartTask = async () => {
    if (photos.length === 0) {
      setError('Please upload at least one before photo');
      return;
    }

    setIsSubmitting(true);
    try {
      await startTask(photos); // ← pass photos here

      // On success navigate back to task details
      router.replace(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${id}`);

    } catch (err) {
      // Error already shown via Toast in the hook
    } finally {
      setIsSubmitting(false);
    }
  };

  const loading = isSubmitting || isStarting;

  // ... rest of your JSX stays the same, just update the button:

  <PrimaryButton
    title={loading ? 'Starting...' : 'Start Task'}
    onPress={handleStartTask}
    disabled={photos.length === 0 || loading}
    style={{
      borderRadius: 12,
      backgroundColor: photos.length === 0 ? '#9CA3AF' : '#10B981',
    }}
  />
};

What changed and why

Before:
  handleStartTask() → startTask() → router.push to start page (wrong, already on start page)
  Backend call: missing — photos were never sent to API

After:
  handleStartTask(photos) → buildFormData → PATCH /task/start-task/:id with images
                         → on success → router.replace back to task details

The key fix is FormData — the backend expects multipart/form-data with a field named before_images, not JSON. That's why the backend was throwing "Before images are required" even when you had photos selected locally.

You said: suggest again felt like I didn't send you the current ask api

suggest again felt like I didn't send you the current ask api

8 May

Claude responded: Looking at your current tasksApi, there's no startTask mutation yet.

Looking at your current tasksApi, there's no startTask mutation yet. Here's exactly what to add:

Step 1: Add to store/api/tasksApi.ts

ts
// Add these interfaces with the others at the top:

export interface StartTaskRequest {
  taskId: string;
  formData: FormData;
}

export interface StartTaskResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: any;
}

Then inside taskApi = baseApi.injectEndpoints, add this endpoint alongside the others:

ts
startTask: builder.mutation<StartTaskResponse, StartTaskRequest>({
  query: ({ taskId, formData }) => ({
    url: `/task/start-task/${taskId}`,
    method: 'PATCH',
    body: formData,
    formData: true, // tells RTK Query not to set Content-Type so fetch sets it with boundary automatically
  }),
  invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
}),

Then add to the export at the bottom:

ts
export const {
  useCreateTaskMutation,
  useCreatePrivateTaskMutation,
  useDeleteTaskMutation,
  useGetMyTasksQuery,
  useGetSingleTaskQuery,
  useAcceptOfferMutation,
  useStartTaskMutation,       // ← add this
} = taskApi;

Step 2: Update useProviderTaskDetails.ts

Add the import at the top:

ts
import { useGetSingleTaskQuery, useDeleteTaskMutation, useStartTaskMutation } from '@/store/api/tasksApi';

Add the mutation inside the hook body (alongside the other mutations):

ts
const [startTaskMutation, { isLoading: isStarting }] = useStartTaskMutation();

Replace the existing startTask callback entirely:

ts
const startTask = useCallback(
  async (photos: string[]) => {
    if (!taskId) return;

    if (!isAssigned) {
      Toast.show({
        type: 'error',
        text1: 'Error',
        text2: 'Cannot start task that is not assigned',
      });
      return;
    }

    const formData = new FormData();
    photos.forEach((uri, index) => {
      const filename = uri.split('/').pop() || `photo_${index}.jpg`;
      const match = /\.(\w+)$/.exec(filename);
      const type = match ? `image/${match[1]}` : 'image/jpeg';

      formData.append('before_images', {
        uri,
        name: filename,
        type,
      } as any);
    });

    const response = await startTaskMutation({ taskId, formData }).unwrap();

    if (response.success) {
      Toast.show({
        type: 'success',
        text1: 'Task Started!',
        text2: 'Task has been marked as started successfully',
        visibilityTime: 3000,
      });
      await refetchTask();
    }
  },
  [taskId, isAssigned, startTaskMutation, refetchTask]
);

Add isStarting to the return object:

ts
return {
  // ... existing returns
  startTask,
  isStarting,   // ← add this
};

Step 3: Update StartTaskPage

tsx
const StartTaskPage = () => {
  const router = useRouter();
  const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();
  const [photos, setPhotos] = useState<string[]>([]);
  const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
  const [error, setError] = useState('');

  const { taskDetails, isLoading, startTask, isStarting } = useProviderTaskDetails(id as string);

  const handlePhotoUpload = (name: string, value: string[]) => {
    setPhotos(value);
    if (value.length > 0) setError('');
  };

  const handleStartTask = async () => {
    if (photos.length === 0) {
      setError('Please upload at least one before photo');
      return;
    }

    setIsSubmitting(true);
    try {
      await startTask(photos);
      router.replace(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${id}`);
    } catch (err: any) {
      // Error toast already shown inside startTask
    } finally {
      setIsSubmitting(false);
    }
  };

  const isButtonLoading = isSubmitting || isStarting;

  // ... your existing loading/error/notFound returns stay the same

  // Update only the button:
  <PrimaryButton
    title={isButtonLoading ? 'Starting...' : 'Start Task'}
    onPress={handleStartTask}
    disabled={photos.length === 0 || isButtonLoading}
    style={{
      borderRadius: 12,
      backgroundColor: photos.length === 0 ? '#9CA3AF' : '#10B981',
    }}
  />
};

The key change is the startTask hook now accepts photos: string[] instead of being a navigation function. It builds a FormData with before_images field (matching what the backend expects) and sends it as multipart/form-data via the formData: true flag in RTK Query.

<ActionButtons
              taskDetails={taskDetails}
              isOpenTask={isOpenTask}
              isPrivateTask={isPrivateTask}
              isAssigned={isAssigned}
              isInProgress={isInProgress}
              isCompleted={isCompleted}
              canAccept={canAccept}
              canStart={canStart}
              canComplete={canComplete}
              canCancel={canCancel}
              canCounter={canCounter}
              onCounter={() => counterSheetRef.current?.open()}
              onAccept={hasUserBid ? undefined : handleAcceptPress} // ✅ Pass undefined if already bid
              onStart={startTask}
              onComplete={completeTask}
              onCancel={() => cancelSheetRef.current?.open()}
              onTrackMap={trackOnMap}
              onViewInvoice={viewInvoice}
              isCancelled={isCancelled}
              isAccepting={isAccepting}
            />

this showing: 
Type '(photos: string[]) => Promise<void>' is not assignable to type '() => void'.
  Target signature provides too few arguments. Expected 1 or more, but got 0.
8 May

Claude responded: The onStart prop in ActionButtons is typed as () => void but startTask now takes photos: string[].

The onStart prop in ActionButtons is typed as () => void but startTask now takes photos: string[].

Since ActionButtons doesn't handle photos (that's StartTaskPage's job), startTask in the hook should still navigate to the start page — and the actual API call happens in StartTaskPage.

Keep the hook's startTask as navigation only, and add a separate function for the API call:

In useProviderTaskDetails.ts, have two separate functions:

ts
// This stays as navigation — called from ActionButtons
const startTask = useCallback(() => {
  if (!isAssigned) {
    Toast.show({
      type: 'error',
      text1: 'Error',
      text2: 'Cannot start task that is not assigned',
    });
    return;
  }
  router.push(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${taskId}/start`);
}, [isAssigned, router, taskId]);

// This is the API call — called from StartTaskPage
const submitStartTask = useCallback(
  async (photos: string[]) => {
    if (!taskId) return;

    const formData = new FormData();
    photos.forEach((uri, index) => {
      const filename = uri.split('/').pop() || `photo_${index}.jpg`;
      const match = /\.(\w+)$/.exec(filename);
      const type = match ? `image/${match[1]}` : 'image/jpeg';
      formData.append('before_images', { uri, name: filename, type } as any);
    });

    const response = await startTaskMutation({ taskId, formData }).unwrap();

    if (response.success) {
      Toast.show({
        type: 'success',
        text1: 'Task Started!',
        text2: 'Task has been marked as started successfully',
        visibilityTime: 3000,
      });
      await refetchTask();
    }
  },
  [taskId, startTaskMutation, refetchTask]
);

Add both to the return:

ts
startTask,       // navigation → used by ActionButtons → onStart
submitStartTask, // API call  → used by StartTaskPage
isStarting,

Then in StartTaskPage:

tsx
const { taskDetails, isLoading, submitStartTask, isStarting } = useProviderTaskDetails(id as string);

const handleStartTask = async () => {
  if (photos.length === 0) {
    setError('Please upload at least one before photo');
    return;
  }
  setIsSubmitting(true);
  try {
    await submitStartTask(photos); // ← use submitStartTask here
    router.replace(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${id}`);
  } catch (err) {
    // toast already shown in hook
  } finally {
    setIsSubmitting(false);
  }
};

ActionButtons stays unchanged — onStart={startTask} still works since startTask is () => void.

// /components/ui/provider/tasks/details/ActionButtons.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View } from 'react-native';
import PrimaryButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import OutlineButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/OutlineButton';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { LocationIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import { ProviderTaskDetailsType } from '@/hooks/providerSide/useProviderTaskDetails';

interface ActionButtonsProps {
  taskDetails: ProviderTaskDetailsType;
  isOpenTask: boolean;
  isPrivateTask: boolean;
  isAssigned: boolean;
  isInProgress: boolean;
  isCompleted: boolean;
  canAccept: boolean;
  canStart: boolean;
  canComplete: boolean;
  canCancel: boolean;
  canCounter: boolean;
  onAccept?: () => void; // Made optional since provider might have already bid
  onCounter: () => void;
  onStart: () => void;
  onComplete: () => void;
  onCancel: () => void;
  onTrackMap: () => void;
  onViewInvoice: () => void;
  isCancelled: boolean;
  isAccepting?: boolean;
  isCountering?: boolean;
}

const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isOpenTask,
  isPrivateTask,
  isAssigned,
  isInProgress,
  isCompleted,
  canAccept,
  canStart,
  canComplete,
  canCancel,
  canCounter,
  onAccept,
  onCounter,
  onStart,
  onComplete,
  onCancel,
  onTrackMap,
  onViewInvoice,
  isCancelled,
  isAccepting = false,
  isCountering = false,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  return (
    <View className="gap-2.5">
      {/* Accept Task Button - Only show if canAccept is true AND onAccept exists */}
      {canAccept && onAccept && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title={isAccepting ? 'Accepting...' : 'Accept Task'}
          onPress={onAccept}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          disabled={isAccepting}
        />
      )}

      {/* Counter Offer Button - Only show if canCounter is true */}
      {canCounter && (
        <OutlineButton
          title={isCountering ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Counter Offer'}
          onPress={onCounter}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          bgColor="#FFFFFF"
          disabled={isCountering}
        />
      )}

      {/* Start Button */}
      {canStart && (
        <PrimaryButton title="Start Task" onPress={onStart} style={{ borderRadius: 12 }} />
      )}

      {/* Complete Button */}
      {canComplete && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Mark as Complete"
          onPress={onComplete}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12, backgroundColor: '#10B981' }}
          rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View on Map Button */}
      {(isAssigned || isInProgress || isOpenTask || isPrivateTask) && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View Invoice Button */}
      {isCompleted && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<Ionicons name="document-text-outline" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* Cancel Button */}
      {canCancel && !isCancelled && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Cancel Task"
          onPress={onCancel}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
        />
      )}
    </View>
  );
};

export default ActionButtons;


showing no response in the toast just start task button color cheged and then back again but no response status.
8 May

Claude responded: The issue is that submitStartTask in the hook doesn't throw errors — it just silently fails.

The issue is that submitStartTask in the hook doesn't throw errors — it just silently fails. Also the startedAt mapping in taskDetails is wrong (it uses isMarkCompletedByProvider instead of checking if task is in progress). Let me fix both.

Problem 1: submitStartTask swallows errors

Add a throw so StartTaskPage knows it failed:

ts
// In useProviderTaskDetails.ts - submitStartTask callback
const submitStartTask = useCallback(
  async (photos: string[]) => {
    if (!taskId) return;

    const formData = new FormData();
    photos.forEach((uri, index) => {
      const filename = uri.split('/').pop() || `photo_${index}.jpg`;
      const match = /\.(\w+)$/.exec(filename);
      const type = match ? `image/${match[1]}` : 'image/jpeg';
      formData.append('before_images', { uri, name: filename, type } as any);
    });

    try {                                          // ← add try/catch
      const response = await startTaskMutation({ taskId, formData }).unwrap();

      if (response.success) {
        Toast.show({
          type: 'success',
          text1: 'Task Started!',
          text2: 'Task has been marked as started successfully',
          visibilityTime: 3000,
        });
        await refetchTask();
      }
    } catch (error: any) {
      console.error('❌ Start task error:', error);
      Toast.show({
        type: 'error',
        text1: 'Failed to Start Task',
        text2: error.data?.message || 'Please try again',
        visibilityTime: 3000,
      });
      throw error;                                 // ← re-throw so page knows
    }
  },
  [taskId, startTaskMutation, refetchTask]
);

Problem 2: Check what the API actually sends back

Add a console log temporarily to see the raw API response:

ts
startTask: builder.mutation<StartTaskResponse, StartTaskRequest>({
  query: ({ taskId, formData }) => ({
    url: `/task/start-task/${taskId}`,
    method: 'PATCH',
    body: formData,
    formData: true,
  }),
  transformResponse: (response: StartTaskResponse) => {
    console.log('✅ Start Task API Response:', JSON.stringify(response)); // ← add this
    return response;
  },
  transformErrorResponse: (response) => {
    console.log('❌ Start Task API Error:', JSON.stringify(response));   // ← add this
    return response;
  },
  invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
}),

Problem 3: baseApi might strip FormData

Check your baseApi.ts — if it sets Content-Type: application/json globally, it will break FormData. Your baseApi likely looks like this:

ts
// store/api/baseApi.ts
prepareHeaders: (headers) => {
  headers.set('Content-Type', 'application/json'); // ← THIS breaks FormData
  return headers;
}

Fix it by only setting JSON content type when not FormData:

ts
prepareHeaders: (headers, { endpoint }) => {
  const token = // ... your token logic
  if (token) {
    headers.set('Authorization', `Bearer ${token}`);
  }
  // ← Remove any Content-Type set here
  // Let fetch set it automatically for FormData
  return headers;
},

RTK Query with formData: true handles the Content-Type boundary automatically — but only if you don't override it manually in prepareHeaders.

Problem 4: StartTaskPage uses wrong id param

Your start page route is /provider/provider-flow/tasks/[id]/start.tsx — note it uses [id] not [taskId]. But the router.replace path after success uses task-details/${id} which should be correct. Double check the file is actually at that path and not task-details/[taskId]/start.tsx.

After adding the logs, share what appears in the console when you tap "Start Task" — that'll tell us exactly where it's failing.

// store/api/baseApi.ts
import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react';
import { Platform } from 'react-native';
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';

// AsyncStorage keys
const TOKEN_KEY = 'auth_token';

// Storage functions using AsyncStorage
const getToken = async () => {
  try {
    return await AsyncStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error getting token:', error);
    return null;
  }
};

const setToken = async (token: string) => {
  try {
    await AsyncStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, token);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error saving token:', error);
  }
};

const removeToken = async () => {
  try {
    await AsyncStorage.removeItem(TOKEN_KEY);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error removing token:', error);
  }
};

// Base query with authentication
// store/api/baseApi.ts - Add debug in prepareHeaders
const baseQuery = fetchBaseQuery({
  baseUrl: process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://10.10.20.9:9000/api/v1',
  prepareHeaders: async (headers) => {
    const token = await getToken();

    console.log('🔑 prepareHeaders - token exists:', !!token);

    if (token) {
      headers.set('Authorization', `Bearer ${token}`);
      console.log('✅ Authorization header set with token');
    } else {
      console.log('⚠️ No token found, Authorization header not set');
    }

    headers.set('Content-Type', 'application/json');
    headers.set('Accept', 'application/json');
    headers.set('X-Platform', Platform.OS);
    headers.set('X-App-Version', '1.0.0');

    return headers;
  },
});

// Custom base query with error handling
const baseQueryWithReauth = async (args: any, api: any, extraOptions: any) => {
  let result = await baseQuery(args, api, extraOptions);

  // Handle 401 unauthorized
  if (result.error?.status === 401) {
    // Clear token on unauthorized
    await removeToken();
    // Dispatch logout action if dispatch is available
    if (api.dispatch) {
      api.dispatch({ type: 'auth/logout' });
    }
  }

  return result;
};

// Create the base API
export const baseApi = createApi({
  reducerPath: 'api',
  baseQuery: baseQueryWithReauth,
  tagTypes: [
    'User', // For user profile
    'Home', // For home data
    'Task', // For tasks
    'Provider',
    'Customer',
    'Bids', // For bids
    'Messages', // For chat
    'Notifications', // For notifications
    'Reports', // For reports
    'PaymentMethods', // For payments
    'Transactions', // For transactions
  ],
  endpoints: () => ({}),
});

export { setToken, removeToken, getToken };


here is the base api
8 May

Claude responded: Found it.

Found it. This line is the problem:

ts
headers.set('Content-Type', 'application/json'); // ← breaks FormData

When you send FormData, the browser/fetch needs to set Content-Type automatically to multipart/form-data; boundary=----xyz.... By hardcoding application/json, you're overriding that and the backend never receives the images.

Fix: Only set Content-Type for non-FormData requests

ts
prepareHeaders: async (headers, { extra }) => {
  const token = await getToken();

  if (token) {
    headers.set('Authorization', `Bearer ${token}`);
  }

  // ← REMOVE these two lines entirely:
  // headers.set('Content-Type', 'application/json');  // DELETE
  // headers.set('Accept', 'application/json');         // DELETE

  // Keep these:
  headers.set('X-Platform', Platform.OS);
  headers.set('X-App-Version', '1.0.0');

  return headers;
},

RTK Query automatically sets Content-Type: application/json for regular JSON requests, and leaves it unset (letting fetch set it with the correct boundary) for FormData when you use formData: true. You don't need to set it manually at all.

Your updated baseApi.ts:

ts
const baseQuery = fetchBaseQuery({
  baseUrl: process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://10.10.20.9:9000/api/v1',
  prepareHeaders: async (headers) => {
    const token = await getToken();

    if (token) {
      headers.set('Authorization', `Bearer ${token}`);
    }

    headers.set('X-Platform', Platform.OS);
    headers.set('X-App-Version', '1.0.0');

    return headers;
  },
});

That's the only change needed. All your existing JSON endpoints will still work fine — RTK Query handles the Content-Type for them automatically.

You said: provide whole basequery code

provide whole basequery code and the related task api 

startTask: builder.mutation<StartTaskResponse, StartTaskRequest>({
      query: ({ taskId, formData }) => ({
        url: `/task/start-task/${taskId}`,
        method: 'PATCH',
        body: formData,
        formData: true,
      }),
      transformResponse: (response: StartTaskResponse) => {
        console.log('✅ Start Task API Response:', JSON.stringify(response)); // ← add this
        return response;
      },
      transformErrorResponse: (response) => {
        console.log('❌ Start Task API Error:', JSON.stringify(response)); // ← add this
        return response;
      },
      invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
    }),
8 May

Claude responded: Here are both files in full:

Here are both files in full:

store/api/baseApi.ts

ts
import { createApi, fetchBaseQuery } from '@reduxjs/toolkit/query/react';
import { Platform } from 'react-native';
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';

const TOKEN_KEY = 'auth_token';

const getToken = async () => {
  try {
    return await AsyncStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error getting token:', error);
    return null;
  }
};

const setToken = async (token: string) => {
  try {
    await AsyncStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, token);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error saving token:', error);
  }
};

const removeToken = async () => {
  try {
    await AsyncStorage.removeItem(TOKEN_KEY);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error removing token:', error);
  }
};

const baseQuery = fetchBaseQuery({
  baseUrl: process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://10.10.20.9:9000/api/v1',
  prepareHeaders: async (headers) => {
    const token = await getToken();

    if (token) {
      headers.set('Authorization', `Bearer ${token}`);
    }

    // ✅ DO NOT set Content-Type here
    // RTK Query sets 'application/json' automatically for JSON requests
    // and sets 'multipart/form-data' with correct boundary for FormData requests
    headers.set('X-Platform', Platform.OS);
    headers.set('X-App-Version', '1.0.0');

    return headers;
  },
});

const baseQueryWithReauth = async (args: any, api: any, extraOptions: any) => {
  let result = await baseQuery(args, api, extraOptions);

  if (result.error?.status === 401) {
    await removeToken();
    if (api.dispatch) {
      api.dispatch({ type: 'auth/logout' });
    }
  }

  return result;
};

export const baseApi = createApi({
  reducerPath: 'api',
  baseQuery: baseQueryWithReauth,
  tagTypes: [
    'User',
    'Home',
    'Task',
    'Provider',
    'Customer',
    'Bids',
    'Messages',
    'Notifications',
    'Reports',
    'PaymentMethods',
    'Transactions',
  ],
  endpoints: () => ({}),
});

export { setToken, removeToken, getToken };

store/api/tasksApi.ts

ts
import { baseApi } from './baseApi';
import { toFrontendFormat } from '@/utils/serviceTypeMapper';
import { TaskItem, TaskStatus } from '@/types/Tasks';

// ============ Create Task Types ============
export interface CreateTaskRequest {
  title: string;
  serviceType: string;
  budget: number;
  taskStartDateTime: string;
  location: {
    type: 'Point';
    coordinates: [number, number];
  };
  address: string;
  description: string;
  task_attachments?: string[];
  provider?: string;
}

export interface TaskResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: {
    _id: string;
    title: string;
    serviceType: string;
    budget: number;
    taskStartDateTime: string;
    status: string;
    customer: string;
    provider: string | null;
    location: {
      type: string;
      coordinates: [number, number];
    };
    address: string;
    description: string;
    task_attachments: string[];
    createdAt: string;
    updatedAt: string;
  };
}

export interface DeleteTaskResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: null;
}

// ============ Get My Tasks Types ============
export interface MyTasksResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: {
    meta: {
      page: number;
      limit: number;
      total: number;
      totalPage: number;
    };
    result: TaskResult[];
  };
}

export interface TaskResult {
  _id: string;
  title: string;
  serviceType: string;
  budget: number;
  taskStartDateTime: string;
  status: string;
  address: string;
  location: {
    type: string;
    coordinates: [number, number];
  };
  description: string;
  task_attachments: string[];
  provider: string | null;
  customer: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
  isMarkCompletedByProvider?: boolean;
  markCompletedByProviderAt?: string | null;
  markCompletedByCustomerAt?: string | null;
  cancelledAt?: string | null;
  totalOffer?: number;
}

export interface GetMyTasksParams {
  status?: string;
  searchTerm?: string;
  page?: number;
  limit?: number;
  sortBy?: string;
  sortOrder?: 'asc' | 'desc';
  serviceType?: string;
}

// ============ Get Single Task Types ============
export interface SingleTaskResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: TaskResult;
}

export interface AcceptOfferRequest {
  taskId: string;
}

export interface AcceptOfferResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: any;
}

// ============ Start Task Types ============
export interface StartTaskRequest {
  taskId: string;
  formData: FormData;
}

export interface StartTaskResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: any;
}

// ============ Helper Functions ============
export const mapStatusToTaskStatus = (status: string): TaskStatus => {
  const statusMap: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
    OPEN: 'open',
    PRIVATE: 'private',
    ASSIGNED: 'assigned',
    IN_PROGRESS: 'in-progress',
    COMPLETED: 'completed',
    CANCELLED: 'cancelled',
  };
  return statusMap[status] || 'open';
};

export const transformToTaskItem = (task: TaskResult): TaskItem => ({
  id: task._id,
  serviceType: toFrontendFormat(task.serviceType),
  taskStatus: mapStatusToTaskStatus(task.status),
  serviceLocation: task.address,
  taskValue: task.budget,
  scheduledDate: task.taskStartDateTime?.split('T')[0],
  scheduledTime: task.taskStartDateTime?.split('T')[1]?.slice(0, 5),
  createdAt: task.createdAt,
  address: task.address,
  title: task.title,
  budget: task.budget,
  coordinates: task.location?.coordinates
    ? {
        lat: task.location.coordinates[1],
        lng: task.location.coordinates[0],
      }
    : undefined,
});

export const taskApi = baseApi.injectEndpoints({
  endpoints: (builder) => ({
    // Create open task
    createTask: builder.mutation<TaskResponse, CreateTaskRequest>({
      query: (taskData) => ({
        url: '/task/create-task',
        method: 'POST',
        body: taskData,
      }),
      invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
    }),

    // Create private task
    createPrivateTask: builder.mutation<TaskResponse, CreateTaskRequest>({
      query: (taskData) => ({
        url: '/task/create-task',
        method: 'POST',
        body: taskData,
      }),
      invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
    }),

    // Delete/Cancel task
    deleteTask: builder.mutation<DeleteTaskResponse, string>({
      query: (taskId) => ({
        url: `/task/delete-task/${taskId}`,
        method: 'DELETE',
      }),
      invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
    }),

    // Get my tasks with filters
    getMyTasks: builder.query<MyTasksResponse['data'], GetMyTasksParams>({
      query: (params) => {
        const queryParams = new URLSearchParams();
        if (params.status) queryParams.append('status', params.status);
        if (params.searchTerm) queryParams.append('searchTerm', params.searchTerm);
        if (params.page) queryParams.append('page', params.page.toString());
        if (params.limit) queryParams.append('limit', params.limit.toString());
        if (params.sortBy) queryParams.append('sortBy', params.sortBy);
        if (params.sortOrder) queryParams.append('sortOrder', params.sortOrder);
        if (params.serviceType) queryParams.append('serviceType', params.serviceType);

        const queryString = queryParams.toString();
        return `/task/my-task${queryString ? `?${queryString}` : ''}`;
      },
      transformResponse: (response: MyTasksResponse) => {
        return response.data;
      },
      providesTags: ['Task'],
    }),

    // Get single task by ID
    getSingleTask: builder.query<TaskResult, string>({
      query: (id) => `/task/single-task/${id}`,
      transformResponse: (response: SingleTaskResponse) => {
        return response.data;
      },
      providesTags: (result, error, id) => [{ type: 'Task', id }],
    }),

    // Accept offer
    acceptOffer: builder.mutation<AcceptOfferResponse, AcceptOfferRequest>({
      query: ({ taskId }) => ({
        url: '/task/acceptOffer',
        method: 'PATCH',
        body: { taskId },
      }),
      invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
    }),

    // ✅ Start task with before images
    startTask: builder.mutation<StartTaskResponse, StartTaskRequest>({
      query: ({ taskId, formData }) => ({
        url: `/task/start-task/${taskId}`,
        method: 'PATCH',
        body: formData,
        formData: true,
      }),
      transformResponse: (response: StartTaskResponse) => {
        console.log('✅ Start Task API Response:', JSON.stringify(response));
        return response;
      },
      transformErrorResponse: (response) => {
        console.log('❌ Start Task API Error:', JSON.stringify(response));
        return response;
      },
      invalidatesTags: ['Task'],
    }),
  }),
});

export const {
  useCreateTaskMutation,
  useCreatePrivateTaskMutation,
  useDeleteTaskMutation,
  useGetMyTasksQuery,
  useGetSingleTaskQuery,
  useAcceptOfferMutation,
  useStartTaskMutation,
} = taskApi;

You said: LOG ❌ Start Task API Error: {"status":500,"data":{"success":false,"message":"Something went wrong","errorDetails":{},"stack":"MulterError: Unexpected field\n …

Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\node_modules\\multer\\index.js:40:19)\n at Multipart.<anonymous> (D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\node_modules\\multer\\lib\\make-middleware.js:109:7)\n at Multipart.emit (node:events:518:28)\n at HeaderParser.cb (D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\node_modules\\busboy\\lib\\types\\multipart.js:358:14)\n at HeaderParser.push (D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\node_modules\\busboy\\lib\\types\\multipart.js:162:20)\n at SBMH.ssCb [as _cb] (D:\\Manik SBMH.push (D:\\Manik Sarker\\Backend\\kebo-backend\\node_modules\\streamsearch\\lib\\sbmh.js:104:16)\n at Multipart._write (D:\\Manik 8 May

Claude responded: The error is clear:

The error is clear:

MulterError: Unexpected field

The backend's Multer is configured to accept a specific field name, but you're sending before_images. Ask your backend developer what the exact field name is, or check the backend code.

Most likely it's one of these:

  • beforeImages
  • before_photos
  • images
  • photos

Quick fix — just change the field name

In useProviderTaskDetails.ts, inside submitStartTask:

ts
// Change this:
formData.append('before_images', { uri, name: filename, type } as any);

// To whatever the backend expects, for example:
formData.append('beforeImages', { uri, name: filename, type } as any);

How to find the correct field name

Tell your backend developer:

"What is the exact Multer field name configured for the /task/start-task/:id endpoint?"

Or if you have access to the backend code, look for something like:

js
// Backend will have something like:
upload.array('beforeImages', 10)   // ← this is the field name
// or
upload.fields([{ name: 'before_photos' }])  // ← or this

Once you know the exact name, replace before_images with it and it will work.

You said: beforeImages

beforeImages It would be this.

guide me properly now.

8 May

Claude responded: Just one change needed.

Just one change needed. In useProviderTaskDetails.ts, inside submitStartTask, change the field name:

ts
// Before:
formData.append('before_images', { uri, name: filename, type } as any);

// After:
formData.append('beforeImages', { uri, name: filename, type } as any);

That's the only change. Your full submitStartTask callback now looks like this:

ts
const submitStartTask = useCallback(
  async (photos: string[]) => {
    if (!taskId) return;

    const formData = new FormData();
    photos.forEach((uri, index) => {
      const filename = uri.split('/').pop() || `photo_${index}.jpg`;
      const match = /\.(\w+)$/.exec(filename);
      const type = match ? `image/${match[1]}` : 'image/jpeg';
      formData.append('beforeImages', { uri, name: filename, type } as any); // ← fixed
    });

    try {
      const response = await startTaskMutation({ taskId, formData }).unwrap();

      if (response.success) {
        Toast.show({
          type: 'success',
          text1: 'Task Started!',
          text2: 'Task has been marked as started successfully',
          visibilityTime: 3000,
        });
        await refetchTask();
      }
    } catch (error: any) {
      console.error('❌ Start task error:', error);
      Toast.show({
        type: 'error',
        text1: 'Failed to Start Task',
        text2: error.data?.message || 'Please try again',
        visibilityTime: 3000,
      });
      throw error;
    }
  },
  [taskId, startTaskMutation, refetchTask]
);

Everything else — baseApi, tasksApi, StartTaskPage, the hook's return — stays exactly as is. Just that one field name from before_images to beforeImages.

You said: I need to make it proper

// hooks/tasks/useTaskBids.ts
import { useMemo } from 'react';
import { useGetTaskBidsQuery } from '@/store/api/bidApi';
import { useAuth } from '@/hooks/useAuth';

export const useTaskBids = (taskId: string | undefined) => {
  const { user } = useAuth();

  const {
    data: bids = [],
    isLoading,
    error,
    refetch,
  } = useGetTaskBidsQuery(taskId || '', {
    skip: !taskId,
  });

  // Filter out the current user's bid (if customer also bid? unlikely)
  const providerBids = useMemo(() => {
    return bids.filter((bid) => bid.provider._id !== user?._id);
  }, [bids, user]);

  return {
    bids: providerBids,
    allBids: bids,
    isLoading,
    error,
    refetch,
    hasBids: providerBids.length > 0,
  };
};


{{local_url}}/task/my-task


I need to show the customer 
// Provider marked complete but customer hasn't confirmed yet
  if (
    isInProgress &&
    taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete &&
    !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isCustomerMarkedComplete
  ) {
    return (
      <>
        <View className="gap-[10px]">
          <BorderCard style={{ padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#E8F5E9', borderRadius: 12 }}>
            <Text className="text-center font-nunitoSemi text-green-600">
              Provider has marked this task as complete. Please review the work and confirm.
            </Text>
          </BorderCard>

            }

          />

          onClose={() => setCancelModalVisible(false)}
          onSubmit={handleCancelSubmit}

        />
      </>
