when the task has started from provider's side other wise task has not started yet properly in in progress and 
also should show provider info accordingly.  

// /components/task/details/ProviderInfo.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text, TouchableOpacity } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import Avatar from '@/components/ui/shared/Avatar';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';
import { TaskDetails } from '@/mock/tasks/taskDetails';

interface Props {
  provider: NonNullable<TaskDetails['provider']>;
  onPress: (providerId: string) => void;
}

const ProviderInfo = ({ provider, onPress }: Props) => {
  const getStatusText = () => {
    switch (provider.responseStatus) {
      case 'pending':
        return (
          <Text className="font-nunitoSemi text-xs text-amber-500">Waiting for response...</Text>
        );
      case 'countered':
        return (
          <Text className="font-nunitoSemi text-xs text-blue-500">
            Counter offered ${provider.counterAmount}
          </Text>
        );
      case 'accepted':
        return (
          <Text className="font-nunitoSemi text-xs text-green-500">Accepted your request</Text>
        );
      default:
        return null;
    }
  };

  return (
    <TouchableOpacity activeOpacity={0.6} onPress={() => onPress(provider.id)}>
      <BorderCard className="gap-[10px]" style={{ padding: 12 }}>
        <View className="flex-row items-center">
          <View className="flex-1 flex-row items-center gap-3">
            <Avatar
              width={52}
              height={52}
              source={{ uri: provider.avatarUrl }}
              verified={provider.isVerified}
              verifiedSize={10}
            />
            <View className="flex-1 gap-1">
              <LabelPrimary
                numberOfLines={1}
                text="Service Provider"
                style={{ color: '#9CA3AF', fontSize: 12 }}
              />
              <LabelPrimary text={provider.fullName} />
              {getStatusText()}
            </View>
          </View>
          <Ionicons name="chevron-forward-outline" size={16} color="#2B7FFF" />
        </View>
      </BorderCard>
    </TouchableOpacity>
  );
};

export default ProviderInfo;

I need to make it proper

8 May

Claude responded: Looking at your API response, the task has provider as just an ID string, not a populated object.

Looking at your API response, the task has provider as just an ID string, not a populated object. Also beforeImages array tells us if the task has truly started. Let me fix everything properly.

Step 1: Update TaskResult type in tasksApi.ts

Add the missing fields from the API response:

ts
export interface TaskResult {
  _id: string;
  title: string;
  serviceType: string;
  budget: number;
  taskStartDateTime: string;
  status: string;
  address: string;
  location: {
    type: string;
    coordinates: [number, number];
  };
  description: string;
  task_attachments: string[];
  provider: string | null;                    // just an ID
  customer: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
  isMarkCompletedByProvider?: boolean;
  markCompletedByProviderAt?: string | null;
  markCompletedByCustomerAt?: string | null;
  cancelledAt?: string | null;
  totalOffer?: number;
  beforeImages?: string[];                    // ← add this
  afterImages?: string[];                     // ← add this
  acceptedBidAmount?: number | null;          // ← add this
  paymentStatus?: string;                     // ← add this
}

Step 2: Update useTaskDetails.ts

Add hasStartedTask, beforeImages, providerInfo properly:

ts
export interface TaskDetailsType {
  id: string;
  serviceType: ServiceType;
  taskStatus: string;
  serviceLocation: string;
  taskValue: number;
  details: string;
  scheduledDate?: string;
  scheduledTime?: string;
  createdAt?: string;
  taskPhotos: string[];
  providerId: string | null;                  // ← raw ID
  counterOffer: number | null;
  paymentInfo: {
    subtotal: number;
    fees: number;
    total: number;
  };
  completionStatus: {
    isProviderMarkedComplete: boolean;
    isCustomerMarkedComplete: boolean;
    isCustomerRated: boolean;
    rating?: number;
    review?: string;
  };
  beforeImages: string[];                     // ← add
  afterImages: string[];                      // ← add
  hasStartedTask: boolean;                    // ← add
  acceptedBidAmount: number | null;           // ← add
  startedAt?: { date: string; time: string; fullDateTime: string } | null;
  completedAt?: { date: string; time: string; fullDateTime: string } | null;
  cancelledAt?: { date: string; time: string; fullDateTime: string } | null;
}

Update taskDetails useMemo:

ts
const taskDetails = useMemo((): TaskDetailsType | null => {
  if (!rawTask) return null;

  const taskStatus = mapStatusToTaskStatus(rawTask.status);
  const serviceType = toFrontendFormat(rawTask.serviceType);
  const scheduledDate = formatDate(rawTask.taskStartDateTime);
  const createdAt = formatDate(rawTask.createdAt);
  const cancelledAt = rawTask.cancelledAt ? formatDate(rawTask.cancelledAt) : null;

  const beforeImages = rawTask.beforeImages || [];
  const afterImages = rawTask.afterImages || [];
  const hasStartedTask = beforeImages.length > 0;   // ← task truly started when before images uploaded

  return {
    id: rawTask._id,
    serviceType: serviceType as ServiceType,
    taskStatus,
    serviceLocation: rawTask.address,
    taskValue: rawTask.budget,
    details: rawTask.description,
    scheduledDate: scheduledDate?.date,
    scheduledTime: scheduledDate?.time,
    createdAt: createdAt?.fullDateTime,
    taskPhotos: rawTask.task_attachments || [],
    providerId: rawTask.provider,              // ← just the ID string
    counterOffer: null,
    paymentInfo: {
      subtotal: rawTask.acceptedBidAmount || rawTask.budget,
      fees: rawTask.acceptedBidAmount || rawTask.budget,
      total: rawTask.acceptedBidAmount || rawTask.budget,
    },
    completionStatus: {
      isProviderMarkedComplete: rawTask.isMarkCompletedByProvider || false,
      isCustomerMarkedComplete: !!rawTask.markCompletedByCustomerAt,
      isCustomerRated: false,
    },
    beforeImages,
    afterImages,
    hasStartedTask,
    acceptedBidAmount: rawTask.acceptedBidAmount || null,
    startedAt: hasStartedTask ? formatDate(rawTask.markCompletedByProviderAt) : null,
    completedAt: rawTask.markCompletedByCustomerAt
      ? formatDate(rawTask.markCompletedByCustomerAt)
      : null,
    cancelledAt,
  };
}, [rawTask]);

Add hasStartedTask to the return:

ts
return {
  // ... existing
  hasStartedTask: taskDetails?.hasStartedTask || false,  // ← add
};

Step 3: Update ProviderInfo.tsx

Since provider is just an ID now, fetch provider info separately or use bid data. Since the customer already has bid data with provider details, use that:

tsx
// /components/ui/customer/tasks/details/AssignedProviderInfo.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text, TouchableOpacity } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import Avatar from '@/components/ui/shared/Avatar';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';
import TextBodySmall from '@/components/ui/shared/TextBodySmall';

interface AssignedProviderInfoProps {
  provider: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    avgRating?: number;
    totalRatingCount?: number;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  acceptedAmount: number;
}

const AssignedProviderInfo = ({ provider, acceptedAmount }: AssignedProviderInfoProps) => {
  return (
    <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 12 }}>
      <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center gap-1">
        <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle" size={14} color="#10B981" />
        <TextBodySmall
          text="Assigned Provider"
          style={{ color: '#10B981', fontWeight: '600' }}
        />
      </View>

      <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
        <Avatar
          width={50}
          height={50}
          source={provider.profile_image ? { uri: provider.profile_image } : undefined}
          verified={provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved}
          verifiedSize={12}
        />
        <View className="flex-1">
          <LabelPrimary text={provider.name} style={{ fontSize: 14 }} numberOfLines={1} />
          <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="star" size={12} color="#F59E0B" />
            <TextBodySmall
              text={`${provider.avgRating?.toFixed(1) || 'New'} (${provider.totalRatingCount || 0} reviews)`}
              style={{ color: '#6B7280', fontSize: 11 }}
            />
          </View>
          <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="cash-outline" size={12} color="#2B7FFF" />
            <TextBodySmall
              text={`Accepted bid: $${acceptedAmount}`}
              style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 11, fontWeight: '600' }}
            />
          </View>
        </View>
      </View>
    </BorderCard>
  );
};

export default AssignedProviderInfo;

Step 4: Update ActionButtons.tsx (customer side)

tsx
const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isCompleted,
  isInProgress,
  isPrivateTask,
  isOpenTask,
  isAssigned,
  hasProvider,
  providerStatus,
  onAcceptCounter,
  onCounterBack,
  onCancelRequest,
  onFindAnother,
  onTrackMap,
  onLeaveRating,
  onViewInvoice,
  isFullyComplete,
  isCancelled,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  const router = useRouter();
  const [cancelModalVisible, setCancelModalVisible] = useState(false);

  const handleCancelSubmit = () => {
    onCancelRequest();
    setCancelModalVisible(false);
  };

  // ✅ Provider marked complete but hasn't truly started (no beforeImages)
  // This means task is IN_PROGRESS but provider hasn't uploaded arrival photos yet
  if (isInProgress && !taskDetails.hasStartedTask) {
    return (
      <BorderCard style={{ padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#FEF3C7', borderRadius: 12 }}>
        <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
          <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={18} color="#D97706" />
          <Text className="flex-1 font-nunitoSemi text-sm text-amber-700">
            Task is in progress. Waiting for provider to arrive and upload before photos.
          </Text>
        </View>
      </BorderCard>
    );
  }

  // ✅ Provider uploaded before photos (truly started) but hasn't marked complete
  if (
    isInProgress &&
    taskDetails.hasStartedTask &&
    !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete
  ) {
    return (
      <View className="gap-[10px]">
        <BorderCard style={{ padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#E8F5E9', borderRadius: 12 }}>
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
            <Ionicons name="construct-outline" size={18} color="#10B981" />
            <Text className="flex-1 font-nunitoSemi text-sm text-green-700">
              Provider has arrived and work is underway.
            </Text>
          </View>
        </BorderCard>
        <OutlineButton
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          title="Track on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
        />
      </View>
    );
  }

  // ✅ Provider marked complete — customer needs to confirm
  if (
    isInProgress &&
    taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete &&
    !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isCustomerMarkedComplete
  ) {
    return (
      <View className="gap-[10px]">
        <BorderCard style={{ padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#E8F5E9', borderRadius: 12 }}>
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
            <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle-outline" size={18} color="#10B981" />
            <Text className="flex-1 font-nunitoSemi text-sm text-green-700">
              Provider has marked this task as complete. Please review the work and confirm.
            </Text>
          </View>
        </BorderCard>
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Review & Confirm Completion"
          onPress={() =>
            router.push(
              `/customer/customer-flow/Task/task-details/${taskDetails.id}/completion`
            )
          }
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
        />
      </View>
    );
  }

  // Fully completed
  if (isFullyComplete) {
    const canLeaveRating = !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isCustomerRated;
    return (
      <View className="gap-[10px]">
        {canLeaveRating && (
          <PrimaryButton
            style={{ flex: 1, borderRadius: 12 }}
            title="Leave a Rating"
            onPress={onLeaveRating}
          />
        )}
        <OutlineButton
          style={{ flex: 1, borderRadius: 12 }}
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
        />
      </View>
    );
  }

  // Completed
  if (isCompleted) {
    const canLeaveRating = !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isCustomerRated;
    return (
      <View className="gap-[10px]">
        {canLeaveRating && (
          <PrimaryButton
            style={{ flex: 1, borderRadius: 12 }}
            title="Leave a Rating"
            onPress={onLeaveRating}
          />
        )}
        <OutlineButton
          style={{ flex: 1, borderRadius: 12 }}
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
        />
      </View>
    );
  }

  // Open task with no provider
  if (isOpenTask && !hasProvider) {
    return (
      <>
        <PrimaryButton
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          title="Cancel Request"
          onPress={() => setCancelModalVisible(true)}
        />
        <CancelRequestModal
          visible={cancelModalVisible}
          onClose={() => setCancelModalVisible(false)}
          onSubmit={handleCancelSubmit}
          taskTitle={taskDetails.serviceType?.replace('-', ' ') || 'this task'}
        />
      </>
    );
  }

  // Assigned
  if (isAssigned) {
    return (
      <>
        <View className="gap-[10px]">
          <PrimaryButton
            leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#FFFFFF" />}
            style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
            title="Track on Map"
            onPress={onTrackMap}
          />
          <PrimaryButton
            gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
            style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
            title="Cancel Request"
            onPress={() => setCancelModalVisible(true)}
          />
        </View>
        <CancelRequestModal
          visible={cancelModalVisible}
          onClose={() => setCancelModalVisible(false)}
          onSubmit={handleCancelSubmit}
          taskTitle={taskDetails.serviceType?.replace('-', ' ') || 'this task'}
          taskStatus={taskDetails.taskStatus}
        />
      </>
    );
  }

  return null;
};

Step 5: Show AssignedProviderInfo in TaskDetailsPage

In your customer TaskDetailsPage, show provider info when task is assigned or in progress. Since the accepted bid has provider details, use the accepted bid:

tsx
// Find the accepted bid to get provider info
const acceptedBid = bids.find(bid => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
                    bids.find(bid => bid.provider); // fallback

// In JSX, after TaskPhotos and before Provider Bids section:
{(isAssigned || isInProgress) && acceptedBid && (
  <AssignedProviderInfo
    provider={acceptedBid.provider}
    acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
  />
)}

Import it at the top:

tsx
import AssignedProviderInfo from '@/components/ui/customer/tasks/details/AssignedProviderInfo';

You said: provide whole updated code and the review and rating logic exsts but will implement later if it is not possible yet.

provide whole updated code and the review and rating logic exsts but will implement later if it is not possible yet.

8 May

Claude responded: Here are all the updated files:

Here are all the updated files:

hooks/tasks/useTaskDetails.ts

ts
import { useState, useEffect, useCallback, useMemo } from 'react';
import { useGetSingleTaskQuery, useDeleteTaskMutation } from '@/store/api/tasksApi';
import { toFrontendFormat } from '@/utils/serviceTypeMapper';
import { ServiceType } from '@/types/services';
import { useAuth } from '@/hooks/useAuth';
import Toast from 'react-native-toast-message';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';

export interface TaskDetailsType {
  id: string;
  serviceType: ServiceType;
  taskStatus: string;
  serviceLocation: string;
  taskValue: number;
  details: string;
  scheduledDate?: string;
  scheduledTime?: string;
  createdAt?: string;
  taskPhotos: string[];
  providerId: string | null;
  counterOffer: number | null;
  acceptedBidAmount: number | null;
  paymentInfo: {
    subtotal: number;
    fees: number;
    total: number;
  };
  completionStatus: {
    isProviderMarkedComplete: boolean;
    isCustomerMarkedComplete: boolean;
    isCustomerRated: boolean;
    rating?: number;
    review?: string;
  };
  beforeImages: string[];
  afterImages: string[];
  hasStartedTask: boolean;
  startedAt?: { date: string; time: string; fullDateTime: string } | null;
  completedAt?: { date: string; time: string; fullDateTime: string } | null;
  cancelledAt?: { date: string; time: string; fullDateTime: string } | null;
}

const mapStatusToTaskStatus = (status: string): string => {
  const statusMap: Record<string, string> = {
    OPEN: 'open',
    PRIVATE: 'private',
    ASSIGNED: 'assigned',
    IN_PROGRESS: 'in-progress',
    COMPLETED: 'completed',
    CANCELLED: 'cancelled',
  };
  return statusMap[status] || status.toLowerCase();
};

const formatDate = (dateString: string | null | undefined) => {
  if (!dateString) return null;
  const date = new Date(dateString);
  if (isNaN(date.getTime())) return null;
  return {
    date: date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { day: '2-digit', month: 'short', year: 'numeric' }),
    time: date.toLocaleTimeString('en-US', { hour: '2-digit', minute: '2-digit' }),
    fullDateTime: date.toLocaleString('en-US', {
      day: '2-digit',
      month: 'short',
      year: 'numeric',
      hour: '2-digit',
      minute: '2-digit',
    }),
  };
};

export const useTaskDetails = (taskId: string | undefined) => {
  const router = useRouter();
  const { user } = useAuth();

  const {
    data: rawTask,
    isLoading,
    error,
    refetch,
  } = useGetSingleTaskQuery(taskId || '', {
    skip: !taskId,
  });

  const [deleteTask, { isLoading: isDeleting }] = useDeleteTaskMutation();

  const userRole = user?.role;
  const isCustomer = userRole === 'customer';
  const isProvider = userRole === 'provider';

  const taskDetails = useMemo((): TaskDetailsType | null => {
    if (!rawTask) return null;

    const taskStatus = mapStatusToTaskStatus(rawTask.status);
    const serviceType = toFrontendFormat(rawTask.serviceType);
    const scheduledDate = formatDate(rawTask.taskStartDateTime);
    const createdAt = formatDate(rawTask.createdAt);
    const cancelledAt = rawTask.cancelledAt ? formatDate(rawTask.cancelledAt) : null;

    const beforeImages = rawTask.beforeImages || [];
    const afterImages = rawTask.afterImages || [];
    const hasStartedTask = beforeImages.length > 0;
    const acceptedBidAmount = rawTask.acceptedBidAmount || null;

    return {
      id: rawTask._id,
      serviceType: serviceType as ServiceType,
      taskStatus,
      serviceLocation: rawTask.address,
      taskValue: rawTask.budget,
      details: rawTask.description,
      scheduledDate: scheduledDate?.date,
      scheduledTime: scheduledDate?.time,
      createdAt: createdAt?.fullDateTime,
      taskPhotos: rawTask.task_attachments || [],
      providerId: rawTask.provider,
      counterOffer: null,
      acceptedBidAmount,
      paymentInfo: {
        subtotal: acceptedBidAmount || rawTask.budget,
        fees: acceptedBidAmount || rawTask.budget,
        total: acceptedBidAmount || rawTask.budget,
      },
      completionStatus: {
        isProviderMarkedComplete: rawTask.isMarkCompletedByProvider || false,
        isCustomerMarkedComplete: !!rawTask.markCompletedByCustomerAt,
        isCustomerRated: false,
      },
      beforeImages,
      afterImages,
      hasStartedTask,
      startedAt: hasStartedTask ? formatDate(rawTask.markCompletedByProviderAt) : null,
      completedAt: rawTask.markCompletedByCustomerAt
        ? formatDate(rawTask.markCompletedByCustomerAt)
        : null,
      cancelledAt,
    };
  }, [rawTask]);

  // Flags
  const isPrivateTask = taskDetails?.taskStatus === 'private';
  const isOpenTask = taskDetails?.taskStatus === 'open';
  const isAssigned = taskDetails?.taskStatus === 'assigned';
  const isInProgress = taskDetails?.taskStatus === 'in-progress';
  const isCompleted = taskDetails?.taskStatus === 'completed';
  const isCancelled = taskDetails?.taskStatus === 'cancelled';
  const hasProvider = !!taskDetails?.providerId;
  const providerStatus = null;
  const isProviderComplete = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete || false;
  const isCustomerComplete = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.isCustomerMarkedComplete || false;
  const isFullyComplete = isCompleted || (isProviderComplete && isCustomerComplete);
  const isRated = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.isCustomerRated || false;
  const rating = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.rating;
  const review = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.review;
  const canLeaveRating = isCustomer && isCompleted && !isRated;

  const cancelRequest = useCallback(async () => {
    if (!taskId) {
      Toast.show({ type: 'error', text1: 'Error', text2: 'Task ID not found' });
      return;
    }
    try {
      const response = await deleteTask(taskId).unwrap();
      if (response.success) {
        Toast.show({
          type: 'success',
          text1: 'Task Cancelled',
          text2: response.message || 'Task has been cancelled successfully',
          visibilityTime: 3000,
        });
        setTimeout(() => router.back(), 1500);
      }
    } catch (error: any) {
      Toast.show({
        type: 'error',
        text1: 'Failed to Cancel',
        text2: error.data?.message || 'Please try again later',
        visibilityTime: 3000,
      });
    }
  }, [taskId, deleteTask, router]);

  const findAnotherProvider = useCallback(() => {
    console.log('Find another provider for task:', taskId);
  }, [taskId]);

  const trackOnMap = useCallback(() => {
    console.log('Track on map for task:', taskId);
  }, [taskId]);

  const viewInvoice = useCallback(() => {
    console.log('View invoice for task:', taskId);
  }, [taskId]);

  const submitRating = useCallback(
    (ratingValue: number, ratingReview: string) => {
      console.log('Submit rating:', ratingValue, ratingReview, 'for task:', taskId);
      // TODO: implement rating API call later
    },
    [taskId]
  );

  const markAsComplete = useCallback(() => {
    console.log('Mark as complete for task:', taskId);
    // TODO: implement later
  }, [taskId]);

  const confirmCompletion = useCallback(() => {
    console.log('Confirm completion for task:', taskId);
    // TODO: implement later
  }, [taskId]);

  const showMarkAsCompleteButton = false; // TODO: implement later
  const showConfirmCompletionButton =
    isInProgress &&
    isProviderComplete &&
    !isCustomerComplete;
  const hasStartedTask = taskDetails?.hasStartedTask || false;

  return {
    taskDetails,
    isLoading: isLoading || isDeleting,
    isError: !!error,
    error: error as string | null,
    refetch,

    userRole,
    isCustomer,
    isProvider,

    cancelRequest,
    findAnotherProvider,
    trackOnMap,
    viewInvoice,
    submitRating,
    markAsComplete,
    confirmCompletion,

    hasProvider,
    providerStatus,
    isProviderComplete,
    isCustomerComplete,
    canLeaveRating,
    isFullyComplete,
    isRated,
    rating,
    review,
    hasStartedTask,
    showMarkAsCompleteButton,
    showConfirmCompletionButton,

    isPrivateTask,
    isOpenTask,
    isAssigned,
    isInProgress,
    isCompleted,
    isCancelled,

    startedAt: taskDetails?.startedAt,
    completedAt: taskDetails?.completedAt,
    cancelledAt: taskDetails?.cancelledAt,
  };
};

components/ui/customer/tasks/details/AssignedProviderInfo.tsx (new file)

tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import Avatar from '@/components/ui/shared/Avatar';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';
import TextBodySmall from '@/components/ui/shared/TextBodySmall';

interface AssignedProviderInfoProps {
  provider: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    avgRating?: number;
    totalRatingCount?: number;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  acceptedAmount: number;
}

const AssignedProviderInfo = ({ provider, acceptedAmount }: AssignedProviderInfoProps) => {
  return (
    <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 12 }}>
      <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center gap-1">
        <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle" size={14} color="#10B981" />
        <TextBodySmall
          text="Assigned Provider"
          style={{ color: '#10B981', fontWeight: '600' }}
        />
      </View>

      <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
        <Avatar
          width={50}
          height={50}
          source={provider.profile_image ? { uri: provider.profile_image } : undefined}
          verified={provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved}
          verifiedSize={12}
        />
        <View className="flex-1">
          <LabelPrimary text={provider.name} style={{ fontSize: 14 }} numberOfLines={1} />
          <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="star" size={12} color="#F59E0B" />
            <TextBodySmall
              text={`${provider.avgRating?.toFixed(1) || 'New'} (${provider.totalRatingCount || 0} reviews)`}
              style={{ color: '#6B7280', fontSize: 11 }}
            />
          </View>
          <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="cash-outline" size={12} color="#2B7FFF" />
            <TextBodySmall
              text={`Accepted bid: $${acceptedAmount}`}
              style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 11, fontWeight: '600' }}
            />
          </View>
        </View>
      </View>
    </BorderCard>
  );
};

export default AssignedProviderInfo;

/components/ui/customer/tasks/details/ActionButtons.tsx

tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import OutlineButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/OutlineButton';
import PrimaryButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import CancelRequestModal from '@/components/ui/modals/CancelRequestModal';
import { LocationIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import { TaskDetailsType } from '@/hooks/tasks/useTaskDetails';

interface ActionButtonsProps {
  taskDetails: TaskDetailsType;
  isCompleted: boolean;
  isInProgress: boolean;
  isPrivateTask: boolean;
  isOpenTask: boolean;
  isAssigned: boolean;
  hasProvider: boolean;
  providerStatus: string | null;
  onAcceptCounter: () => void;
  onCounterBack: (amount: number) => void;
  onCancelRequest: () => void;
  onFindAnother: () => void;
  onTrackMap: () => void;
  onLeaveRating: () => void;
  onViewInvoice: () => void;
  isFullyComplete: boolean;
  isCancelled: boolean;
}

const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isCompleted,
  isInProgress,
  isPrivateTask,
  isOpenTask,
  isAssigned,
  hasProvider,
  onCancelRequest,
  onTrackMap,
  onLeaveRating,
  onViewInvoice,
  isFullyComplete,
  isCancelled,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  const router = useRouter();
  const [cancelModalVisible, setCancelModalVisible] = useState(false);

  const handleCancelSubmit = () => {
    onCancelRequest();
    setCancelModalVisible(false);
  };

  // ─── IN PROGRESS states ───────────────────────────────────────────

  // IN_PROGRESS: provider hasn't uploaded before photos yet (not truly started)
  if (isInProgress && !taskDetails.hasStartedTask) {
    return (
      <BorderCard style={{ padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#FEF3C7', borderRadius: 12 }}>
        <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
          <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={18} color="#D97706" />
          <Text className="flex-1 font-nunitoSemi text-sm text-amber-700">
            Task is assigned. Waiting for provider to arrive and start the work.
          </Text>
        </View>
      </BorderCard>
    );
  }

  // IN_PROGRESS: provider uploaded before photos (truly started) but hasn't marked complete
  if (
    isInProgress &&
    taskDetails.hasStartedTask &&
    !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete
  ) {
    return (
      <View className="gap-[10px]">
        <BorderCard style={{ padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#E8F5E9', borderRadius: 12 }}>
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
            <Ionicons name="construct-outline" size={18} color="#10B981" />
            <Text className="flex-1 font-nunitoSemi text-sm text-green-700">
              Provider has arrived and work is underway.
            </Text>
          </View>
        </BorderCard>
        <OutlineButton
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          title="Track on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
        />
      </View>
    );
  }

  // IN_PROGRESS: provider marked complete — customer needs to confirm
  if (
    isInProgress &&
    taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete &&
    !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isCustomerMarkedComplete
  ) {
    return (
      <View className="gap-[10px]">
        <BorderCard style={{ padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#E8F5E9', borderRadius: 12 }}>
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
            <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle-outline" size={18} color="#10B981" />
            <Text className="flex-1 font-nunitoSemi text-sm text-green-700">
              Provider has marked this task as complete. Please review the work and confirm.
            </Text>
          </View>
        </BorderCard>
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Review & Confirm Completion"
          onPress={() =>
            router.push(
              `/customer/customer-flow/Task/task-details/${taskDetails.id}/completion`
            )
          }
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
        />
      </View>
    );
  }

  // ─── COMPLETED / FULLY COMPLETE ───────────────────────────────────

  if (isFullyComplete || isCompleted) {
    const canLeaveRating = !taskDetails.completionStatus?.isCustomerRated;
    return (
      <View className="gap-[10px]">
        {canLeaveRating && (
          <PrimaryButton
            style={{ flex: 1, borderRadius: 12 }}
            title="Leave a Rating"
            onPress={onLeaveRating}
          />
        )}
        <OutlineButton
          style={{ flex: 1, borderRadius: 12 }}
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
        />
      </View>
    );
  }

  // ─── OPEN task with no provider ───────────────────────────────────

  if (isOpenTask && !hasProvider) {
    return (
      <>
        <PrimaryButton
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          title="Cancel Request"
          onPress={() => setCancelModalVisible(true)}
        />
        <CancelRequestModal
          visible={cancelModalVisible}
          onClose={() => setCancelModalVisible(false)}
          onSubmit={handleCancelSubmit}
          taskTitle={taskDetails.serviceType?.replace('-', ' ') || 'this task'}
        />
      </>
    );
  }

  // ─── ASSIGNED ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  if (isAssigned) {
    return (
      <>
        <View className="gap-[10px]">
          <PrimaryButton
            leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#FFFFFF" />}
            style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
            title="Track on Map"
            onPress={onTrackMap}
          />
          <PrimaryButton
            gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
            style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
            title="Cancel Request"
            onPress={() => setCancelModalVisible(true)}
          />
        </View>
        <CancelRequestModal
          visible={cancelModalVisible}
          onClose={() => setCancelModalVisible(false)}
          onSubmit={handleCancelSubmit}
          taskTitle={taskDetails.serviceType?.replace('-', ' ') || 'this task'}
          taskStatus={taskDetails.taskStatus}
        />
      </>
    );
  }

  return null;
};

export default ActionButtons;

TaskResult type update in tasksApi.ts

  afterImages?: string[];        // ← add
  acceptedBidAmount?: number | null;  // ← add
  paymentStatus?: string;        // ← add
}

              (() => {

                if (!acceptedBid) return null;

the backend dev not updated the bid status the reason why it's not being able to check properly. 
what can we do as that needs to be fixed?
8 May

Claude responded: Since the bid status isn't being updated to ACCEPTED by the backend, we can use alternative logic to find the assigned provider.

Since the bid status isn't being updated to ACCEPTED by the backend, we can use alternative logic to find the assigned provider. Looking at your API response, when a task is ASSIGNED or IN_PROGRESS, the task itself has a provider field with the provider's ID.

So we can match the provider ID from the task against the bids:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress) &&
  (() => {
    // Try accepted status first (for when backend fixes it)
    const acceptedBid =
      bids.find((bid) => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
      // Fallback: match provider ID from task against bids
      bids.find((bid) => bid.provider._id === taskDetails.providerId) ||
      // Last resort: just take the first bid if only one exists
      (bids.length === 1 ? bids[0] : null);

    if (!acceptedBid) return null;

    return (
      <AssignedProviderInfo
        provider={acceptedBid.provider}
        acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
      />
    );
  })()}

The logic cascades through three fallbacks:

1. bid.status === 'ACCEPTED'     → works when backend fixes it
      ↓ not found
2. bid.provider._id === task.provider  → matches using task's provider ID field
      ↓ not found  
3. only one bid exists → just use it
      ↓ not found
4. show nothing

This works because your task API response already returns provider: "69face1cd08c1af0793d2712" (the provider's ID) when a task is assigned. We just match that ID against the bids list to find the right provider's details. Once your backend dev fixes the bid status, option 1 will kick in automatically and the fallbacks become irrelevant.

You said: but still the assigned provider info card not showing here

but still the assigned provider info card not showing here

8 May

Claude responded: The issue is useTaskBids filters out bids by current user ID, and the allBids isn't being used.

The issue is useTaskBids filters out bids by current user ID, and the allBids isn't being used. Also hasProvider is being passed as hardcoded false. Let me fix both.

Problem 1: hasProvider is hardcoded false

In your JSX:

tsx
<ActionButtons
  hasProvider={false}  // ← this is always false, should use the hook value

Fix:

tsx
<ActionButtons
  hasProvider={hasProvider}  // ← use from useTaskDetails hook

Problem 2: Bids are being filtered — use allBids instead

useTaskBids returns bids (filtered) and allBids (unfiltered). For finding the assigned provider, use allBids:

tsx
// Change this in TaskDetailsPage:
const { bids, allBids, isLoading: isLoadingBids, refetch: refetchBids, hasBids } = useTaskBids(taskId);

Then update the assigned provider block to use allBids:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress) &&
  (() => {
    const acceptedBid =
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.provider._id === taskDetails.providerId) ||
      (allBids.length === 1 ? allBids[0] : null);

    if (!acceptedBid) return null;

    return (
      <AssignedProviderInfo
        provider={acceptedBid.provider}
        acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
      />
    );
  })()}

Problem 3: Add console log to debug

Add this temporarily right before the assigned provider block to confirm what data you have:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress) && console.log('🔍 Provider Debug:', {
  taskProviderId: taskDetails.providerId,
  allBidsCount: allBids.length,
  allBids: allBids.map(b => ({ id: b._id, providerId: b.provider._id, status: b.status })),
})}

This will tell you exactly what's available so we can confirm the matching is working correctly.

import React from 'react';
import { View } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import Avatar from '@/components/ui/shared/Avatar';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';
import TextBodySmall from '@/components/ui/shared/TextBodySmall';

interface AssignedProviderInfoProps {
  provider: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    avgRating?: number;
    totalRatingCount?: number;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  acceptedAmount: number;
}

const AssignedProviderInfo = ({ provider, acceptedAmount }: AssignedProviderInfoProps) => {
  return (
    <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 12 }}>
      <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center gap-1">
        <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle" size={14} color="#10B981" />
        <TextBodySmall text="Assigned Provider" style={{ color: '#10B981', fontWeight: '600' }} />
      </View>

      <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
        <Avatar
          width={50}
          height={50}
          source={provider.profile_image ? { uri: provider.profile_image } : undefined}
          verified={provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved}
          verifiedSize={12}
        />
        <View className="flex-1">
          <LabelPrimary text={provider.name} style={{ fontSize: 14 }} numberOfLines={1} />
          <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="star" size={12} color="#F59E0B" />
            <TextBodySmall
              text={`${provider.avgRating?.toFixed(1) || 'New'} (${provider.totalRatingCount || 0} reviews)`}
              style={{ color: '#6B7280', fontSize: 11 }}
            />
          </View>
          <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="cash-outline" size={12} color="#2B7FFF" />
            <TextBodySmall
              text={`Accepted bid: $${acceptedAmount}`}
              style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 11, fontWeight: '600' }}
            />
          </View>
        </View>
      </View>
    </BorderCard>
  );
};

export default AssignedProviderInfo;


can we make it clickable? 
so the customer can see the provider's profile? 


on press it will go into provider profile page. 
// hooks/useProviderProfileView.ts
import { useGetProviderByIdQuery } from '@/store/api/providerApi';

export const useProviderProfileView = (providerId: string) => {
  const {
    data: profile,
    isLoading,
    error,
    refetch,
  } = useGetProviderByIdQuery(providerId, {
    skip: !providerId,
  });

  return {
    profile,
    isLoading,
    isError: !!error,
    refetch,
  };
};

// store/api/providerApi.ts
import { baseApi } from './baseApi';
import { Provider } from '@/types/provider';
import { toFrontendFormat } from '@/utils/serviceTypeMapper';

export interface SingleProviderResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: {
    _id: string;
    user: {
      _id: string;
      isBlocked: boolean;
      isActive: boolean;
    };
    name: string;
    phone: string;
    email: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved: boolean;
    serviceTypes: string[];
    isStripeConnected: boolean;
    createdAt: string;
    updatedAt: string;
    address: string;
    dateOfBirth: string;
    profile_image: string;
  };
}

export const providerApi = baseApi.injectEndpoints({
  endpoints: (builder) => ({
    getProviderById: builder.query<Provider, string>({
      query: (id) => `/provider/get-single/${id}`,
      transformResponse: (response: SingleProviderResponse) => {
        if (response.success && response.data) {
          const data = response.data;
          return {
            id: data._id,
            fullName: data.name,
            avatarUrl: data.profile_image || '',
            email: data.email,
            phoneNumber: data.phone,
            servicesOffered: data.serviceTypes.map(toFrontendFormat),
            rating: 0, // Not available in this response
            totalReviews: 0, // Not available in this response
            isVerified: data.isIdentificationDocumentApproved,
            blocked: data.user?.isBlocked || false,
            completedJobs: 0, // Not available in this response
            bio: '', // Not available in this response
            createdAt: data.createdAt,
            updatedAt: data.updatedAt,
            stripeConnected: data.isStripeConnected || false,
            address: data.address,
          } as Provider;
        }
        throw new Error('Provider not found');
      },
      providesTags: ['Provider'],
    }),
  }),
});

export const { useGetProviderByIdQuery } = providerApi;
8 May

Claude responded: Just add TouchableOpacity and useRouter to make the card clickable:

Just add TouchableOpacity and useRouter to make the card clickable:

tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, TouchableOpacity } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import Avatar from '@/components/ui/shared/Avatar';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';
import TextBodySmall from '@/components/ui/shared/TextBodySmall';

interface AssignedProviderInfoProps {
  provider: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    avgRating?: number;
    totalRatingCount?: number;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  acceptedAmount: number;
}

const AssignedProviderInfo = ({ provider, acceptedAmount }: AssignedProviderInfoProps) => {
  const router = useRouter();

  const handlePress = () => {
    router.push({
      pathname: '/customer/customer-flow/provider/[id]',
      params: { id: provider._id },
    });
  };

  return (
    <TouchableOpacity activeOpacity={0.7} onPress={handlePress}>
      <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 12 }}>
        <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center justify-between">
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle" size={14} color="#10B981" />
            <TextBodySmall
              text="Assigned Provider"
              style={{ color: '#10B981', fontWeight: '600' }}
            />
          </View>
          <Ionicons name="chevron-forward" size={16} color="#9CA3AF" />
        </View>

        <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
          <Avatar
            width={50}
            height={50}
            source={provider.profile_image ? { uri: provider.profile_image } : undefined}
            verified={provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved}
            verifiedSize={12}
          />
          <View className="flex-1">
            <LabelPrimary text={provider.name} style={{ fontSize: 14 }} numberOfLines={1} />
            <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
              <Ionicons name="star" size={12} color="#F59E0B" />
              <TextBodySmall
                text={`${provider.avgRating?.toFixed(1) || 'New'} (${provider.totalRatingCount || 0} reviews)`}
                style={{ color: '#6B7280', fontSize: 11 }}
              />
            </View>
            <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
              <Ionicons name="cash-outline" size={12} color="#2B7FFF" />
              <TextBodySmall
                text={`Accepted bid: $${acceptedAmount}`}
                style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 11, fontWeight: '600' }}
              />
            </View>
          </View>
        </View>
      </BorderCard>
    </TouchableOpacity>
  );
};

export default AssignedProviderInfo;

The route /customer/customer-flow/provider/[id] matches your existing ProviderProfileView page exactly, so it will open the full provider profile with all the details, call, message, and private task request buttons already working.

<TouchableOpacity activeOpacity={0.7} onPress={handlePress}>
      <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 12 }}>
        {/* <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center justify-between">
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-1">
            <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle" size={14} color="#10B981" />
            <TextBodySmall

              style={{ color: '#10B981', fontWeight: '600' }}
            />
          </View>
          <Ionicons name="chevron-forward" size={16} color="#9CA3AF" />
        </View> */}

          text="Assigned Provider"

        />

              width={50}
              height={50}
              source={provider.profile_image ? { uri: provider.profile_image } : undefined}
              verified={provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved}
              verifiedSize={12}
            />

                  style={{ color: '#6B7280', fontSize: 11 }}
                />
              </View>

                <TextBodySmall
                  text={`Accepted bid: $${acceptedAmount}`}
                  style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 11, fontWeight: '600' }}

        </View>
      </BorderCard>

do it properly 
I need that right corner arrow vertically center of the after assigned provider content
8 May

Claude responded: The key fix is the provider row uses flex-row items-center on the outer View, which vertically centers all three children — avatar, info block, and chevron — r…

tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, TouchableOpacity } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import Avatar from '@/components/ui/shared/Avatar';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';
import TextBodySmall from '@/components/ui/shared/TextBodySmall';

interface AssignedProviderInfoProps {
  provider: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    avgRating?: number;
    totalRatingCount?: number;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  acceptedAmount: number;
}

const AssignedProviderInfo = ({ provider, acceptedAmount }: AssignedProviderInfoProps) => {
  const router = useRouter();

  const handlePress = () => {
    router.push({
      pathname: '/customer/customer-flow/provider/[id]',
      params: { id: provider._id },
    });
  };

  return (
    <TouchableOpacity activeOpacity={0.7} onPress={handlePress}>
      <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 12, gap: 8 }}>
        {/* Label */}
        <View className="flex-row items-center gap-1">
          <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle" size={13} color="#10B981" />
          <TextBodySmall
            text="Assigned Provider"
            style={{ color: '#10B981', fontWeight: '600' }}
          />
        </View>

        {/* Provider Row */}
        <View className="flex-row items-center">
          {/* Avatar */}
          <Avatar
            width={50}
            height={50}
            source={provider.profile_image ? { uri: provider.profile_image } : undefined}
            verified={provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved}
            verifiedSize={12}
          />

          {/* Info */}
          <View className="mx-3 flex-1">
            <LabelPrimary text={provider.name} style={{ fontSize: 14 }} numberOfLines={1} />
            <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
              <Ionicons name="star" size={12} color="#F59E0B" />
              <TextBodySmall
                text={`${provider.avgRating?.toFixed(1) || 'New'} (${provider.totalRatingCount || 0} reviews)`}
                style={{ color: '#6B7280', fontSize: 11 }}
              />
            </View>
            <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center gap-1">
              <Ionicons name="cash-outline" size={12} color="#2B7FFF" />
              <TextBodySmall
                text={`Accepted bid: $${acceptedAmount}`}
                style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 11, fontWeight: '600' }}
              />
            </View>
          </View>

          {/* Chevron — vertically centered by parent items-center */}
          <Ionicons name="chevron-forward" size={18} color="#9CA3AF" />
        </View>
      </BorderCard>
    </TouchableOpacity>
  );
};

export default AssignedProviderInfo;

The key fix is the provider row uses flex-row items-center on the outer View, which vertically centers all three children — avatar, info block, and chevron — relative to each other. The mx-3 on the info block gives breathing room on both sides.

{(isAssigned || isInProgress) &&
              (() => {
                const acceptedBid =
                  allBids.find((bid) => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
                  allBids.find((bid) => bid.provider._id === taskDetails.providerId) ||
                  (allBids.length === 1 ? allBids[0] : null);

                if (!acceptedBid) return null;

                {
                  (isAssigned || isInProgress) &&
                    console.log('🔍 Provider Debug:', {
                      taskProviderId: taskDetails.providerId,
                      allBidsCount: allBids.length,
                      allBids: allBids.map((b) => ({
                        id: b._id,
                        providerId: b.provider._id,
                        status: b.status,
                      })),
                    });
                }

                return (
                  <AssignedProviderInfo
                    provider={acceptedBid.provider}
                    acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
                  />
                );
              })()}

this assigned provider info card only showing in the assigned but not in progress 
but shouldn't we show it in in progress, complete as well?
8 May

Claude responded: Yes, you should show it in isAssigned, isInProgress, and isCompleted.

Yes, you should show it in isAssigned, isInProgress, and isCompleted. Update the condition:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress || isCompleted) &&
  (() => {
    const acceptedBid =
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.provider._id === taskDetails.providerId) ||
      (allBids.length === 1 ? allBids[0] : null);

    if (!acceptedBid) return null;

    return (
      <AssignedProviderInfo
        provider={acceptedBid.provider}
        acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
      />
    );
  })()}

But the reason it's not showing in isInProgress even though you had the condition is likely because allBids is empty for in-progress tasks. This happens because useTaskBids calls useGetTaskBidsQuery which might return an empty array once the task moves past OPEN status — the backend may stop returning bids for non-open tasks.

Add this log temporarily to confirm:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress || isCompleted) &&
  console.log('🔍 Bid check:', {
    isAssigned,
    isInProgress,
    isCompleted,
    allBidsLength: allBids.length,
    providerId: taskDetails.providerId,
  })}

If allBids is empty for in-progress tasks, we need a different approach — store the provider info directly from taskDetails instead of relying on bids. In that case, tell me and I'll adjust AssignedProviderInfo to accept a simpler fallback using just the providerId to fetch provider details directly.

You said: LOG 🔍 Bid check: {"allBidsLength": 0, "isAssigned": false, "isCompleted": false, "isInProgress": true, "providerId": {"id": "69face1cd08c1af0793d2712", "emai…

LOG 🔍 Bid check: {"allBidsLength": 0, "isAssigned": false, "isCompleted": false, "isInProgress": true, "providerId": {"_id": "69face1cd08c1af0793d2712", "email": "provider_monir@yopmail.com", "isIdentificationDocumentApproved": false, "name": "provider_monir", "profile_image": ""}} LOG 🔍 Bid check: {"allBidsLength": 0, "isAssigned": false, "isCompleted": false, "isInProgress": true, "providerId": {"_id": "69face1cd08c1af0793d2712", "email": "provider_monir@yopmail.com", "isIdentificationDocumentApproved": false, "name": "provider_monir", "profile_image": ""}}

still not showing in in progress but showing in the assigned. 

// /components/ui/provider/tasks/details/ActionButtons.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View } from 'react-native';
import PrimaryButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import OutlineButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/OutlineButton';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { LocationIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import { ProviderTaskDetailsType } from '@/hooks/providerSide/useProviderTaskDetails';

interface ActionButtonsProps {
  taskDetails: ProviderTaskDetailsType;
  isOpenTask: boolean;
  isPrivateTask: boolean;
  isAssigned: boolean;
  isInProgress: boolean;
  isCompleted: boolean;
  canAccept: boolean;
  canStart: boolean;
  canComplete: boolean;
  canCancel: boolean;
  canCounter: boolean;
  onAccept?: () => void; // Made optional since provider might have already bid
  onCounter: () => void;
  onStart: () => void;
  onComplete: () => void;
  onCancel: () => void;
  onTrackMap: () => void;
  onViewInvoice: () => void;
  isCancelled: boolean;
  isAccepting?: boolean;
  isCountering?: boolean;
}

const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isOpenTask,
  isPrivateTask,
  isAssigned,
  isInProgress,
  isCompleted,
  canAccept,
  canStart,
  canComplete,
  canCancel,
  canCounter,
  onAccept,
  onCounter,
  onStart,
  onComplete,
  onCancel,
  onTrackMap,
  onViewInvoice,
  isCancelled,
  isAccepting = false,
  isCountering = false,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  return (
    <View className="gap-2.5">
      {/* Accept Task Button - Only show if canAccept is true AND onAccept exists */}
      {canAccept && onAccept && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title={isAccepting ? 'Accepting...' : 'Accept Task'}
          onPress={onAccept}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          disabled={isAccepting}
        />
      )}

      {/* Counter Offer Button - Only show if canCounter is true */}
      {canCounter && (
        <OutlineButton
          title={isCountering ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Counter Offer'}
          onPress={onCounter}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          bgColor="#FFFFFF"
          disabled={isCountering}
        />
      )}

      {/* Start Button */}
      {canStart && (
        <PrimaryButton title="Start Task" onPress={onStart} style={{ borderRadius: 12 }} />
      )}

      {/* Complete Button */}
      {canComplete && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Mark as Complete"
          onPress={onComplete}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12, backgroundColor: '#10B981' }}
          rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View on Map Button */}
      {(isAssigned || isInProgress || isOpenTask || isPrivateTask) && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View Invoice Button */}
      {isCompleted && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<Ionicons name="document-text-outline" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* Cancel Button */}
      {canCancel && !isCancelled && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Cancel Task"
          onPress={onCancel}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
        />
      )}
    </View>
  );
};

export default ActionButtons;
8 May

Claude responded: Two things from the log:

Two things from the log:

  1. allBidsLength: 0 — backend returns no bids for in-progress tasks
  2. providerId is actually a populated object not just an ID string — the backend is returning the full provider object

So we can use taskDetails.providerId directly as the provider object. Update useTaskDetails.ts and the page.

Fix useTaskDetails.ts

Update the TaskDetailsType interface and the mapping:

ts
export interface TaskDetailsType {
  // ... existing fields
  providerId: string | null;
  // ← add this:
  providerInfo: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    email: string;
    profile_image: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved: boolean;
  } | null;
  // ... rest of fields
}

In the taskDetails useMemo, update the provider mapping:

ts
// Replace:
providerId: rawTask.provider,

// With:
providerId: typeof rawTask.provider === 'object' ? rawTask.provider?._id : rawTask.provider,
providerInfo: typeof rawTask.provider === 'object' ? {
  _id: rawTask.provider._id,
  name: rawTask.provider.name,
  email: rawTask.provider.email,
  profile_image: rawTask.provider.profile_image || '',
  isIdentificationDocumentApproved: rawTask.provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved || false,
} : null,

Also update TaskResult in tasksApi.ts to reflect the populated provider:

ts
export interface TaskResult {
  // ... existing fields
  provider: string | {           // ← can be ID string or populated object
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    email: string;
    profile_image: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved: boolean;
  } | null;
  // ... rest
}

Fix TaskDetailsPage — use providerInfo directly

Replace the assigned provider block:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress || isCompleted) && (() => {
  // First try from bids (when available)
  const bidProvider =
    allBids.find((bid) => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
    allBids.find((bid) => bid.provider._id === taskDetails.providerId) ||
    (allBids.length === 1 ? allBids[0] : null);

  // Fallback: use populated provider object from task itself
  const providerData = bidProvider?.provider || taskDetails.providerInfo;

  if (!providerData) return null;

  return (
    <AssignedProviderInfo
      provider={providerData}
      acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
    />
  );
})()}

This works because when allBids is empty (in-progress/completed), it falls back to taskDetails.providerInfo which is already populated by the backend as you can see in the log — the provider object has _id, name, email, profile_image, and isIdentificationDocumentApproved all ready to use.

You said: // Replace:

// Replace: providerId: rawTask.provider, // With: providerId: typeof rawTask.provider === 'object' ? rawTask.provider?._id : rawTask.provider, providerInfo: typeof rawTask.provider === 'object' ? { _id: rawTask.provider._id, name: rawTask.provider.name, email: rawTask.provider.email, profile_image: rawTask.provider.profile_image || '', isIdentificationDocumentApproved: rawTask.provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved || false, } : null,

where to do this?

8 May

Claude responded: In hooks/tasks/useTaskDetails.

In hooks/tasks/useTaskDetails.ts, inside the taskDetails useMemo, find this section and replace it:

ts
// FIND this:
return {
  id: rawTask._id,
  serviceType: serviceType as ServiceType,
  taskStatus,
  serviceLocation: rawTask.address,
  taskValue: rawTask.budget,
  details: rawTask.description,
  scheduledDate: scheduledDate?.date,
  scheduledTime: scheduledDate?.time,
  createdAt: createdAt?.fullDateTime,
  taskPhotos: rawTask.task_attachments || [],
  providerId: rawTask.provider,        // ← this line
  counterOffer: null,
  acceptedBidAmount,
  // ... rest
};

// REPLACE with:
return {
  id: rawTask._id,
  serviceType: serviceType as ServiceType,
  taskStatus,
  serviceLocation: rawTask.address,
  taskValue: rawTask.budget,
  details: rawTask.description,
  scheduledDate: scheduledDate?.date,
  scheduledTime: scheduledDate?.time,
  createdAt: createdAt?.fullDateTime,
  taskPhotos: rawTask.task_attachments || [],
  providerId: typeof rawTask.provider === 'object'   // ← replace these two lines
    ? rawTask.provider?._id ?? null
    : rawTask.provider ?? null,
  providerInfo: typeof rawTask.provider === 'object' && rawTask.provider !== null
    ? {
        _id: rawTask.provider._id,
        name: rawTask.provider.name,
        email: rawTask.provider.email,
        profile_image: rawTask.provider.profile_image || '',
        isIdentificationDocumentApproved: rawTask.provider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved || false,
      }
    : null,
  counterOffer: null,
  acceptedBidAmount,
  // ... rest stays the same
};

You said: Property '_id' does not exist on type 'never'.

Property '_id' does not exist on type 'never'. Property 'name' does not exist on type 'never'. Property 'profile_image' does not exist on type 'never'. Property 'isIdentificationDocumentApproved' does not exist on type 'never'.

showing :

8 May

Claude responded: The issue is TypeScript doesn't know the shape of rawTask.

The issue is TypeScript doesn't know the shape of rawTask.provider. Fix it by updating TaskResult in tasksApi.ts first, then the hook will work.

Step 1: Update TaskResult in store/api/tasksApi.ts

ts
export interface TaskResult {
  _id: string;
  title: string;
  serviceType: string;
  budget: number;
  taskStartDateTime: string;
  status: string;
  address: string;
  location: {
    type: string;
    coordinates: [number, number];
  };
  description: string;
  task_attachments: string[];
  provider: string | {                              // ← update this
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    email: string;
    profile_image: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved: boolean;
  } | null;
  customer: {
    _id: string;
    name: string;
    profile_image: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved?: boolean;
  };
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
  isMarkCompletedByProvider?: boolean;
  markCompletedByProviderAt?: string | null;
  markCompletedByCustomerAt?: string | null;
  cancelledAt?: string | null;
  totalOffer?: number;
  beforeImages?: string[];
  afterImages?: string[];
  acceptedBidAmount?: number | null;
  paymentStatus?: string;
}

Step 2: Fix the useMemo in useTaskDetails.ts

Now TypeScript knows the type, so use a proper type guard:

ts
// Replace the providerId and providerInfo lines with:

const rawProvider = rawTask.provider;
const isProviderObject = rawProvider !== null &&
  typeof rawProvider === 'object' &&
  '_id' in rawProvider;

return {
  // ... other fields
  providerId: isProviderObject
    ? (rawProvider as { _id: string }).._id
    : (rawProvider as string | null),
  providerInfo: isProviderObject
    ? {
        _id: (rawProvider as any)._id,
        name: (rawProvider as any).name,
        email: (rawProvider as any).email,
        profile_image: (rawProvider as any).profile_image || '',
        isIdentificationDocumentApproved:
          (rawProvider as any).isIdentificationDocumentApproved || false,
      }
    : null,
  // ... rest of fields
};

Wait — the cleaner way once TaskResult is updated is:

ts
const rawProvider = rawTask.provider;

const providerId =
  rawProvider === null
    ? null
    : typeof rawProvider === 'string'
    ? rawProvider
    : rawProvider._id;

const providerInfo =
  rawProvider !== null && typeof rawProvider === 'object'
    ? {
        _id: rawProvider._id,
        name: rawProvider.name,
        email: rawProvider.email,
        profile_image: rawProvider.profile_image || '',
        isIdentificationDocumentApproved: rawProvider.isIdentificationDocumentApproved || false,
      }
    : null;

return {
  // ... other fields
  providerId,
  providerInfo,
  // ... rest
};

Once TaskResult has the correct union type, TypeScript will narrow it properly and all the _id, name, etc. errors will disappear.

{
                "_id": "69facef6d08c1af0793d2743",
                "title": "lawn mowing task",
                "serviceType": "LAWN_MOWING",
                "budget": 944,
                "taskStartDateTime": "2026-05-06T05:17:43.823Z",
                "acceptedBidAmount": null,
                "bidAcceptAt": null,
                "customerPayingAmount": null,
                "providerEarningAmount": null,
                "status": "IN_PROGRESS",
                "isDeleted": false,
                "paymentStatus": "UNPAID",
                "provider": "69face1cd08c1af0793d2712",
                "customer": {
                    "_id": "69e88f25360e2e91db1ad8ce",
                    "name": "Customer",
                    "profile_image": "file:///data/user/0/com.snowout/cache/ImagePicker/f538c0c8-e8c2-4d08-b2f4-35bd1c63e483.jpeg"
                },
                "location": {
                    "type": "Point",
                    "coordinates": [
                        90.4138667,
                        23.9953697
                    ]
                },
                "address": "Joydebpur Rd, Gazipur, Bangladesh",
                "description": "Nxmzkxhd",
                "task_attachments": [
                    "file:///data/user/0/com.snowout/cache/ImagePicker/fb6ac79f-401e-447b-a82b-4e004011aa39.jpeg"
                ],
                "afterImages": [],
                "beforeImages": [],
                "isMarkCompletedByProvider": false,
                "markCompletedByProviderAt": null,
                "markCompletedByCustomerAt": null,
                "cancelledAt": null,
                "transactionId": null,
                "createdAt": "2026-05-06T05:17:42.005Z",
                "updatedAt": "2026-05-06T05:34:51.152Z",
                "__v": 0,
                "totalOffer": 0
            },

in case of this data why the assigned provider card is not showing? 
but shown for another one?
8 May

Claude responded: Looking at the data, for this task provider is just a string ID "69face1cd08c1af0793d2712" — not a populated object.

Looking at the data, for this task provider is just a string ID "69face1cd08c1af0793d2712" — not a populated object. But for the other task it was a populated object. So the backend is inconsistent — sometimes it populates the provider, sometimes it doesn't.

The current code in TaskDetailsPage still relies on allBids to find the provider, and allBids is empty for in-progress tasks. The providerInfo fallback isn't being used in the page yet.

Fix in TaskDetailsPage

Update the assigned provider block to use taskDetails.providerInfo as fallback, and also handle the case where provider is just an ID string by fetching it:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress || isCompleted || isCancelled) &&
  (() => {
    // Try bids first (available for assigned tasks)
    const bidProvider =
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.provider._id === taskDetails.providerId) ||
      (allBids.length === 1 ? allBids[0] : null);

    // Use bid provider data if available, otherwise fall back to providerInfo from task
    const providerData = bidProvider?.provider ?? taskDetails.providerInfo;

    if (!providerData) return null;

    return (
      <AssignedProviderInfo
        provider={providerData}
        acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
      />
    );
  })()}

For the lawn mowing task, provider is a plain string ID so providerInfo will be null and allBids is empty — so nothing shows. The only fix for that case is to either:

Option A — Ask backend to always populate the provider object (recommended, one line change on their end).

Option B — Fetch provider by ID when providerInfo is null but providerId exists. Add this near the top of TaskDetailsPage:

tsx
import { useGetProviderByIdQuery } from '@/store/api/providerApi';

// Inside TaskDetailsPage, after useTaskDetails:
const shouldFetchProvider =
  (isAssigned || isInProgress || isCompleted || isCancelled) &&
  !!taskDetails?.providerId &&
  !taskDetails?.providerInfo;

const { data: fetchedProvider } = useGetProviderByIdQuery(
  taskDetails?.providerId || '',
  { skip: !shouldFetchProvider }
);

Then in the assigned provider block:

tsx
{(isAssigned || isInProgress || isCompleted || isCancelled) &&
  (() => {
    const bidProvider =
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.status?.toUpperCase() === 'ACCEPTED') ||
      allBids.find((bid) => bid.provider._id === taskDetails.providerId) ||
      (allBids.length === 1 ? allBids[0] : null);

    // Build provider data from all possible sources
    const providerData = bidProvider?.provider ??
      taskDetails.providerInfo ??
      (fetchedProvider ? {
        _id: fetchedProvider.id,
        name: fetchedProvider.fullName,
        email: fetchedProvider.email,
        profile_image: fetchedProvider.avatarUrl || '',
        isIdentificationDocumentApproved: fetchedProvider.isVerified || false,
      } : null);

    if (!providerData) return null;

    return (
      <AssignedProviderInfo
        provider={providerData}
        acceptedAmount={taskDetails.acceptedBidAmount || taskDetails.taskValue}
      />
    );
  })()}

Option A is better — just ask your backend dev to always populate the provider field in the task response. It's a one-word change on their end (populate('provider')). Option B adds an extra API call just to compensate for inconsistent backend behavior.

8 May

Claude responded: Update useCalendarTasks.

Update useCalendarTasks.ts to use the real API instead of mock data:

ts
// /hooks/providerSide/useCalendarTasks.ts
import { useState, useMemo, useCallback } from 'react';
import { useGetMyTasksQuery } from '@/store/api/tasksApi';
import { transformToTaskItem } from '@/store/api/tasksApi';
import { TaskItem } from '@/types/Tasks';

export interface CalendarDay {
  date: string;
  tasks: TaskItem[];
}

const groupTasksByDate = (tasks: TaskItem[]): CalendarDay[] => {
  const grouped: Record<string, TaskItem[]> = {};

  tasks.forEach((task) => {
    if (!task.scheduledDate) return;
    const date = task.scheduledDate; // already 'YYYY-MM-DD' from transformToTaskItem
    if (!grouped[date]) grouped[date] = [];
    grouped[date].push(task);
  });

  return Object.entries(grouped)
    .map(([date, tasks]) => ({ date, tasks }))
    .sort((a, b) => a.date.localeCompare(b.date));
};

export const useCalendarTasks = () => {
  const [selectedDate, setSelectedDate] = useState<string>(
    new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]
  );

  // Fetch assigned tasks from real API
  const {
    data,
    isLoading,
    isError,
    refetch,
  } = useGetMyTasksQuery({ status: 'ASSIGNED', limit: 100 });

  // Transform API results to TaskItem[]
  const tasks = useMemo((): TaskItem[] => {
    if (!data?.result) return [];
    return data.result.map(transformToTaskItem);
  }, [data]);

  // Group tasks by date
  const calendarData = useMemo(() => groupTasksByDate(tasks), [tasks]);

  // Get tasks for selected date
  const selectedDateTasks = useMemo(() => {
    const day = calendarData.find((d) => d.date === selectedDate);
    return day?.tasks || [];
  }, [calendarData, selectedDate]);

  return {
    tasks,
    calendarData,
    selectedDate,
    selectedDateTasks,
    setSelectedDate,
    isLoading,
    isError: !!isError,
    refetch,
    hasTasks: tasks.length > 0,
    hasTasksOnSelectedDate: selectedDateTasks.length > 0,
  };
};

That's the only file that needs to change. The mock import, the fake useEffect with setTimeout, and MOCK_CALENDAR_TASKS are all removed. The getMyTasks endpoint already exists in your tasksApi and transformToTaskItem is already exported from it, so nothing else needs updating.

The scheduledDate in transformToTaskItem does task.taskStartDateTime?.split('T')[0] which gives YYYY-MM-DD format — exactly what the calendar component expects for markedDates keys and date comparison.

8 May

Claude responded: The selected date circle isn't centered because the default calendar styling needs explicit alignment.

The selected date circle isn't centered because the default calendar styling needs explicit alignment. Fix it by adding selectedDayTextColor and adjusting the theme:

tsx
<Calendar
  current={selectedDate}
  onDayPress={(day: any) => setSelectedDate(day.dateString)}
  markedDates={markedDates}
  theme={{
    selectedDayBackgroundColor: '#2B7FFF',
    selectedDayTextColor: '#FFFFFF',        // ← add this
    todayTextColor: '#2B7FFF',
    arrowColor: '#2B7FFF',
    textSectionTitleColor: '#6B7280',
    monthTextColor: '#111827',
    textMonthFontFamily: 'Nunito-BoldItalic',
    textDayFontFamily: 'Nunito-Medium',
    textDayHeaderFontFamily: 'Nunito-SemiBold',
    textDayFontSize: 14,
    textMonthFontSize: 14,
    dayTextColor: '#111827',               // ← add this
    textDisabledColor: '#D1D5DB',          // ← add this
    dotColor: '#2B7FFF',                   // ← add this
    selectedDotColor: '#FFFFFF',           // ← add this
  }}
/>

If the circle still looks off-center, it's usually caused by a custom font affecting the day number alignment. Add this to the theme:

tsx
textDayStyle: { textAlign: 'center' },    // ← add this

If you're using a version of react-native-calendars that doesn't support textDayStyle in theme, wrap the Calendar in a View with explicit styling:

tsx
<View 
  className="mb-6 rounded-[16px] border border-inputborder bg-white p-2"
  style={{ overflow: 'hidden' }}           // ← add overflow hidden
>
  <Calendar ... />
</View>

The most common cause is the Nunito font having different metrics than the default system font, pushing the number slightly off-center within the circle. Setting textDayFontFamily to a font with standard metrics or removing it temporarily will confirm if that's the issue.

You said: here from the home page when clicking active tasks view all button.

here from the home page when clicking active tasks view all button.

it should go into the tasks screen with filtering in progress tasks . how can we do this?

8 May

Claude responded: Looking at your code, handleViewAllActive pushes to tasks?

Looking at your code, handleViewAllActive pushes to tasks?status=assigned but you want in-progress tasks. Also useProviderTasks needs to read the URL param on mount.

Step 1: Fix the route in ProviderHome

tsx
// Change this:
const handleViewAllActive = () => {
  router.push('/provider/(tabs)/tasks?status=assigned');
};

// To this:
const handleViewAllActive = () => {
  router.push('/provider/(tabs)/tasks?status=in-progress');
};

Step 2: Read the URL param in ProviderTasksScreen

tsx
// Add this import at the top:
import { useLocalSearchParams } from 'expo-router';

// Inside ProviderTasksScreen, add this before useProviderTasks:
const { status: initialStatus } = useLocalSearchParams<{ status?: string }>();

// Pass it to the hook:
const {
  tasks,
  activeStatus,
  changeStatus,
  // ... rest
} = useProviderTasks(initialStatus || 'assigned');

Step 3: Update useProviderTasks to accept an initial status

Check your useProviderTasks hook — it likely looks like this:

ts
export const useProviderTasks = (initialStatus: string = 'assigned') => {
  const [activeStatus, setActiveStatus] = useState(initialStatus);
  // ...
};

If it already accepts a parameter, you're done. If it doesn't, add the parameter:

ts
export const useProviderTasks = (initialStatus: string = 'assigned') => {
  const [activeStatus, setActiveStatus] = useState(initialStatus);

  // ... rest of hook stays the same
};

That's all. Now when the "View all" button is tapped from the Active Tasks section on the home screen, it navigates to the tasks screen with in-progress pre-selected in the filter tabs.

You said: Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'TaskStatus | undefined'.

Argument of type 'string' is not assignable to parameter of type 'TaskStatus | undefined'.

8 May

Claude responded: The useProviderTasks hook expects TaskStatus type, not a plain string.

The useProviderTasks hook expects TaskStatus type, not a plain string. Fix it by casting the URL param:

tsx
// In ProviderTasksScreen, replace:
const { status: initialStatus } = useLocalSearchParams<{ status?: string }>();

const {
  tasks,
  activeStatus,
  changeStatus,
  // ...
} = useProviderTasks(initialStatus || 'assigned');

// With:
const { status: initialStatus } = useLocalSearchParams<{ status?: string }>();

const {
  tasks,
  activeStatus,
  changeStatus,
  // ...
} = useProviderTasks((initialStatus as TaskStatus) || 'assigned');

And make sure TaskStatus is imported at the top of ProviderTasksScreen:

tsx
import { TaskStatus } from '@/types/Tasks';

That's it. The cast is safe here because you control what values get passed via router.push — as long as you only push valid status strings like 'in-progress', 'assigned', etc., it will work correctly.

// hooks/providerSide/useProviderTasks.ts
import { useState, useMemo, useCallback } from 'react';
import { useGetMyTasksQuery } from '@/store/api/tasksApi';
import { TaskItem, TaskStatus } from '@/types/Tasks';
import { ServiceType } from '@/types/services';
import { toFrontendFormat } from '@/utils/serviceTypeMapper';

// Map backend status to frontend TaskStatus
const mapStatusToTaskStatus = (status: string): TaskStatus => {
  const statusMap: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
    OPEN: 'open',
    PRIVATE: 'private',
    ASSIGNED: 'assigned',
    IN_PROGRESS: 'in-progress',
    COMPLETED: 'completed',
    CANCELLED: 'cancelled',
  };
  return statusMap[status] || 'open';
};

const transformToTaskItem = (task: any): TaskItem => ({
  id: task._id,
  serviceType: toFrontendFormat(task.serviceType),
  taskStatus: mapStatusToTaskStatus(task.status),
  serviceLocation: task.address,
  taskValue: task.budget,
  scheduledDate: task.taskStartDateTime?.split('T')[0],
  scheduledTime: task.taskStartDateTime?.split('T')[1]?.slice(0, 5),
  createdAt: task.createdAt,
  address: task.address,
  title: task.title,
  budget: task.budget,
  coordinates: task.location?.coordinates
    ? {
        lat: task.location.coordinates[1],
        lng: task.location.coordinates[0],
      }
    : undefined,
});

// Updated mapping to include OPEN
const PROVIDER_STATUS_MAPPING: Record<string, string> = {
  open: 'OPEN', // Added
  private: 'PRIVATE',
  assigned: 'ASSIGNED',
  'in-progress': 'IN_PROGRESS',
  completed: 'COMPLETED',
  cancelled: 'CANCELLED',
};

export const useProviderTasks = (initialStatus: TaskStatus = 'open') => {
  const [activeStatus, setActiveStatus] = useState<TaskStatus>(initialStatus);
  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
  const [sortBy, setSortBy] = useState('createdAt');
