  const [sortOrder, setSortOrder] = useState<'asc' | 'desc'>('desc');
  const [selectedServiceType, setSelectedServiceType] = useState<ServiceType | ''>('');

  const { data, isLoading, error, refetch } = useGetMyTasksQuery({
    status: PROVIDER_STATUS_MAPPING[activeStatus] || activeStatus.toUpperCase(),
    searchTerm: searchTerm || undefined,
    sortBy,
    sortOrder,
    serviceType: selectedServiceType || undefined,
    page: 1,
    limit: 50,
  });

  const tasks: TaskItem[] = useMemo(() => {
    if (!data?.result) return [];
    return data.result.map(transformToTaskItem);
  }, [data]);

  const taskCount = data?.meta?.total || 0;

  const changeStatus = useCallback((status: TaskStatus) => {
    setActiveStatus(status);
  }, []);

  const refetchTasks = useCallback(() => {
    refetch();
  }, [refetch]);

  return {
    tasks,
    activeStatus,
    changeStatus,
    isLoading,
    isError: !!error,
    refetch: refetchTasks,
    taskCount,
    searchTerm,
    setSearchTerm,
    sortBy,
    setSortBy,
    sortOrder,
    setSortOrder,
    selectedServiceType,
    setSelectedServiceType,
  };
};



still didnt work .
8 May

Claude responded: The issue is that useProviderTasks initializes activeStatus with useState(initialStatus) — but useState only uses the initial value once on mount.

The issue is that useProviderTasks initializes activeStatus with useState(initialStatus) — but useState only uses the initial value once on mount. If the component is already mounted from a previous navigation and you navigate to it again with a different status param, the state doesn't reset.

The fix is to use useEffect to sync the param with the state:

ts
// In useProviderTasks.ts — add useEffect import
import { useState, useMemo, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react';

export const useProviderTasks = (initialStatus: TaskStatus = 'open') => {
  const [activeStatus, setActiveStatus] = useState<TaskStatus>(initialStatus);

  // ← Add this: sync when initialStatus changes (e.g. navigating from home)
  useEffect(() => {
    if (initialStatus) {
      setActiveStatus(initialStatus);
    }
  }, [initialStatus]);

  // ... rest of hook stays exactly the same
};

And in ProviderTasksScreen, make sure the fallback is 'assigned' not 'open' since that's what the tab shows by default:

tsx
} = useProviderTasks((initialStatus as TaskStatus) ?? 'assigned');

Also double-check the home page push uses the correct key matching PROVIDER_TASK_STATUS_OPTIONS:

tsx
// In ProviderHome:
const handleViewAllActive = () => {
  router.push('/provider/(tabs)/tasks?status=in-progress');
};

The status value in the URL must exactly match the key in PROVIDER_TASK_STATUS_OPTIONS which is 'in-progress', and that maps to IN_PROGRESS via PROVIDER_STATUS_MAPPING. That chain is already correct in your code — the only missing piece was the useEffect sync.

// hooks/providerSide/useProviderTasks.ts
import { useState, useMemo, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react';
import { useGetMyTasksQuery } from '@/store/api/tasksApi';
import { TaskItem, TaskStatus } from '@/types/Tasks';
import { ServiceType } from '@/types/services';
import { toFrontendFormat } from '@/utils/serviceTypeMapper';

// Map backend status to frontend TaskStatus
const mapStatusToTaskStatus = (status: string): TaskStatus => {
  const statusMap: Record<string, TaskStatus> = {
    OPEN: 'open',
    PRIVATE: 'private',
    ASSIGNED: 'assigned',
    IN_PROGRESS: 'in-progress',
    COMPLETED: 'completed',
    CANCELLED: 'cancelled',
  };
  return statusMap[status] || 'open';
};

const transformToTaskItem = (task: any): TaskItem => ({
  id: task._id,
  serviceType: toFrontendFormat(task.serviceType),
  taskStatus: mapStatusToTaskStatus(task.status),
  serviceLocation: task.address,
  taskValue: task.budget,
  scheduledDate: task.taskStartDateTime?.split('T')[0],
  scheduledTime: task.taskStartDateTime?.split('T')[1]?.slice(0, 5),
  createdAt: task.createdAt,
  address: task.address,
  title: task.title,
  budget: task.budget,
  coordinates: task.location?.coordinates
    ? {
        lat: task.location.coordinates[1],
        lng: task.location.coordinates[0],
      }
    : undefined,
});

// Updated mapping to include OPEN
const PROVIDER_STATUS_MAPPING: Record<string, string> = {
  open: 'OPEN', // Added
  private: 'PRIVATE',
  assigned: 'ASSIGNED',
  'in-progress': 'IN_PROGRESS',
  completed: 'COMPLETED',
  cancelled: 'CANCELLED',
};

export const useProviderTasks = (initialStatus: TaskStatus = 'open') => {
  const [activeStatus, setActiveStatus] = useState<TaskStatus>(initialStatus);
  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
  const [sortBy, setSortBy] = useState('createdAt');
  const [sortOrder, setSortOrder] = useState<'asc' | 'desc'>('desc');
  const [selectedServiceType, setSelectedServiceType] = useState<ServiceType | ''>('');

  const { data, isLoading, error, refetch } = useGetMyTasksQuery({
    status: PROVIDER_STATUS_MAPPING[activeStatus] || activeStatus.toUpperCase(),
    searchTerm: searchTerm || undefined,
    sortBy,
    sortOrder,
    serviceType: selectedServiceType || undefined,
    page: 1,
    limit: 50,
  });

  const tasks: TaskItem[] = useMemo(() => {
    if (!data?.result) return [];
    return data.result.map(transformToTaskItem);
  }, [data]);

  const taskCount = data?.meta?.total || 0;

  const changeStatus = useCallback((status: TaskStatus) => {
    setActiveStatus(status);
  }, []);

  const refetchTasks = useCallback(() => {
    refetch();
  }, [refetch]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (initialStatus) {
      setActiveStatus(initialStatus);
    }
  }, [initialStatus]);

  return {
    tasks,
    activeStatus,
    changeStatus,
    isLoading,
    isError: !!error,
    refetch: refetchTasks,
    taskCount,
    searchTerm,
    setSearchTerm,
    sortBy,
    setSortBy,
    sortOrder,
    setSortOrder,
    selectedServiceType,
    setSelectedServiceType,
  };
};
when I am switching between the tasks tabs it not showing any active indicator loading that looks disturbing
8 May

Claude responded: The issue is that when switching status tabs, the old data stays visible while new data loads — there's no visual feedback.

The issue is that when switching status tabs, the old data stays visible while new data loads — there's no visual feedback. Fix it by tracking the previous status and showing a loading overlay when the status changes:

In useProviderTasks.ts, expose isFetching separately from isLoading:

ts
const { data, isLoading, isFetching, error, refetch } = useGetMyTasksQuery({
  status: PROVIDER_STATUS_MAPPING[activeStatus] || activeStatus.toUpperCase(),
  searchTerm: searchTerm || undefined,
  sortBy,
  sortOrder,
  serviceType: selectedServiceType || undefined,
  page: 1,
  limit: 50,
});

// Return both:
return {
  // ... existing
  isLoading,
  isFetching,  // ← add this
  isError: !!error,
  // ...
};

Then in ProviderTasksScreen, use isFetching to show an overlay on the list while switching tabs:

tsx
const {
  tasks,
  activeStatus,
  changeStatus,
  isLoading,
  isFetching,   // ← add this
  isError,
  refetch,
  taskCount,
  searchTerm,
  setSearchTerm,
  sortBy,
  setSortBy,
  sortOrder,
  setSortOrder,
  selectedServiceType,
  setSelectedServiceType,
} = useProviderTasks((initialStatus as TaskStatus) ?? 'assigned');

Then wrap the FlatList in a View with a relative position and add an overlay:

tsx
{/* Replace your existing FlatList with this: */}
<View style={{ flex: 1 }}>
  <FlatList
    data={tasks}
    keyExtractor={(item) => item.id}
    renderItem={({ item }) => (
      <ProviderTaskCard task={item} onView={handleViewTask} style={{ marginBottom: 12 }} />
    )}
    contentContainerStyle={{
      paddingHorizontal: LAYOUT.screen.paddingX,
      paddingBottom: LAYOUT.screen.scrollViewPaddingBottom,
    }}
    showsVerticalScrollIndicator={false}
    refreshing={false}
    onRefresh={refetch}
    ListEmptyComponent={
      !isLoading && !isFetching ? (
        <View className="mt-4">
          <NotFoundCard text={getStatusMessage()} />
        </View>
      ) : null
    }
    ListHeaderComponent={
      isLoading ? (
        <View className="items-center py-8">
          <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2B7FFF" />
        </View>
      ) : null
    }
  />

  {/* Fetching overlay — shows when switching tabs */}
  {isFetching && !isLoading && (
    <View
      style={{
        position: 'absolute',
        top: 0,
        left: 0,
        right: 0,
        bottom: 0,
        backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.7)',
        alignItems: 'center',
        justifyContent: 'center',
      }}>
      <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2B7FFF" />
    </View>
  )}
</View>

The difference between isLoading and isFetching in RTK Query is:

  • isLoading — true only on the first load when there's no cached data yet
  • isFetching — true every time a request is in flight, including when switching tabs

This way the first load shows the full loading state, and subsequent tab switches show a semi-transparent overlay over the existing list content rather than a blank screen.

You said: need to do here same as well.

need to do here same as well. need to show loading on switching the task tab

8 May

Claude responded: In hooks/tasks/useTasks.

In hooks/tasks/useTasks.ts, expose isFetching:

ts
const { data, isLoading, isFetching, error, refetch } = useGetMyTasksQuery({...});

return {
  // ... existing
  isLoading,
  isFetching,  // ← add
  isError: !!error,
  // ...
};

Then in Tasks screen, destructure isFetching and wrap the FlatList:

tsx
const {
  tasks,
  activeStatus,
  changeStatus,
  isLoading,
  isFetching,        // ← add
  isError,
  refetch,
  taskCount,
  searchTerm,
  setSearchTerm,
  sortBy,
  setSortBy,
  sortOrder,
  setSortOrder,
  selectedServiceType,
  setSelectedServiceType,
} = useTasks('open');

Then replace the {/* Tasks List */} section with:

tsx
{/* Tasks List */}
<View style={{ flex: 1 }}>
  <FlatList
    data={tasks}
    keyExtractor={(item) => item.id}
    renderItem={({ item }) => (
      <TaskCard task={item} onView={handleViewTask} style={{ marginBottom: 12 }} />
    )}
    contentContainerStyle={{
      paddingHorizontal: LAYOUT.screen.paddingX,
      paddingBottom: LAYOUT.screen.scrollViewPaddingBottom,
    }}
    showsVerticalScrollIndicator={false}
    refreshing={false}
    onRefresh={refetch}
    ListEmptyComponent={
      !isLoading && !isFetching ? (
        <View style={{ marginTop: 12 }}>
          <NotFoundCard
            text={
              activeStatus === 'private'
                ? 'No private tasks found'
                : `No ${activeStatus} tasks found`
            }
          />
        </View>
      ) : null
    }
    ListHeaderComponent={
      isLoading ? (
        <View style={{ paddingVertical: 30, alignItems: 'center' }}>
          <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2B7FFF" />
        </View>
      ) : null
    }
  />

  {/* Overlay when switching tabs */}
  {isFetching && !isLoading && (
    <View
      style={{
        position: 'absolute',
        top: 0,
        left: 0,
        right: 0,
        bottom: 0,
        backgroundColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.7)',
        alignItems: 'center',
        justifyContent: 'center',
      }}>
      <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2B7FFF" />
    </View>
  )}
</View>

Also make sure useTasks hook has isFetching — check the hook and if it uses useGetMyTasksQuery, RTK Query already provides it, just destructure and return it.

You said: can we also accept here any task filter type ?

can we also accept here any task filter type ?

8 May

Claude responded: Yes.

Yes. Add useLocalSearchParams to read an initial status from the URL, same pattern as the provider tasks screen:

tsx
// Add to imports:
import { useRouter, useLocalSearchParams } from 'expo-router';
import { TaskStatus } from '@/types/Tasks'; // add if not already imported

// Inside Tasks component, add before useTasks:
const { status: initialStatus } = useLocalSearchParams<{ status?: string }>();

// Update useTasks call:
const {
  tasks,
  activeStatus,
  changeStatus,
  isLoading,
  isFetching,
  isError,
  refetch,
  taskCount,
  searchTerm,
  setSearchTerm,
  sortBy,
  setSortBy,
  sortOrder,
  setSortOrder,
  selectedServiceType,
  setSelectedServiceType,
} = useTasks((initialStatus as TaskStatus) ?? 'open');

Then in useTasks.ts, add the useEffect sync just like useProviderTasks:

ts
import { useState, useMemo, useCallback, useEffect } from 'react';

export const useTasks = (initialStatus: TaskStatus = 'open') => {
  const [activeStatus, setActiveStatus] = useState<TaskStatus>(initialStatus);

  // Sync when navigating with a different status param
  useEffect(() => {
    if (initialStatus) {
      setActiveStatus(initialStatus);
    }
  }, [initialStatus]);

  // ... rest of hook stays the same
};

Now you can navigate to the customer tasks screen with any status pre-selected:

tsx
router.push('/customer/(tabs)/tasks?status=assigned');
router.push('/customer/(tabs)/tasks?status=in-progress');
router.push('/customer/(tabs)/tasks?status=completed');
// /components/ui/provider/tasks/details/TimelineInfoCard.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';

interface TimelineInfoCardProps {
  type: 'started' | 'completed';
  date: string;
  time: string;
  fullDateTime: string;
}

const TimelineInfoCard = ({ type, date, time, fullDateTime }: TimelineInfoCardProps) => {
  const isStarted = type === 'started';
  const icon = isStarted ? 'play-circle-outline' : 'checkmark-circle-outline';
  const iconColor = isStarted ? '#F97316' : '#10B981';
  const title = isStarted ? 'Task Started' : 'Task Completed';
  const bgColor = isStarted ? '#FEF3E8' : '#F0FDF4';
  const textColor = isStarted ? '#F97316' : '#10B981';

  return (
    <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: bgColor }}>
      <View className="flex-row items-center">
        <Ionicons name={icon as any} size={24} color={iconColor} />
        <View className="ml-3 flex-1">
          <Text className="font-nunitoSemi text-sm" style={{ color: textColor }}>
            {title}
          </Text>
          <Text className="mt-0.5 font-nunito text-sm text-gray-600">{fullDateTime}</Text>
        </View>
      </View>
    </BorderCard>
  );
};

export default TimelineInfoCard;


timeline info not working properly. 
when the task is completed showing task started text with the date and time. 

and the provider should be able to go on complete screen with the related action button.
9 May

Claude responded: Two issues to fix:

Two issues to fix:

Issue 1: startedAt mapping is wrong

In useProviderTaskDetails.ts, startedAt uses isMarkCompletedByProvider as the condition but should check if beforeImages exist:

ts
// In taskDetails useMemo, replace:
startedAt: rawTask.isMarkCompletedByProvider
  ? formatDate(rawTask.markCompletedByProviderAt)
  : null,

// With:
startedAt: (rawTask.beforeImages?.length ?? 0) > 0
  ? formatDate(rawTask.markCompletedByProviderAt)   // when provider started (uploaded before photos)
  : null,

Issue 2: Timeline shows wrong card

In ProviderTaskDetailsPage, the timeline section shows startedAt even when completed. Fix the condition:

tsx
{/* Timeline Info */}
{(isInProgress || isCompleted) && (
  <View className="gap-2.5">
    {/* Started card — only show if task has truly started (before images uploaded) */}
    {taskDetails.startedAt && (
      <TimelineInfoCard
        type="started"
        date={taskDetails.startedAt.date}
        time={taskDetails.startedAt.time}
        fullDateTime={taskDetails.startedAt.fullDateTime}
      />
    )}

    {/* Completed card — only show when fully completed */}
    {isCompleted && taskDetails.completedAt && (
      <TimelineInfoCard
        type="completed"
        date={taskDetails.completedAt.date}
        time={taskDetails.completedAt.time}
        fullDateTime={taskDetails.completedAt.fullDateTime}
      />
    )}
  </View>
)}

Issue 3: Complete button not navigating properly

The completeTask navigates to /complete but the route uses taskId not id. Check your file is at:

/app/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/[taskId]/complete.tsx

But your details page uses [taskId] as the param name and reads it as id:

tsx
const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();

So the route segment is [taskId] but you're reading id — that's inconsistent. Either rename the folder to [id] or fix the completeTask navigation in the hook:

ts
// In completeTask callback, it currently does:
router.push(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${taskId}/complete`);

// This is correct as long as the complete page reads useLocalSearchParams with the right key
// In your complete page, make sure you use:
const { taskId } = useLocalSearchParams<{ taskId: string }>();
// NOT:
const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();

Check your complete page's param name matches the folder name [taskId].

You said: // In completeTask callback, it currently does:

// In completeTask callback, it currently does: router.push(/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${taskId}/complete); // This is correct as long as the complete page reads useLocalSearchParams with the right key // In your complete page, make sure you use: const { taskId } = useLocalSearchParams<{ taskId: string }>(); // NOT: const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();

where to make this change?

9 May

Claude responded: In your complete page file.

In your complete page file. Based on your folder structure it should be at:

/app/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/[taskId]/complete.tsx

Open that file and find this line:

tsx
// Change this:
const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();

// To this:
const { taskId } = useLocalSearchParams<{ taskId: string }>();

Then wherever id is used in that file to pass to useProviderTaskDetails, change it to taskId:

tsx
// Change this:
const { taskDetails, isLoading, submitCompleteTask, isCompleting } = useProviderTaskDetails(id as string);

// To this:
const { taskDetails, isLoading, submitCompleteTask, isCompleting } = useProviderTaskDetails(taskId as string);

And the router.replace after success:

tsx
// Change this:
router.replace(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${id}`);

// To this:
router.replace(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${taskId}`);

That's all the changes in the complete page. The folder is named [taskId] so the param key must be taskId everywhere in that file.

You said: provider should have the complete screen view after the task mark as complete from his side.

provider should have the complete screen view after the task mark as complete from his side. u know. and need to show something like waiting for customer approval u know.

9 May

Claude responded: Looking at your code, the complete page is at /app/provider/provider-flow/tasks/[id]/complete.

Looking at your code, the complete page is at /app/provider/provider-flow/tasks/[id]/complete.tsx — note it uses [id] not [taskId]. So the param name id is actually correct for this file. Leave it as is.

The issue is after submitCompleteTask succeeds, it navigates back to task details — but the task details page should now show a "waiting for customer" state. Let me fix the complete page navigation and add the waiting state to the task details page.

Fix 1: Complete page — remove duplicate Toast and fix navigation

tsx
const handleCompleteTask = async () => {
  if (photos.length === 0) {
    setError('Please upload at least one after photo');
    return;
  }

  try {
    await submitCompleteTask(photos);
    // Navigate back — task details will show waiting state automatically
    router.replace(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${id}`);
  } catch (error) {
    // toast already shown in hook
  }
};

Fix 2: Add "Waiting for customer" state in ProviderTaskDetailsPage

In ActionButtons for the provider side, add this case. In /components/ui/provider/tasks/details/ActionButtons.tsx:

tsx
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';

// Add this BEFORE the canComplete button block:

{/* Provider marked complete — waiting for customer */}
{isInProgress && taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete && (
  <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0FDF4' }}>
    <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
      <View
        style={{
          width: 40,
          height: 40,
          borderRadius: 20,
          backgroundColor: '#10B98120',
          alignItems: 'center',
          justifyContent: 'center',
        }}>
        <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={20} color="#10B981" />
      </View>
      <View className="flex-1">
        <Text className="font-nunitoBold text-sm text-green-700">
          Waiting for Customer Approval
        </Text>
        <Text className="mt-0.5 font-nunito text-xs text-green-600">
          You've marked this task as complete. The customer will review and confirm.
        </Text>
      </View>
    </View>
  </BorderCard>
)}

The full updated ActionButtons for the provider:

tsx
const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isOpenTask,
  isPrivateTask,
  isAssigned,
  isInProgress,
  isCompleted,
  canAccept,
  canStart,
  canComplete,
  canCancel,
  canCounter,
  onAccept,
  onCounter,
  onStart,
  onComplete,
  onCancel,
  onTrackMap,
  onViewInvoice,
  isCancelled,
  isAccepting = false,
  isCountering = false,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  return (
    <View className="gap-2.5">
      {/* Accept Task Button */}
      {canAccept && onAccept && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title={isAccepting ? 'Accepting...' : 'Accept Task'}
          onPress={onAccept}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          disabled={isAccepting}
        />
      )}

      {/* Counter Offer Button */}
      {canCounter && (
        <OutlineButton
          title={isCountering ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Counter Offer'}
          onPress={onCounter}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          bgColor="#FFFFFF"
          disabled={isCountering}
        />
      )}

      {/* Start Button */}
      {canStart && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Start Task"
          onPress={onStart}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
        />
      )}

      {/* Complete Button — only if not already marked complete */}
      {canComplete && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Mark as Complete"
          onPress={onComplete}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12, backgroundColor: '#10B981' }}
          rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* ✅ Waiting for customer approval — shown after provider marks complete */}
      {isInProgress && taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete && (
        <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0FDF4' }}>
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
            <View
              style={{
                width: 40,
                height: 40,
                borderRadius: 20,
                backgroundColor: '#10B98120',
                alignItems: 'center',
                justifyContent: 'center',
              }}>
              <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={20} color="#10B981" />
            </View>
            <View className="flex-1">
              <Text className="font-nunitoBold text-sm text-green-700">
                Waiting for Customer Approval
              </Text>
              <Text className="mt-0.5 font-nunito text-xs text-green-600">
                You've marked this task as complete. The customer will review and confirm.
              </Text>
            </View>
          </View>
        </BorderCard>
      )}

      {/* View on Map Button */}
      {(isAssigned || isInProgress || isOpenTask || isPrivateTask) && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View Invoice Button */}
      {isCompleted && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<Ionicons name="document-text-outline" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* Cancel Button */}
      {canCancel && !isCancelled && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Cancel Task"
          onPress={onCancel}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
        />
      )}
    </View>
  );
};

Now the flow is:

Provider taps "Mark as Complete"
  → Uploads after photos → API call
    → Success → navigates back to task details
      → Task details shows:
          - TimelineInfoCard (started)
          - "Waiting for Customer Approval" card  ← new
          - View on Map button
// /components/ui/provider/tasks/details/ActionButtons.tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View } from 'react-native';
import PrimaryButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import OutlineButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/OutlineButton';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { LocationIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import { ProviderTaskDetailsType } from '@/hooks/providerSide/useProviderTaskDetails';

interface ActionButtonsProps {
  taskDetails: ProviderTaskDetailsType;
  isOpenTask: boolean;
  isPrivateTask: boolean;
  isAssigned: boolean;
  isInProgress: boolean;
  isCompleted: boolean;
  canAccept: boolean;
  canStart: boolean;
  canComplete: boolean;
  canCancel: boolean;
  canCounter: boolean;
  onAccept?: () => void; // Made optional since provider might have already bid
  onCounter: () => void;
  onStart: () => void;
  onComplete: () => void;
  onCancel: () => void;
  onTrackMap: () => void;
  onViewInvoice: () => void;
  isCancelled: boolean;
  isAccepting?: boolean;
  isCountering?: boolean;
}

const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isOpenTask,
  isPrivateTask,
  isAssigned,
  isInProgress,
  isCompleted,
  canAccept,
  canStart,
  canComplete,
  canCancel,
  canCounter,
  onAccept,
  onCounter,
  onStart,
  onComplete,
  onCancel,
  onTrackMap,
  onViewInvoice,
  isCancelled,
  isAccepting = false,
  isCountering = false,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  return (
    <View className="gap-2.5">
      {/* Accept Task Button - Only show if canAccept is true AND onAccept exists */}
      {canAccept && onAccept && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title={isAccepting ? 'Accepting...' : 'Accept Task'}
          onPress={onAccept}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          disabled={isAccepting}
        />
      )}

      {/* Counter Offer Button - Only show if canCounter is true */}
      {canCounter && (
        <OutlineButton
          title={isCountering ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Counter Offer'}
          onPress={onCounter}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          bgColor="#FFFFFF"
          disabled={isCountering}
        />
      )}

      {/* Start Button */}
      {canStart && (
        <PrimaryButton title="Start Task" onPress={onStart} style={{ borderRadius: 12 }} />
      )}

      {/* Complete Button */}
      {canComplete && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Mark as Complete"
          onPress={onComplete}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12, backgroundColor: '#10B981' }}
          rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View on Map Button */}
      {(isAssigned || isInProgress || isOpenTask || isPrivateTask) && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View Invoice Button */}
      {isCompleted && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<Ionicons name="document-text-outline" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* Cancel Button */}
      {canCancel && !isCancelled && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Cancel Task"
          onPress={onCancel}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
        />
      )}
    </View>
  );
};

export default ActionButtons;


provide here accordingly
9 May
tsx
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text } from 'react-native';
import PrimaryButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import OutlineButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/OutlineButton';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { LocationIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import { ProviderTaskDetailsType } from '@/hooks/providerSide/useProviderTaskDetails';

interface ActionButtonsProps {
  taskDetails: ProviderTaskDetailsType;
  isOpenTask: boolean;
  isPrivateTask: boolean;
  isAssigned: boolean;
  isInProgress: boolean;
  isCompleted: boolean;
  canAccept: boolean;
  canStart: boolean;
  canComplete: boolean;
  canCancel: boolean;
  canCounter: boolean;
  onAccept?: () => void;
  onCounter: () => void;
  onStart: () => void;
  onComplete: () => void;
  onCancel: () => void;
  onTrackMap: () => void;
  onViewInvoice: () => void;
  isCancelled: boolean;
  isAccepting?: boolean;
  isCountering?: boolean;
}

const ActionButtons = ({
  taskDetails,
  isOpenTask,
  isPrivateTask,
  isAssigned,
  isInProgress,
  isCompleted,
  canAccept,
  canStart,
  canComplete,
  canCancel,
  canCounter,
  onAccept,
  onCounter,
  onStart,
  onComplete,
  onCancel,
  onTrackMap,
  onViewInvoice,
  isCancelled,
  isAccepting = false,
  isCountering = false,
}: ActionButtonsProps) => {
  const isProviderMarkedComplete = taskDetails.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete;

  return (
    <View className="gap-2.5">
      {/* Accept Task Button */}
      {canAccept && onAccept && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title={isAccepting ? 'Accepting...' : 'Accept Task'}
          onPress={onAccept}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          disabled={isAccepting}
        />
      )}

      {/* Counter Offer Button */}
      {canCounter && (
        <OutlineButton
          title={isCountering ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Counter Offer'}
          onPress={onCounter}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          bgColor="#FFFFFF"
          disabled={isCountering}
        />
      )}

      {/* Start Button */}
      {canStart && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Start Task"
          onPress={onStart}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
        />
      )}

      {/* Complete Button — hidden once provider has already marked complete */}
      {canComplete && !isProviderMarkedComplete && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Mark as Complete"
          onPress={onComplete}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12, backgroundColor: '#10B981' }}
          rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* Waiting for customer approval */}
      {isInProgress && isProviderMarkedComplete && (
        <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0FDF4' }}>
          <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
            <View
              style={{
                width: 40,
                height: 40,
                borderRadius: 20,
                backgroundColor: '#10B98120',
                alignItems: 'center',
                justifyContent: 'center',
              }}>
              <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={20} color="#10B981" />
            </View>
            <View className="flex-1">
              <Text className="font-nunitoBold text-sm text-green-700">
                Waiting for Customer Approval
              </Text>
              <Text className="mt-0.5 font-nunito text-xs text-green-600">
                You've marked this task as complete. The customer will review and confirm.
              </Text>
            </View>
          </View>
        </BorderCard>
      )}

      {/* View on Map Button */}
      {(isAssigned || isInProgress || isOpenTask || isPrivateTask) && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View on Map"
          onPress={onTrackMap}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<LocationIcon size={14} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* View Invoice Button */}
      {isCompleted && (
        <OutlineButton
          title="View Invoice"
          onPress={onViewInvoice}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          textColor="#2B7FFF"
          leftIcon={<Ionicons name="document-text-outline" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />}
        />
      )}

      {/* Cancel Button */}
      {canCancel && !isCancelled && (
        <PrimaryButton
          title="Cancel Task"
          onPress={onCancel}
          style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
          gradientColors={['#DC3545', '#FE4C5D']}
        />
      )}
    </View>
  );
};

export default ActionButtons;

You said: but I need to allow him to see the complete screen so he can also view the screen before after images u know.

but I need to allow him to see the complete screen so he can also view the screen before after images u know.

9 May

Claude responded: Add a "View Completion Details" button that shows when the provider has already marked complete:

Add a "View Completion Details" button that shows when the provider has already marked complete:

tsx
{/* Waiting for customer approval */}
{isInProgress && isProviderMarkedComplete && (
  <View className="gap-2.5">
    <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 12, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0FDF4' }}>
      <View className="flex-row items-center gap-3">
        <View
          style={{
            width: 40,
            height: 40,
            borderRadius: 20,
            backgroundColor: '#10B98120',
            alignItems: 'center',
            justifyContent: 'center',
          }}>
          <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={20} color="#10B981" />
        </View>
        <View className="flex-1">
          <Text className="font-nunitoBold text-sm text-green-700">
            Waiting for Customer Approval
          </Text>
          <Text className="mt-0.5 font-nunito text-xs text-green-600">
            You&apos;ve marked this task as complete. The customer will review and confirm.
          </Text>
        </View>
      </View>
    </BorderCard>

    {/* View completion details button */}
    <OutlineButton
      title="View Completion Details"
      onPress={onComplete}
      style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
      textColor="#10B981"
      borderColor="#10B981"
      leftIcon={<Ionicons name="images-outline" size={16} color="#10B981" />}
    />
  </View>
)}

Then in CompleteTaskPage, update it to handle both modes — viewing existing completion or uploading new photos. Add a view-only mode at the top of the file:

tsx
const CompleteTaskPage = () => {
  const router = useRouter();
  const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();
  const [photos, setPhotos] = useState<string[]>([]);
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const [showBeforeImages, setShowBeforeImages] = useState(true); // ← default open
  const [selectedImageIndex, setSelectedImageIndex] = useState<number>(-1);
  const [showAfterImages, setShowAfterImages] = useState(true); // ← add this

  const { taskDetails, isLoading, submitCompleteTask, isCompleting } = useProviderTaskDetails(
    id as string
  );

  // ← add this: if provider already marked complete, it's view-only mode
  const isViewOnly = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete ?? false;
  const afterImages = taskDetails?.afterImages || [];
  const beforeImages = taskDetails?.beforeImages || [];

  // ... rest of handlers stay the same

  return (
    <>
      <SafeAreaView edges={['top', 'bottom']} className="flex-1 bg-white">
        <CustomHeader
          title={isViewOnly ? 'Completion Details' : 'Mark Task as Complete'}
          backButton
          backgroundColor="#FFFFFF"
        />

        <ScrollView ...>
          <View className="gap-4">

            {/* Before Images Section */}
            {beforeImages.length > 0 && (
              <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0F9FF' }}>
                <TouchableOpacity
                  onPress={() => setShowBeforeImages(!showBeforeImages)}
                  className="mb-3 flex-row items-center justify-between">
                  <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
                    <Ionicons name="images-outline" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />
                    <LabelPrimary text="Before Photos" style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 12 }} />
                  </View>
                  <View className="flex-row items-center gap-1">
                    <TextBodySmall
                      text={`${beforeImages.length} photo${beforeImages.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`}
                      style={{ color: '#6B7280' }}
                    />
                    <Ionicons
                      name={showBeforeImages ? 'chevron-up' : 'chevron-down'}
                      size={18}
                      color="#6B7280"
                    />
                  </View>
                </TouchableOpacity>

                {showBeforeImages && (
                  <ScrollView horizontal showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false} contentContainerStyle={{ gap: 8 }}>
                    {beforeImages.map((imageUri, index) => (
                      <TouchableOpacity key={index} onPress={() => setSelectedImageIndex(index)} activeOpacity={0.9}>
                        <Image source={{ uri: imageUri }} className="h-24 w-24 rounded-lg" resizeMode="cover" />
                      </TouchableOpacity>
                    ))}
                  </ScrollView>
                )}
              </BorderCard>
            )}

            {/* ✅ After Images — view only when already submitted */}
            {isViewOnly && afterImages.length > 0 && (
              <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0FDF4' }}>
                <TouchableOpacity
                  onPress={() => setShowAfterImages(!showAfterImages)}
                  className="mb-3 flex-row items-center justify-between">
                  <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
                    <Ionicons name="checkmark-circle-outline" size={20} color="#10B981" />
                    <LabelPrimary text="After Photos" style={{ color: '#10B981', fontSize: 12 }} />
                  </View>
                  <View className="flex-row items-center gap-1">
                    <TextBodySmall
                      text={`${afterImages.length} photo${afterImages.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`}
                      style={{ color: '#6B7280' }}
                    />
                    <Ionicons
                      name={showAfterImages ? 'chevron-up' : 'chevron-down'}
                      size={18}
                      color="#6B7280"
                    />
                  </View>
                </TouchableOpacity>

                {showAfterImages && (
                  <ScrollView horizontal showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false} contentContainerStyle={{ gap: 8 }}>
                    {afterImages.map((imageUri, index) => (
                      <TouchableOpacity key={index} onPress={() => setSelectedImageIndex(index)} activeOpacity={0.9}>
                        <Image source={{ uri: imageUri }} className="h-24 w-24 rounded-lg" resizeMode="cover" />
                      </TouchableOpacity>
                    ))}
                  </ScrollView>
                )}
              </BorderCard>
            )}

            {/* ✅ Upload section — only show if NOT view only */}
            {!isViewOnly && (
              <>
                <ImageUploadInputField
                  label="Upload After Photos"
                  value={photos}
                  name="after_photos"
                  handler={handlePhotoUpload}
                  placeholder="Add up to 8 images"
                  required={true}
                  max={8}
                  error={!!error}
                  errorMessage={error}
                />

                <View className="mb-6 flex-row items-center gap-1.5">
                  <IconBadge
                    size={24}
                    icon={<ExclamatoryInRoundedSquareIcon size={16} color="#EF4444" />}
                    style={{ backgroundColor: '#EF44441A', borderRadius: 4 }}
                  />
                  <View className="flex-1">
                    <TextBodySmall
                      text="Upload photos showing the completed work. Required to mark the task as complete."
                      style={{ color: '#EF4444' }}
                    />
                  </View>
                </View>
              </>
            )}

            {/* ✅ Status badge when view only */}
            {isViewOnly && (
              <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0FDF4' }}>
                <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
                  <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={18} color="#10B981" />
                  <TextBodySmall
                    text="Task marked as complete. Waiting for customer confirmation."
                    style={{ color: '#065F46', fontWeight: '600' }}
                  />
                </View>
              </BorderCard>
            )}

            {/* Task Info Card — always show */}
            <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0F9FF' }}>
              {/* ... same as before */}
            </BorderCard>

            {/* Action Buttons */}
            <View className="mt-4 gap-3">
              {!isViewOnly && (
                <PrimaryButton
                  title={isCompleting ? 'Completing...' : 'Mark as Complete'}
                  onPress={handleCompleteTask}
                  disabled={photos.length === 0 || isCompleting}
                  style={{
                    borderRadius: 12,
                    backgroundColor: photos.length === 0 ? '#9CA3AF' : '#10B981',
                  }}
                  rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
                />
              )}

              <OutlineButton
                onPress={() => router.back()}
                title={isViewOnly ? 'Go Back' : 'Cancel'}
                style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
              />
            </View>

          </View>
        </ScrollView>
      </SafeAreaView>

      {selectedImageIndex !== -1 && (
        <FullScreenImageViewer
          visible={selectedImageIndex !== -1}
          images={isViewOnly && showAfterImages ? afterImages : beforeImages}
          initialIndex={selectedImageIndex}
          onClose={() => setSelectedImageIndex(-1)}
        />
      )}
    </>
  );
};

The key logic is isViewOnly = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete — when true, the page switches to a read-only view showing both before and after photos, hides the upload field, and changes the button to "Go Back".

You said: so provide the updated code here.

so provide the updated code here. like when the provider has marked as completed by uploading the after images. he should see the after images as well like before photos. can we do this as well?

9 May

Claude responded: Key changes from the previous version: a shared renderImageSection helper renders both before and after photo sections with the same collapsible pattern, viewe…

tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { View, ScrollView, TouchableOpacity, Text, ActivityIndicator, Image } from 'react-native';
import { useLocalSearchParams, useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import { SafeAreaView } from 'react-native-safe-area-context';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';

import CustomHeader from '@/components/ui/shared/CustomHeader';
import PrimaryButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import BorderCard from '@/components/ui/shared/BorderCard';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';
import TextBodySmall from '@/components/ui/shared/TextBodySmall';
import ImageUploadInputField from '@/components/formFields/ImageUploadInputField';
import { ExclamatoryInRoundedSquareIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import IconBadge from '@/components/ui/icons/IconBadge';
import OutlineButton from '@/components/ui/shared/button/OutlineButton';
import FullScreenImageViewer from '@/components/ui/shared/FullScreenImageViewer';

import { useProviderTaskDetails } from '@/hooks/providerSide/useProviderTaskDetails';
import { LAYOUT } from '@/constants/constants';
import { SERVICE_THEMES } from '@/constants/serviceThemes';

const CompleteTaskPage = () => {
  const router = useRouter();
  const { id } = useLocalSearchParams<{ id: string }>();
  const [photos, setPhotos] = useState<string[]>([]);
  const [error, setError] = useState('');
  const [showBeforeImages, setShowBeforeImages] = useState(true);
  const [showAfterImages, setShowAfterImages] = useState(true);
  const [viewerImages, setViewerImages] = useState<string[]>([]);
  const [selectedImageIndex, setSelectedImageIndex] = useState<number>(-1);

  const { taskDetails, isLoading, submitCompleteTask, isCompleting } = useProviderTaskDetails(
    id as string
  );

  const isViewOnly = taskDetails?.completionStatus?.isProviderMarkedComplete ?? false;
  const beforeImages = taskDetails?.beforeImages || [];
  const afterImages = taskDetails?.afterImages || [];

  const handlePhotoUpload = (name: string, value: string[]) => {
    setPhotos(value);
    if (value.length > 0) setError('');
  };

  const handleOpenViewer = (images: string[], index: number) => {
    setViewerImages(images);
    setSelectedImageIndex(index);
  };

  const handleCompleteTask = async () => {
    if (photos.length === 0) {
      setError('Please upload at least one after photo');
      return;
    }
    try {
      await submitCompleteTask(photos);
      router.replace(`/provider/provider-flow/tasks/task-details/${id}`);
    } catch (error) {
      // toast already shown in hook
    }
  };

  if (isLoading) {
    return (
      <SafeAreaView edges={['top', 'bottom']} className="flex-1 bg-white">
        <CustomHeader
          title={isViewOnly ? 'Completion Details' : 'Mark Task as Complete'}
          backButton
          backgroundColor="#FFFFFF"
        />
        <View className="flex-1 items-center justify-center">
          <ActivityIndicator size="large" color="#2B7FFF" />
        </View>
      </SafeAreaView>
    );
  }

  if (!taskDetails) {
    return (
      <SafeAreaView edges={['top', 'bottom']} className="flex-1 bg-white">
        <CustomHeader
          title={isViewOnly ? 'Completion Details' : 'Mark Task as Complete'}
          backButton
          backgroundColor="#FFFFFF"
        />
        <View className="flex-1 items-center justify-center px-4">
          <Text className="font-nunito text-gray-500">Task not found</Text>
          <TouchableOpacity onPress={() => router.back()} className="mt-4">
            <Text className="font-nunitoSemi text-[#2B7FFF]">Go Back</Text>
          </TouchableOpacity>
        </View>
      </SafeAreaView>
    );
  }

  const theme = SERVICE_THEMES[taskDetails.serviceType];

  const renderImageSection = (
    title: string,
    images: string[],
    isVisible: boolean,
    onToggle: () => void,
    iconName: string,
    iconColor: string,
    bgColor: string
  ) => (
    <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: bgColor }}>
      <TouchableOpacity
        onPress={onToggle}
        className="flex-row items-center justify-between"
        style={{ marginBottom: isVisible && images.length > 0 ? 12 : 0 }}>
        <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
          <Ionicons name={iconName as any} size={20} color={iconColor} />
          <LabelPrimary text={title} style={{ color: iconColor, fontSize: 12 }} />
        </View>
        <View className="flex-row items-center gap-1">
          <TextBodySmall
            text={`${images.length} photo${images.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`}
            style={{ color: '#6B7280' }}
          />
          <Ionicons
            name={isVisible ? 'chevron-up' : 'chevron-down'}
            size={18}
            color="#6B7280"
          />
        </View>
      </TouchableOpacity>

      {isVisible && (
        <ScrollView
          horizontal
          showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}
          contentContainerStyle={{ gap: 8 }}>
          {images.map((imageUri, index) => (
            <TouchableOpacity
              key={index}
              onPress={() => handleOpenViewer(images, index)}
              activeOpacity={0.9}>
              <Image
                source={{ uri: imageUri }}
                style={{ width: 96, height: 96, borderRadius: 8 }}
                resizeMode="cover"
              />
            </TouchableOpacity>
          ))}
        </ScrollView>
      )}
    </BorderCard>
  );

  return (
    <>
      <SafeAreaView edges={['top', 'bottom']} className="flex-1 bg-white">
        <CustomHeader
          title={isViewOnly ? 'Completion Details' : 'Mark Task as Complete'}
          backButton
          backgroundColor="#FFFFFF"
        />

        <ScrollView
          showsVerticalScrollIndicator={false}
          contentContainerStyle={{
            paddingBottom: LAYOUT.screen.scrollViewPaddingBottom,
            paddingHorizontal: 16,
            marginTop: LAYOUT.innerPage.marginTop,
          }}>
          <View className="gap-4">

            {/* Before Images */}
            {beforeImages.length > 0 &&
              renderImageSection(
                'Before Photos',
                beforeImages,
                showBeforeImages,
                () => setShowBeforeImages(!showBeforeImages),
                'images-outline',
                '#2B7FFF',
                '#F0F9FF'
              )}

            {/* After Images — only in view mode */}
            {isViewOnly && afterImages.length > 0 &&
              renderImageSection(
                'After Photos',
                afterImages,
                showAfterImages,
                () => setShowAfterImages(!showAfterImages),
                'checkmark-circle-outline',
                '#10B981',
                '#F0FDF4'
              )}

            {/* Waiting status badge — view only */}
            {isViewOnly && (
              <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0FDF4' }}>
                <View className="flex-row items-center gap-2">
                  <Ionicons name="time-outline" size={18} color="#10B981" />
                  <TextBodySmall
                    text="Task marked as complete. Waiting for customer confirmation."
                    style={{ color: '#065F46', fontWeight: '600', flex: 1 }}
                  />
                </View>
              </BorderCard>
            )}

            {/* Upload section — only when NOT view only */}
            {!isViewOnly && (
              <>
                <ImageUploadInputField
                  label="Upload After Photos"
                  value={photos}
                  name="after_photos"
                  handler={handlePhotoUpload}
                  placeholder="Add up to 8 images"
                  required={true}
                  max={8}
                  error={!!error}
                  errorMessage={error}
                />

                <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center gap-1.5">
                  <IconBadge
                    size={24}
                    icon={<ExclamatoryInRoundedSquareIcon size={16} color="#EF4444" />}
                    style={{ backgroundColor: '#EF44441A', borderRadius: 4 }}
                  />
                  <View className="flex-1">
                    <TextBodySmall
                      text="Upload photos showing the completed work. Required to mark the task as complete."
                      style={{ color: '#EF4444' }}
                    />
                  </View>
                </View>
              </>
            )}

            {/* Task Info Card */}
            <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#F0F9FF' }}>
              <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center gap-2">
                <Ionicons name="information-circle" size={20} color="#2B7FFF" />
                <LabelPrimary text="Task Information" style={{ color: '#2B7FFF', fontSize: 12 }} />
              </View>
              <View className="gap-1">
                <Text className="font-nunitoBold text-gray-900">
                  {theme?.title || taskDetails.serviceType.replace('-', ' ')}
                </Text>
                <Text className="font-nunito text-sm text-gray-500">
                  {taskDetails.serviceLocation}
                </Text>
                <View className="mt-1 flex-row items-center">
                  <Ionicons name="calendar-outline" size={14} color="#9CA3AF" />
                  <Text className="ml-1 font-nunito text-xs text-gray-400">
                    {taskDetails.scheduledDate} at {taskDetails.scheduledTime}
                  </Text>
                </View>
              </View>
            </BorderCard>

            {/* Instructions — only when uploading */}
            {!isViewOnly && (
              <BorderCard style={{ borderRadius: 10, padding: 14, backgroundColor: '#FEF3E8' }}>
                <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center gap-2">
                  <Ionicons name="bulb-outline" size={20} color="#F97316" />
                  <LabelPrimary
                    text="Before You Complete"
                    style={{ color: '#F97316', fontSize: 12 }}
                  />
                </View>
                <TextBodySmall
                  text="Take clear photos of the completed work area. This helps protect both you and the customer and provides proof of completion."
                  style={{ color: '#4B5563', lineHeight: 18 }}
                />
              </BorderCard>
            )}

            {/* Action Buttons */}
            <View className="mt-4 gap-3">
              {!isViewOnly && (
                <PrimaryButton
                  title={isCompleting ? 'Completing...' : 'Mark as Complete'}
                  onPress={handleCompleteTask}
                  disabled={photos.length === 0 || isCompleting}
                  style={{
                    borderRadius: 12,
                    backgroundColor: photos.length === 0 ? '#9CA3AF' : '#10B981',
                  }}
                  rightIcon={<Ionicons name="checkmark-done" size={20} color="white" />}
                />
              )}

              <OutlineButton
                onPress={() => router.back()}
                title={isViewOnly ? 'Go Back' : 'Cancel'}
                style={{ borderRadius: 12 }}
              />
            </View>

          </View>
        </ScrollView>
      </SafeAreaView>

      {/* Full Screen Image Viewer */}
      {selectedImageIndex !== -1 && viewerImages.length > 0 && (
        <FullScreenImageViewer
          visible={selectedImageIndex !== -1}
          images={viewerImages}
          initialIndex={selectedImageIndex}
          onClose={() => {
            setSelectedImageIndex(-1);
            setViewerImages([]);
          }}
        />
      )}
    </>
  );
};

export default CompleteTaskPage;

Key changes from the previous version: a shared renderImageSection helper renders both before and after photo sections with the same collapsible pattern, viewerImages state tracks which set of images (before or after) to show in the full-screen viewer, and isViewOnly controls whether the upload UI or the read-only view is shown.


// components/ui/shared/FullScreenImageViewer.tsx
import React, { useRef, useState } from 'react';
import { Modal, View, TouchableOpacity, Dimensions, Animated, FlatList, Text } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { GestureHandlerRootView, PinchGestureHandler, State } from 'react-native-gesture-handler';

const { width, height } = Dimensions.get('window');

interface ZoomableImageProps {
  imageUrl: string;
}

// Separate component for zoomable image to use hooks properly
const ZoomableImage = ({ imageUrl }: ZoomableImageProps) => {
  const scale = useRef(new Animated.Value(1)).current;
  const baseScale = useRef(1);

  const onPinchEvent = (event: any) => {
    if (event.nativeEvent.state === State.ACTIVE) {
      const newScale = baseScale.current * event.nativeEvent.scale;
      scale.setValue(newScale);
    }
  };

  const onPinchStateChange = (event: any) => {
    if (event.nativeEvent.oldState === State.ACTIVE) {
      baseScale.current = baseScale.current * event.nativeEvent.scale;
      scale.setValue(baseScale.current);

      if (baseScale.current < 1) {
        baseScale.current = 1;
        scale.setValue(1);
      }
    }
  };

  return (
    <View style={{ width, height }}>
      <PinchGestureHandler onGestureEvent={onPinchEvent} onHandlerStateChange={onPinchStateChange}>
        <Animated.Image
          source={{ uri: imageUrl }}
          style={{
            width: width,
            height: height,
            transform: [{ scale: scale }],
          }}
          resizeMode="contain"
        />
      </PinchGestureHandler>
    </View>
  );
};

interface FullScreenImageViewerProps {
  visible: boolean;
  images: string[];
  initialIndex?: number;
  onClose: () => void;
}

const FullScreenImageViewer = ({
  visible,
  images,
  initialIndex = 0,
  onClose,
}: FullScreenImageViewerProps) => {
  const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState(initialIndex);
  const flatListRef = useRef<FlatList>(null);

  const renderImage = ({ item }: { item: string }) => {
    return <ZoomableImage imageUrl={item} />;
  };

  const onScrollEnd = (event: any) => {
    const index = Math.round(event.nativeEvent.contentOffset.x / width);
    setCurrentIndex(index);
  };

  return (
    <Modal visible={visible} transparent={true} animationType="fade" onRequestClose={onClose}>
      <GestureHandlerRootView className="flex-1 bg-black/95">
        {/* Header with counter and close button */}
        <View className="absolute top-12 z-10 w-full flex-row items-center justify-between px-4">
          <View className="rounded-full bg-black/50 px-4 py-2">
            <Text className="font-nunitoSemi text-white">
              {currentIndex + 1} / {images.length}
            </Text>
          </View>
          <TouchableOpacity onPress={onClose} className="rounded-full bg-black/50 p-2">
            <Ionicons name="close" size={28} color="white" />
          </TouchableOpacity>
        </View>

        {/* Horizontal FlatList for images */}
        <FlatList
          ref={flatListRef}
          data={images}
          renderItem={renderImage}
          keyExtractor={(item, index) => `${item}-${index}`}
          horizontal
          pagingEnabled
          showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}
          initialScrollIndex={initialIndex}
          getItemLayout={(data, index) => ({
            length: width,
            offset: width * index,
            index,
          })}
          onMomentumScrollEnd={onScrollEnd}
        />
      </GestureHandlerRootView>
    </Modal>
  );
};

export default FullScreenImageViewer;


follow the same patter like the before photos for the after photos
9 May

Claude responded: The FullScreenImageViewer already supports multiple images with swipe.

The FullScreenImageViewer already supports multiple images with swipe. The issue is just that when opening after photos, the viewer needs to know which image set to show. Your current CompleteTaskPage already handles this correctly with viewerImages state — when you tap a before photo it sets viewerImages = beforeImages, and after photo sets viewerImages = afterImages.

The code is already correct. But looking at your FullScreenImageViewer, there's one issue — initialScrollIndex on FlatList can fail silently when the list hasn't rendered yet. Fix it:

tsx
const FullScreenImageViewer = ({
  visible,
  images,
  initialIndex = 0,
  onClose,
}: FullScreenImageViewerProps) => {
  const [currentIndex, setCurrentIndex] = useState(initialIndex);
  const flatListRef = useRef<FlatList>(null);

  // ← Reset currentIndex when initialIndex changes (different image set opened)
  React.useEffect(() => {
    setCurrentIndex(initialIndex);
  }, [initialIndex, visible]);

  const renderImage = ({ item }: { item: string }) => {
    return <ZoomableImage imageUrl={item} />;
  };

  const onScrollEnd = (event: any) => {
    const index = Math.round(event.nativeEvent.contentOffset.x / width);
    setCurrentIndex(index);
  };

  return (
    <Modal visible={visible} transparent={true} animationType="fade" onRequestClose={onClose}>
      <GestureHandlerRootView className="flex-1 bg-black/95">
        {/* Header */}
        <View className="absolute top-12 z-10 w-full flex-row items-center justify-between px-4">
          <View className="rounded-full bg-black/50 px-4 py-2">
            <Text className="font-nunitoSemi text-white">
              {currentIndex + 1} / {images.length}
            </Text>
          </View>
          <TouchableOpacity onPress={onClose} className="rounded-full bg-black/50 p-2">
            <Ionicons name="close" size={28} color="white" />
          </TouchableOpacity>
        </View>

        {/* Images */}
        <FlatList
          ref={flatListRef}
          data={images}
          renderItem={renderImage}
          keyExtractor={(item, index) => `${item}-${index}`}
          horizontal
          pagingEnabled
          showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}
          initialScrollIndex={initialIndex}
          getItemLayout={(data, index) => ({
            length: width,
            offset: width * index,
            index,
          })}
          onMomentumScrollEnd={onScrollEnd}
          // ← add this to handle scroll to index properly
          onLayout={() => {
            if (initialIndex > 0) {
              flatListRef.current?.scrollToIndex({
                index: initialIndex,
                animated: false,
              });
            }
          }}
        />
      </GestureHandlerRootView>
    </Modal>
  );
};

The CompleteTaskPage already does the right thing — handleOpenViewer(beforeImages, index) sets viewer to before photos, and handleOpenViewer(afterImages, index) sets it to after photos. Both sections use the same renderImageSection helper and the same viewer. No other changes needed.

import React from 'react';
import { View, TouchableOpacity, TextInput } from 'react-native';
import { useRouter } from 'expo-router';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import { LinearGradient } from 'expo-linear-gradient';

import { LAYOUT } from '@/constants/constants';
import { FilterIcon, SearchIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import IconContainer from '../icons/IconContainer';
import GradientIconBox from './button/GradientIconBox';

type Props = {
  value: string;
  onChangeText: (text: string) => void;
  placeholder?: string;
  onPressFilter?: () => void;
  backButton?: boolean;
  backgroundColor?: string;
  height?: number;
};

const SearchTabCustomHeader: React.FC<Props> = ({
  value,
  onChangeText,
  placeholder = 'Search providers...',
  onPressFilter,
  backButton = false,
  backgroundColor = '#FFFFFF',
  height = 70,
}) => {
  const router = useRouter();

  return (
    <View
      className="flex-row items-center"
      style={{
        height,
        backgroundColor,
        paddingHorizontal: LAYOUT.screen.paddingX,
      }}>
      {backButton && (
        <TouchableOpacity
          onPress={() => router.back()}
          hitSlop={{ top: 10, bottom: 10, left: 10, right: 10 }}
          className="mr-[10px]">
          <LinearGradient
            colors={['#2B7FFF1A', '#2B7FFF1A']}
            start={{ x: 0, y: 0 }}
            end={{ x: 1, y: 1 }}
            style={{ borderRadius: 100 }}
            className="h-[44px] w-[44px] items-center justify-center rounded-full border border-[#E5E7EB]">
            <Ionicons name="arrow-back" size={22} color="#111827" />
          </LinearGradient>
        </TouchableOpacity>
      )}

      {/* Search Bar */}
      <View className="h-[44px] flex-1 flex-row items-center gap-[6px] rounded-[12px] border border-inputborder bg-white px-[16px] ">
        <SearchIcon size={14} color="#7E8792" />
        <TextInput
          value={value}
          onChangeText={onChangeText}
          placeholder={placeholder}
          placeholderTextColor="#9CA3AF"
          className="flex-1 font-nunitoSemi text-[14px] text-color-text-placeholder"
          autoCorrect={false}
          autoCapitalize="none"
          clearButtonMode="while-editing"
        />
      </View>

      {/* Filter Button */}
      <TouchableOpacity onPress={onPressFilter} activeOpacity={0.7} className="ml-[8px]">
        <GradientIconBox
          size={44}
          borderRadius={12}
          // colors={['#FF0000', '#00FF00']}
          Icon={FilterIcon}
          iconSize={20}
          iconColor="#FFFFFF"
          style={{ borderWidth: 1, borderColor: '#E5E7EB' }}
        />
      </TouchableOpacity>
    </View>
  );
};

export default React.memo(SearchTabCustomHeader);
import React from 'react';
import { View, Text, ViewStyle, StyleProp } from 'react-native';
import { Provider } from '@/types/provider';
import Avatar from '../../shared/Avatar';
import LabelPrimary from '../../shared/LabelPrimary';
import Subtitle from '../../shared/Subtitle';
import { SERVICE_THEMES } from '@/constants/serviceThemes';
import { StarIcon } from '@/components/icons';
import PrimaryButton from '../../shared/button/PrimaryButton';
import AnimatedServices from './AnimatedServices';
import { ServiceType } from '@/types/services';

interface Props {
  provider: Provider;
  style?: StyleProp<ViewStyle>;
  onView?: (provider: Provider) => void;
}

const ProviderCard = ({ provider, style, onView }: Props) => {
  // Filter and type assert services
  const services = provider.servicesOffered.filter(
    (service): service is ServiceType => service in SERVICE_THEMES
  );

  return (
    <View
      style={[{ padding: 16, borderRadius: 22 }, style]}
      className="rounded-xl border border-gray-200 bg-white">
      {/* Avatar + Name */}
      <View className="mb-2 flex-row items-center gap-2">
        <Avatar
          verified={provider.isVerified}
          width={44}
          height={44}
          verifiedSize={10}
          verifiedOffsetX={-2}
          verifiedOffsetY={-2}
          source={provider.avatarUrl ? { uri: provider.avatarUrl } : undefined}
        />

        <View className="flex-1 justify-center">
          <LabelPrimary
            text={provider.fullName}
            style={{ fontSize: 15, fontFamily: 'Nunito-SemiBoldItalic', marginBottom: 4 }}
          />
          <Subtitle text={provider.email} style={{ fontFamily: 'Nunito-Medium' }} />
        </View>
      </View>

      {/* Divider */}
      <View
        className="border-t border-gray-200"
        style={{ marginHorizontal: -16, marginVertical: 12 }}
      />

      {/* Services + Rating */}
      <View className="flex-row items-start justify-start gap-3">
        {/* Services Section */}
        <View className="flex-1">
          <Text className="mb-[6px] font-nunitoSemi text-[13px]" style={{ color: '#9CA3AF' }}>
            Services:
          </Text>

          {services.length > 0 ? (
            <AnimatedServices services={services} />
          ) : (
            <Text className="font-nunitoItalic text-[12px]" style={{ color: '#9CA3AF' }}>
              No services offered
            </Text>
          )}
        </View>

        {/* Reviews Section */}
        <View className="flex-1">
          <Text className="mb-[6px] font-nunitoSemi text-[13px]" style={{ color: '#9CA3AF' }}>
            Reviews:
          </Text>

          <View className="h-[24px] flex-row items-center gap-[3px]">
            <StarIcon size={12} color="#FFB020" />
            <LabelPrimary text={`${provider.rating.toFixed(1)} (${provider.totalReviews})`} />
          </View>
        </View>
      </View>

      {/* Divider */}
      <View
        className="border-t border-gray-200"
        style={{ marginHorizontal: -16, marginVertical: 12 }}
      />

      <PrimaryButton
        title="View Profile"
        style={{ maxWidth: 139, borderRadius: 12 }}
        onPress={() => onView?.(provider)}
      />
    </View>
  );
};

export default ProviderCard;
// The "Value" used for logic, database, and IDs
export type ServiceType = 
  | 'snow-plowing' 
  | 'snow-shoveling' 
  | 'salting-deicing' 
  | 'lawn-mowing' 
  | 'landscaping' 
  | 'seasonal-contracts';

// The interface for the Service Object
export interface Service {
  id: ServiceType;
  type: ServiceType; 
  title: string;
  Icon: React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; color?: string }>;
  color: string;
  bg: string;
  border: string;
}


export type DistanceRange = '0-2' | '2-5' | '5-10' | '10-20' | '20+';

export type SortOption = 'best-rated' | 'most-completed' | 'nearest';


import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { View, Text, TouchableOpacity, Modal, FlatList } from 'react-native';
import { Ionicons } from '@expo/vector-icons';
import LabelPrimary from '@/components/ui/shared/LabelPrimary';

interface Option {
  label: string;
  value: string;
}

interface CustomPickerProps {
  selectedValue: string;
  onValueChange: (value: string) => void;
  options: Option[];
  placeholder: string;
  placeholderStyle?: any;
  selectedStyle?: any;
}

const CustomPicker: React.FC<CustomPickerProps> = ({
  selectedValue,
  onValueChange,
  options = [], // 👈 Default empty array
  placeholder,
  placeholderStyle,
  selectedStyle,
}) => {
  const [modalVisible, setModalVisible] = useState(false);

  const selectedOption = options.find((opt) => opt.value === selectedValue);

  return (
    <>
      <TouchableOpacity
        onPress={() => setModalVisible(true)}
        className="flex-row items-center justify-between rounded-[12px] border border-[#E5E7EB] bg-white px-4 py-3"
        style={{ paddingHorizontal: 16, paddingVertical: 11 }}>
        <Text
          style={
            selectedValue
              ? { color: '#111827', fontSize: 14, fontFamily: 'Nunito-Regular', ...selectedStyle }
              : {
                  color: '#9CA3AF',
                  fontSize: 14,
                  fontFamily: 'Nunito-Regular',
                  ...placeholderStyle,
                }
          }>
          {selectedOption ? selectedOption.label : placeholder}
        </Text>
        <Ionicons name="chevron-down" size={20} color="#9CA3AF" />
      </TouchableOpacity>

      <Modal visible={modalVisible} transparent animationType="fade">
        <TouchableOpacity
          className="flex-1 justify-center bg-black/50 px-4"
          activeOpacity={1}
          onPress={() => setModalVisible(false)}>
          <View className="overflow-hidden rounded-[16px] bg-white">
            {options.length > 0 ? (
              <FlatList
                data={options}
                keyExtractor={(item) => item.value}
                renderItem={({ item }) => {
                  const isSelected = item.value === selectedValue;
                  return (
                    <TouchableOpacity
                      onPress={() => {
                        onValueChange(item.value);
                        setModalVisible(false);
                      }}
                      className="flex-row items-center justify-between border-b border-[#E5E7EB] px-4 py-3">
                      <LabelPrimary
                        text={item.label}
                        style={{
                          fontFamily: isSelected ? 'Nunito-BoldItalic' : 'Nunito-MediumItalic',
                          color: isSelected ? '#2B7FFF' : '#4B5563',
                          flex: 1,
                        }}
                      />
                      <View className="ml-2">
                        {isSelected ? (
                          <View className="h-5 w-5 items-center justify-center rounded-full border-2 border-[#2B7FFF]">
                            <View className="h-3 w-3 rounded-full bg-[#2B7FFF]" />
                          </View>
                        ) : (
                          <View className="h-5 w-5 rounded-full border-2 border-[#D1D5DB]" />
                        )}
                      </View>
                    </TouchableOpacity>
                  );
                }}
              />
            ) : (
              <View className="p-4">
                <Text className="text-center text-gray-400">No options available</Text>
              </View>
            )}
          </View>
        </TouchableOpacity>
      </Modal>
    </>
  );
};

export default CustomPicker;


import {
  BushIcon,
  ShovelIcon,
  WaterDropIcon,
  GrassCuttingMachineIcon,
  LeafIcon,
  CalanderWithClockIcon,
} from '@/components/icons/index';
import { ServiceType } from '@/types/services';

export interface ServiceTheme {
  title: string;
  Icon: React.ComponentType<{ size?: number; color?: string }>;
  color: string;
  bg: string;
  border: string;
}

export const SERVICE_THEMES: Record<ServiceType, ServiceTheme> = {
  'snow-plowing': {
    title: 'Snow Plowing',
    Icon: BushIcon,
    color: '#13c2fe',
    bg: '#FFF7ED', // orange-50
    border: '#F97316', // orange-500
  },
  'snow-shoveling': {
    title: 'Snow Shoveling',
    Icon: ShovelIcon,
    color: '#23afe0',
    bg: '#F7FEE7', // lime-50
    border: '#84cc16', // lime-500
  },
  'salting-deicing': {
    title: 'Salting / De-icing',
    Icon: WaterDropIcon,
    color: '#32ecea',
    bg: '#ECFDF5', // emerald-50
    border: '#10B981', // emerald-500
  },
  'lawn-mowing': {
    title: 'Lawn Mowing',
    Icon: GrassCuttingMachineIcon,
    color: '#61a617',
    bg: '#ECFEFF', // cyan-50
    border: '#06B6D4', // cyan-500
  },
  landscaping: {
    title: 'Landscaping',
    Icon: LeafIcon,
    color: '#75d50c',
    bg: '#EFF6FF', // blue-50
    border: '#3B82F6', // blue-500
  },
  'seasonal-contracts': {
    title: 'Seasonal Contracts',
    Icon: CalanderWithClockIcon,
    color: '#16d5bc',
    bg: '#FAF5FF', // purple-50
    border: '#a855f7', // purple-500
  },
};


{{local_url}}/provider/all-provider

{{local_url}}/provider/all-provider?searchTerm=monir&isBlocked=true&page=&limit=
it has this filter as well in the post man. 



// store/api/userApi.ts
import { baseApi } from './baseApi';

export interface UserProfile {
  _id: string;
  user: {
    _id: string;
    isBlocked: boolean;
    isActive: boolean;
    isAdminVerified?: boolean;
  };
  name: string;
  phone: string;
  email: string;
  isIdentificationDocumentApproved: boolean;
  serviceTypes?: string[];
  isStripeConnected?: boolean;
  stripeAccountId?: string | null;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
  address: string;
  dateOfBirth: string | null;
  profile_image?: string;
  city?: string;
  street?: string;
  referralCode?: string;
  totalJobComplete?: number;
  totalRatingCount?: number;
  avgRating?: number;
}

export interface CustomerProfileResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: {
    stripeCustomerId: string | null;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved: boolean;
    _id: string;
    user: {
      _id: string;
      isBlocked: boolean;
      isActive: boolean;
      isAdminVerified: boolean;
    };
    name: string;
    email: string;
    phone: string;
    profile_image: string;
    city: string;
    street: string;
    address: string;
    createdAt: string;
    updatedAt: string;
    referralCode: string;
    dateOfBirth: string | null;
  };
}

export const userApi = baseApi.injectEndpoints({
  endpoints: (builder) => ({
    getUserProfile: builder.query<UserProfile | null, void>({
      query: () => '/user/get-my-profile',
      transformResponse: (response: any) => {
        // console.log('🔄 Transforming response:', response);
        if (response.success && response.data) {
          return response.data;
        }
        return null;
      },
      providesTags: ['User'],
    }),

    // Get customer by ID for provider view
    getCustomerById: builder.query<CustomerProfileResponse['data'], string>({
      query: (customerId) => `/customer/get-single/${customerId}`,
      transformResponse: (response: CustomerProfileResponse) => {
        // console.log('🔄 Transforming customer profile:', response);
        return response.data;
      },
      providesTags: ['Customer'],
    }),
  }),
});

export const { useGetUserProfileQuery, useGetCustomerByIdQuery } = userApi;

// store/api/providerApi.ts
import { baseApi } from './baseApi';
import { Provider } from '@/types/provider';
import { toFrontendFormat } from '@/utils/serviceTypeMapper';

export interface SingleProviderResponse {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  data: {
    _id: string;
    user: {
      _id: string;
      isBlocked: boolean;
      isActive: boolean;
    };

    totalJobComplete?: number;
    totalRatingCount?: number;
    avgRating?: number;
    stripeAccountId?: string;
    name: string;
    phone: string;
    email: string;
    isIdentificationDocumentApproved: boolean;
    serviceTypes: string[];
    isStripeConnected: boolean;
    createdAt: string;
    updatedAt: string;
    address: string;
    dateOfBirth: string;
    profile_image: string;
  };
}

export const providerApi = baseApi.injectEndpoints({
  endpoints: (builder) => ({
    getProviderById: builder.query<Provider, string>({
      query: (id) => `/provider/get-single/${id}`,
      transformResponse: (response: SingleProviderResponse) => {
        if (response.success && response.data) {
          const data = response.data;
          return {
            id: data._id,
            fullName: data.name,
            avatarUrl: data.profile_image || '',
            email: data.email,
            phoneNumber: data.phone,
            servicesOffered: data.serviceTypes.map(toFrontendFormat),
            rating: data.avgRating || 0, // 👈 was hardcoded 0
            totalReviews: data.totalRatingCount || 0, // 👈 was hardcoded 0
            isVerified: data.isIdentificationDocumentApproved,
            blocked: data.user?.isBlocked || false,
            completedJobs: data.totalJobComplete || 0, // 👈 was hardcoded 0
            bio: '',
            createdAt: data.createdAt,
            updatedAt: data.updatedAt,
            stripeConnected: data.isStripeConnected || false,
            address: data.address,
          } as Provider;
        }
        throw new Error('Provider not found');
      },
      providesTags: ['Provider'],
    }),
  }),
});

export const { useGetProviderByIdQuery } = providerApi;

I need to properly integrate this search screen now.
9 May

Claude responded: Looking at your API response, the search endpoint is /provider/all-provider with optional searchTerm filter.
